features.name: Rage
features.description: On your turn, you can enter a Rage as a Bonus Action if you aren't wearing Heavy armor. While raging, you gain the following benefits: Damage Bonus — When you make an attack using Strength — with either a weapon or an Unarmed Strike — and deal damage on the hit, you gain a bonus to the damage equal to your Rage Damage value. Resistance — You have Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage. Strength Advantage — You have Advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws. Your Rage lasts until the end of your next turn, and it ends early if you don Heavy armor or have the Incapacitated condition. If your Rage is still active on your next turn, you can extend the Rage for another round by doing one of the following: making an attack roll against an enemy, forcing an enemy to make a saving throw, or taking a Bonus Action to extend it. Once you have Raged the number of times shown in the Rages column of the Barbarian Features table for your Barbarian level, you must finish a Long Rest before you can Rage again.

benefits: Bonus damage on Strength-based attacks and Unarmed Strikes
Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage
Advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws
extend_by: Making an attack roll against an enemy
Forcing an enemy to make a saving throw
Taking a Bonus Action to extend

features.name: Unarmored Defense
features.description: While you aren't wearing any armor, your base Armor Class equals 10 plus your Dexterity modifier plus your Constitution modifier. You can use a Shield and still gain this benefit.

mechanics.note: Can use Shield and still benefit

features.name: Weapon Mastery
features.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of Simple or Martial Melee weapons of your choice, such as Greataxes and Handaxes. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices. When you reach certain Barbarian levels, you gain the ability to use the mastery properties of more weapon types, as shown in the Weapon Mastery column of the Barbarian Features table.

features.name: Danger Sense
features.description: You gain an uncanny sense of when things nearby aren't as they should be, giving you an edge when you dodge out of the way. You have Advantage on Dexterity saving throws unless you have the Incapacitated condition.

mechanics.benefit: Advantage on Dexterity saving throws
mechanics.condition: Unless you have the Incapacitated condition

features.name: Reckless Attack
features.description: You can throw aside all concern for defense to attack with fierce desperation. When you make your first attack roll on your turn, you can decide to attack recklessly. Doing so gives you Advantage on attack rolls using Strength until the start of your next turn, but attack rolls against you have Advantage during that time.

mechanics.activation: Declared before first attack roll on your turn
mechanics.benefit: Advantage on Strength-based attack rolls until start of next turn
mechanics.drawback: Attack rolls against you have Advantage until start of next turn

features.name: Barbarian Subclass
features.description: You gain a Barbarian subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Barbarian levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Barbarian level or lower.

features.name: Primal Knowledge
features.description: You gain proficiency in another skill of your choice from the skill list available to Barbarians at level 1. In addition, while your Rage is active, you can channel primal power when you attempt certain tasks; whenever you make an ability check using one of the following skills, you can make it as a Strength check even if it normally uses a different ability: Acrobatics, Intimidation, Perception, Stealth, or Survival.

mechanics.benefit_1: Gain proficiency in one additional Barbarian skill
mechanics.benefit_2: While Raging: Acrobatics, Intimidation, Perception, Stealth, Survival can be made as Strength checks

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Barbarian levels 8, 12, and 16.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Fast Movement
features.description: Your speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing Heavy armor.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Barbarian subclass.

features.name: Feral Instinct
features.description: Your instincts are so honed that you have Advantage on Initiative rolls.

mechanics.benefit: Advantage on Initiative rolls

features.name: Instinctive Pounce
features.description: As part of the Bonus Action you take to enter your Rage, you can move up to half your Speed.

mechanics.activation: Part of the Bonus Action to enter Rage
mechanics.benefit: Move up to half your Speed

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Brutal Strike
features.description: If you use Reckless Attack, you can forgo any Advantage on one Strength-based attack roll of your choice on your turn. The chosen attack roll mustn't have Disadvantage. If the chosen attack roll hits, the target takes an extra 1d10 damage of the same type dealt by the weapon or Unarmed Strike, and you can cause one Brutal Strike effect of your choice. You have the following effect options: Forceful Blow — The target is pushed 15 feet straight away from you. You can then move up to half your Speed straight toward the target without provoking Opportunity Attacks. Hamstring Blow — The target's Speed is reduced by 15 feet until the start of your next turn. This movement reduction increases if you reach certain levels: the reduction becomes 20 feet at level 13, and 25 feet at level 17.

mechanics.activation: Forgo one instance of Advantage on a Strength-based attack roll

effects.name: Forceful Blow
effects.description: Push target 15 feet away. You may then move up to half your Speed toward the target without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

effects.name: Hamstring Blow
effects.description: Reduce target's Speed by 15 feet until start of your next turn (20 ft at level 13, 25 ft at level 17).

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Barbarian subclass.

features.name: Relentless Rage
features.description: Your Rage can keep you fighting despite grievous wounds. If you drop to 0 Hit Points while your Rage is active and don't die outright, you can make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. If you succeed, your Hit Points instead change to a number equal to twice your Barbarian level. Each time you use this feature after the first, the DC increases by 5. When you finish a Short or Long Rest, the DC resets to 10.

mechanics.trigger: Drop to 0 HP while Raging (and don't die outright)
mechanics.saving_throw: Constitution DC 10 (increases by 5 each use; resets on Short or Long Rest)
mechanics.success: HP set to twice your Barbarian level
mechanics.dc_scaling: DC 10 → 15 → 20 → etc., resets on Short/Long Rest

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Improved Brutal Strike
features.description: You have honed new ways to wound foes with your Brutal Strike. The following effects are now among your Brutal Strike options, and the Hamstring Blow's speed reduction increases to 20 feet. Staggering Blow — The target has Disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes, and it can't make Opportunity Attacks until the start of your next turn. Sundering Blow — Before the start of your next turn, the next attack roll made by another creature against the target gains a +5 bonus to the roll. If no such attack is made before your next turn starts, the benefit is lost.

new_effects.name: Staggering Blow
new_effects.description: Target has Disadvantage on its next saving throw and can't make Opportunity Attacks until the start of your next turn.

new_effects.name: Sundering Blow
new_effects.description: The next attack roll against the target before the start of your next turn gains a +5 bonus. Lost if no attack is made in time.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Barbarian subclass.

features.name: Persistent Rage
features.description: When you roll Initiative, you can regain all expended uses of Rage. After you regain Rage uses this way, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.trigger: Roll Initiative
mechanics.benefit: Regain all expended Rage uses

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Improved Brutal Strike (II)
features.description: The extra damage of your Brutal Strike increases to 2d10. In addition, you can use two Brutal Strike effects whenever you use that feature.

features.name: Indomitable Might
features.description: If your total for a Strength check or Strength saving throw is less than your Strength score, you can use that score in place of the total.

mechanics.benefit: Minimum result on Strength checks and Strength saving throws equals your Strength score

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain the Epic Boon of Fortitude feat or another Epic Boon feat of your choice.

mechanics.type: Epic Boon feat (e.g. Epic Boon of Fortitude)

features.name: Primal Champion
features.description: You embody primal power. Your Strength and Constitution scores increase by 4. Your maximum for those scores is now 25.

subclasses.name: Path of the Berserker
subclasses.description: For Barbarians who follow the Path of the Berserker, rage is a means to an end — that end being violence. The Berserker learns to harness their rage into a vicious frenzy, transcending their own physical limits.

features.name: Frenzy
features.description: If you use Reckless Attack while your Rage is active, you deal extra damage to the first target you hit on your turn with a Strength-based attack. To determine this extra damage, roll a number of d6s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. The damage has the same type as the weapon or Unarmed Strike used for the attack.

mechanics.trigger: Use Reckless Attack while Raging
mechanics.extra_damage: Roll a number of d6s equal to your Rage Damage bonus (same type as weapon)

features.name: Mindless Rage
features.description: You have Immunity to the Charmed and Frightened conditions while your Rage is active. If you are Charmed or Frightened when you enter your Rage, the condition ends on you.

features.name: Retaliation
features.description: When you take damage from a creature that is within 5 feet of you, you can use your Reaction to make one melee attack against that creature, using a weapon or an Unarmed Strike.

mechanics.trigger: Take damage from a creature within 5 feet
mechanics.action: One melee attack (weapon or Unarmed Strike)

features.name: Intimidating Presence
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can strike terror into others with your menacing presence and primal power. When you do so, each creature of your choice in a 30-foot Emanation originating from you must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC equal to 8 plus your proficiency bonus plus your Strength modifier). On a failed save, a creature has the Frightened condition until the start of your next turn. At the start of your next turn, you can extend the duration of this effect on the Frightened creatures until the start of your subsequent turn. This extension can last a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1). The effect ends if the creature ends its turn out of line of sight or more than 60 feet away from you. If the creature succeeds on its save, you can't use this feature on that creature again for 24 hours.

mechanics.saving_throw: Wisdom DC = 8 + proficiency bonus + Strength modifier
mechanics.failure: Frightened until start of your next turn (extendable)
mechanics.extension: Can extend duration each turn; max extensions = Constitution modifier (min 1)
mechanics.ends_early: Target ends turn out of line of sight or more than 60 ft away
mechanics.on_success: Cannot use feature on that creature for 24 hours

subclasses.name: Path of the Wild Heart
subclasses.description: Barbarians who follow the Path of the Wild Heart view themselves as kin to animals. These Barbarians learn to channel their rage through communion with the animal world, adopting bestial aspects in the heat of battle.

features.name: Animal Speaker
features.description: You can cast the Beast Sense and Speak with Animals spells, but only as Rituals. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

features.name: Rage of the Wilds
features.description: Your Rage taps into the primal power of animals. Whenever you activate your Rage, you gain one of the following options of your choice: Bear — While your Rage is active, you have Resistance to every damage type except Force, Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant. Eagle — When you activate your Rage and as a Bonus Action while it's active, you can take the Disengage and Dash actions. Wolf — While your Rage is active, you can use a Bonus Action on each of your turns to force one Large or smaller creature within 10 feet of you to make a Strength saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and Proficiency Bonus). On a failure, the creature has the Prone condition.

mechanics.activation: Choose one option when activating Rage

options.name: Bear
options.description: Resistance to all damage types except Force, Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant while Raging.

options.name: Eagle
options.description: On Rage activation and as Bonus Action while Raging, take both Disengage and Dash actions.

options.name: Wolf
options.description: Bonus Action each turn: one Large or smaller creature within 10 ft makes Strength save (DC 8 + STR mod + PB) or falls Prone.

features.name: Aspect of the Wilds
features.description: You gain one of the following benefits of your choice, and you can change your choice whenever you finish a Long Rest: Cheetah — You gain a Climb Speed equal to your Speed. In addition, whenever you use the Dash action, difficult terrain doesn't cost you extra movement for the rest of the turn. Elephant — You gain proficiency with the Wisdom (Insight) and Wisdom (Medicine) skills. If you already have proficiency in one of those skills, you instead gain Expertise in that skill. Owl — You gain Darkvision with a range of 60 feet. If you already have Darkvision, its range increases by 60 feet. You also have Advantage on Perception checks.

options.name: Cheetah
options.description: Gain Climb Speed equal to Speed. Difficult terrain doesn't cost extra movement during Dash.

options.name: Elephant
options.description: Gain proficiency in Insight and Medicine (or Expertise if already proficient).

options.name: Owl
options.description: Gain Darkvision 60 ft (or +60 ft if already have it). Advantage on Perception checks.

features.name: Nature Speaker
features.description: You can cast the Commune with Nature spell, but only as a Ritual. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

features.name: Power of the Wilds
features.description: Whenever you activate your Rage, you gain one of the following options of your choice: Falcon — While your Rage is active, you have a Fly Speed equal to your Speed if you are not wearing any armor. Tiger — While your Rage is active, any creature that starts its turn in a 5-foot Emanation originating from you takes 1d6 Slashing damage. Ram — While your Rage is active, you can cause Large or smaller creatures to have the Prone condition when you hit them with a melee attack.

mechanics.activation: Choose one option when activating Rage

options.name: Falcon
options.description: Fly Speed equal to your Speed while Raging (requires no armor).

options.name: Tiger
options.description: Creatures that start their turn within 5 ft of you take 1d6 Slashing damage.

options.name: Ram
options.description: Hit a Large or smaller creature with a melee attack to knock them Prone.

subclasses.name: Path of the World Tree
subclasses.description: Barbarians who follow the Path of the World Tree draw on the cosmic forces of Yggdrasil, the World Tree. These Barbarians can tap into the immense power it represents, gaining access to magical abilities and using it to travel between realms.

features.name: Vitality of the Tree
features.description: Your Rage taps into the life force of the World Tree. You gain the following benefits: Vitality Surge — When you activate your Rage, you gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to your Barbarian level. Life-Giving Force — At the start of each of your turns while your Rage is active, you can choose another creature within 10 feet of you to give some of those Temporary Hit Points to. If you do, that creature gains a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to half your Barbarian level (round down). You can't give Temporary Hit Points to a creature that already has Temporary Hit Points.

mechanics.vitality_surge: Gain Temp HP equal to Barbarian level on Rage activation

life_giving_force.trigger: Start of each turn while Raging
life_giving_force.restriction: Target must not already have Temporary Hit Points

features.name: Branches of the Tree
features.description: Whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you while your Rage is active, you can take a Reaction to summon spectral branches of the World Tree around it. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and proficiency bonus) or be teleported to an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of you or in the nearest unoccupied space you can see. After the teleportation, you can reduce the target's Speed to 0 until the end of the current turn.

mechanics.trigger: Creature you can see starts its turn within 30 ft while Raging
mechanics.failure: Teleported to unoccupied space within 5 ft of you (or nearest unoccupied)
mechanics.optional: Reduce target's Speed to 0 until end of turn

features.name: Battering Roots
features.description: During your Rage, your reach is 10 feet greater than normal, as spectral roots of the World Tree extend from you. When you hit with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike that has the Heavy or Versatile property, you can activate the Push or Topple mastery property in addition to a different mastery property you're already using with that weapon.

mechanics.extra_mastery: Can use Push or Topple in addition to another mastery property on Heavy or Versatile weapons

features.name: Travel Along the Tree
features.description: When you activate your Rage and as a Bonus Action while it's active, you can teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. In addition, once per Rage, you can increase the teleport range to 500 feet. If you do, you can bring up to six willing creatures within 10 feet of your destination.

mechanics.on_rage_activation_and_bonus_action: Teleport up to 60 feet to unoccupied visible space
mechanics.once_per_rage: Extend teleport to 500 feet; can bring up to 6 willing creatures within 10 ft of destination

subclasses.name: Path of the Zealot
subclasses.description: Barbarians who follow the Path of the Zealot receive boons from a deity or pantheon. These Barbarians are granted power to channel divine fury in the form of divine strikes, holy auras, and potent healing.

features.name: Divine Fury
features.description: You can channel divine power into your strikes. On each of your turns while your Rage is active, the first creature you hit with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike takes extra damage equal to 1d6 plus half your Barbarian level (round down). The extra damage is Necrotic or Radiant; you choose the type each time you activate your Rage.

mechanics.trigger: First hit each turn while Raging
mechanics.damage_type: Necrotic or Radiant (chosen on Rage activation)

features.name: Warrior of the Gods
features.description: A divine entity helps ensure you can keep fighting. You have a pool of four d12s that you can spend to heal yourself. As a Bonus Action, you can expend dice from the pool, roll them, and regain a number of Hit Points equal to the roll. Your pool regains all expended dice when you finish a Long Rest. In addition, if a spell, such as the Raise Dead spell, has the sole effect of restoring you to life (but not undeath), the caster doesn't need material components to cast the spell on you.

mechanics.effect: Roll expended dice and regain HP equal to result
mechanics.resurrection_bonus: No material components needed for spells that solely restore you to life

features.name: Fanatical Focus
features.description: Once per active Rage, if you fail a saving throw, you can reroll it with a bonus equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and you must use the new roll.

mechanics.trigger: Fail a saving throw while Raging
mechanics.benefit: Reroll with bonus equal to Rage Damage bonus (must use new roll)

features.name: Zealous Presence
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you unleash a battle cry infused with divine energy. Up to ten other creatures of your choice within 60 feet of you that can hear you gain Advantage on attack rolls and saving throws until the start of your next turn. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.benefit: Advantage on attack rolls and saving throws until start of your next turn

features.name: Rage Beyond Death
features.description: While your Rage is active, having 0 Hit Points doesn't incapacitate you. You still must make Death Saving Throws, and you suffer the normal effects of taking damage while at 0 Hit Points. However, if you would die due to failing Death Saving Throws, you don't die until your Rage ends, and you die then only if you still have 0 Hit Points.

mechanics.while_raging_at_0_hp: Not Incapacitated, still make Death Saving Throws
mechanics.death_delay: If you would die from failed Death Saving Throws, death is delayed until Rage ends (if still at 0 HP)

class.name: Bard
tool_proficiencies: Three musical instruments of your choice
multiclass_proficiencies_gained: one musical instrument of your choice

spellcasting.ritual_note: You can cast any Bard spell you know as a ritual if it has the ritual tag.

features.name: Bardic Inspiration
features.description: You can supernaturally inspire others through words, music, or dance. A creature can use a Bardic Inspiration die after it rolls a d20 Test, but before it knows whether it succeeded or failed. The Bardic Inspiration die is a d6. As a Bonus Action, you can inspire another creature within 60 feet of you that can see or hear you. That creature gains one of your Bardic Inspiration dice. A creature can have only one Bardic Inspiration die at a time. Once within the next hour, the creature can roll that die and add the number rolled to one d20 Test it makes. The creature can wait until after it rolls the d20 before deciding to use the Bardic Inspiration die, but must decide before the DM says whether the roll succeeds or fails. Once the Bardic Inspiration die is rolled, it is lost. A creature can have only one Bardic Inspiration die at a time. You can confer a number of these dice equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1). You regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.range: 60 feet (target must see or hear you)
mechanics.uses: Charisma modifier (minimum 1), regain on Long Rest
mechanics.usage: After rolling a d20 Test, before knowing success or failure; adds to the d20 result

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You have learned to untangle and reshape the fabric of reality in harmony with your wishes and music. Your spells are part of your vast repertoire, magic that you can tune to different situations.

features.name: Expertise
features.description: You gain Expertise in two of your skill proficiencies of your choice. Expertise means your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make using the chosen proficiency. At level 9, you gain Expertise in two more of your skill proficiencies.

mechanics.effect: Double proficiency bonus on chosen skills

features.name: Jack of All Trades
features.description: You can add half your Proficiency Bonus (rounded down) to any ability check you make that uses a skill proficiency you lack, as long as you aren't already adding your Proficiency Bonus to it.

mechanics.benefit: Add half proficiency bonus (rounded down) to skill checks you're not proficient in

features.name: Bard Subclass
features.description: You gain a Bard subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Bard levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Bard level or lower.

features.name: Bardic Inspiration (d8)
features.description: Your Bardic Inspiration die changes to a d8 at level 5, d10 at level 10, and d12 at level 15.
features.note: Die upgrade happens at level 5, not level 3. Level 3 only brings the subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Bard levels 8, 12, 16, and 19.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Font of Inspiration
features.description: You now regain all your expended uses of Bardic Inspiration when you finish a Short or Long Rest. In addition, if you have no uses of Bardic Inspiration left, you can give a creature a Bardic Inspiration die by expending a spell slot (no action required).

mechanics.emergency_use: Expend a spell slot to grant one Bardic Inspiration die (no action required)

features.name: Bardic Inspiration Die upgrade
features.description: Your Bardic Inspiration die becomes a d8.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Bard subclass.

features.name: Countercharm
features.description: You can use musical notes or words of power to disrupt mind-influencing effects. If you or a creature within 30 feet of you fails a saving throw against an effect that applies the Charmed or Frightened condition, you can use your Reaction to expend one use of Bardic Inspiration, roll the die, and add the number rolled to the saving throw, potentially turning it into a success.

mechanics.trigger: You or a creature within 30 feet fails a save against Charmed or Frightened
mechanics.effect: Roll the Bardic Inspiration die and add to the saving throw (potentially turning failure to success)

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Expertise (2 more skills)
features.description: You gain Expertise in two more of your skill proficiencies of your choice.

features.name: Magical Secrets
features.description: You have collected magical knowledge from a wide spectrum of disciplines. Choose two spells from any class, including this one. A spell you choose must be of a level you can cast, as shown in the Bard Features table, or a cantrip. The chosen spells count as Bard spells for you and are included in the number in the Spells Known column of the Bard Features table. You gain two more spells this way at Bard levels 14 and 18.

mechanics.restriction: Must be castable at your current level or a cantrip

features.name: Bardic Inspiration Die upgrade
features.description: Your Bardic Inspiration die becomes a d10.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Bard subclass.

features.name: Magical Secrets (2 more)
features.description: You learn two more spells from any class list, as described in the Magical Secrets feature.

features.name: Bardic Inspiration Die upgrade
features.description: Your Bardic Inspiration die becomes a d12.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Superior Inspiration
features.description: When you roll Initiative and have no uses of Bardic Inspiration left, you regain two uses of it.

mechanics.trigger: Roll Initiative with 0 Bardic Inspiration uses remaining
mechanics.benefit: Regain 2 Bardic Inspiration uses

features.name: Magical Secrets (2 more)
features.description: You learn two more spells from any class list, as described in the Magical Secrets feature.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Words of Creation
features.description: You have mastered two of the prime words of creation, the words that shaped the universe. You always have the Power Word Heal and Power Word Kill spells prepared. When you cast either spell, you can target a second creature with it by expending a spell slot of 9th level or lower — you do not need to expend a separate spell slot for the second target.

mechanics.bonus: Can target a second creature by expending a spell slot of 9th level or lower (no extra slot for second target)

subclasses.name: College of Dance
subclasses.description: Bards of the College of Dance know that the words of a song are not the only things that move an audience. The College of Dance teaches these Bards that the body itself can channel the magic of music and story, and these Bards learn to use movement and gesture to express and shape the world around them.

features.name: Dazzling Footwork
features.description: While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a Shield, you gain the following benefits: Dance Moves — Your base Armor Class equals 10 plus your Dexterity modifier plus your Charisma modifier. Bardic Damage — When you make an Unarmed Strike as part of the Attack action, you can use your Bardic Inspiration to deal extra damage. Expend one die and roll it; add the result to the damage of one Unarmed Strike you make this turn. Unarmored Movement — Your Speed increases by 10 feet.

mechanics.condition: Not wearing armor and not wielding a Shield
mechanics.bardic_damage: Expend one Bardic Inspiration die and add result to one Unarmed Strike damage (as part of Attack action)

features.name: Inspiring Movement
features.description: When an enemy you can see ends its turn within 5 feet of an ally of yours, and that ally isn't incapacitated, you can use your Reaction and expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to move that ally up to half their Speed. This movement doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks.

mechanics.trigger: Enemy ends its turn within 5 feet of an ally you can see
mechanics.effect: Move the ally up to half their Speed (no Opportunity Attacks)

features.name: Tandem Footwork
features.description: When you roll Initiative, you can spend one use of Bardic Inspiration if you have no Bardic Inspiration dice left (ignore this if you have any left). You and up to five friendly creatures within 30 feet of you who can hear you gain a bonus to their Initiative rolls equal to a roll of your Bardic Inspiration die.

mechanics.trigger: Roll Initiative
mechanics.targets: You and up to 5 friendly creatures within 30 ft who can hear you
mechanics.benefit: Roll Bardic Inspiration die and add result to all Initiative rolls

features.name: Leading Evasion
features.description: When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if you fail. If any creatures within 5 feet of you are making the same Dexterity saving throw, you can share this benefit with them.

mechanics.benefit: Evasion: no damage on successful Dex save, half on failure
mechanics.share: Can extend benefit to creatures within 5 feet making the same Dex save

features.name: Irresistible Dance
features.description: You always have the Otto's Irresistible Dance spell prepared. When a creature fails the saving throw to resist the spell, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration, roll the die, and the creature takes Psychic damage equal to the roll. Additionally, the creature has Disadvantage on saving throws to end the spell on itself.

on_failed_save.additional: Creature has Disadvantage on saves to end the spell

subclasses.name: College of Glamour
subclasses.description: The College of Glamour is open to those Bards who have mastered their craft in the vibrant, deadly realm of the Feywild or under the tutelage of someone who dwells there. These Bards learn to untangle and reshape the desires of those around them through the beguiling magic of the fey.

features.name: Beguiling Magic
features.description: You always have the Charm Person and Mirror Image spells prepared. In addition, immediately after you cast an Enchantment or Illusion spell using a spell slot, you can cause a creature you can see within 60 feet of you to be Charmed or Frightened by you until the end of your next turn. The target makes a Wisdom saving throw (DC equals your spell save DC), and the condition ends early if the save succeeds.

mechanics.trigger: Cast an Enchantment or Illusion spell using a spell slot

bonus_effect.target: One creature you can see within 60 feet
bonus_effect.saving_throw: Wisdom vs your spell save DC
bonus_effect.duration: Until end of your next turn (ends early on successful save)

features.name: Mantle of Inspiration
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration and choose a number of creatures you can see within 60 feet of you, up to a number equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1). Each of them gains a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to 2 times the number you rolled on the Bardic Inspiration die. In addition, each of them can immediately use its Reaction to move up to its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

mechanics.targets: Up to CHA modifier creatures (min 1) within 60 ft you can see
mechanics.bonus_movement: Each target can immediately Reaction-move up to their Speed (no Opportunity Attacks)

features.name: Mantle of Majesty
features.description: You always have the Command spell prepared. As a Bonus Action, you cast Command without expending a spell slot, and you take on an unearthly appearance for 1 minute or until your Concentration ends. During this time, you can cast Command as a Bonus Action on each of your turns without expending a spell slot. Any creature Charmed by you automatically fails its saving throw against this Command.

mechanics.ongoing: Cast Command as Bonus Action each turn (no slot) for the duration
mechanics.charmed_creatures: Automatically fail saving throw against your Command

features.name: Unbreakable Majesty
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can assume a magically majestic presence for 1 minute or until you are Incapacitated. For the duration, whenever any creature tries to attack you for the first time on a turn, the attacker must make a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, it can't attack you on this turn, and it must choose a new target for its attack or the attack is wasted. On a successful save, it can attack you on this turn, but it has Disadvantage on any saving throw it makes against your spells on your next turn. Once you assume this majestic presence, you can't do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

mechanics.saving_throw: Charisma vs your spell save DC (first time each turn an attacker targets you)
mechanics.failure: Cannot attack you this turn; must choose new target
mechanics.success: Can attack you, but has Disadvantage on saving throws against your spells next turn
mechanics.limit: Once per Short or Long Rest

subclasses.name: College of Lore
subclasses.description: Bards of the College of Lore know something about most things, collecting bits of knowledge from sources as diverse as scholarly tomes and peasant tales. Whether singing folk ballads in taverns or elaborate compositions in royal courts, these Bards use their gifts to hold audiences spellbound.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency in three skills of your choice.

features.name: Cutting Words
features.description: You learn how to use your wit to distract, confuse, and otherwise sap the confidence and competence of others. When a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you makes a damage roll or succeeds on an ability check or attack roll, you can use your Reaction and expend one of your uses of Bardic Inspiration to roll a Bardic Inspiration die and subtract the number rolled from the creature's roll. You can choose to use this feature after the creature makes its roll, but before the DM determines whether the attack roll or ability check succeeds or fails, or before the creature deals its damage. The creature is immune if it can't hear you or if it's immune to being Charmed.

mechanics.trigger: Creature within 60 ft makes a damage roll or succeeds on ability check/attack roll
mechanics.effect: Roll Bardic Inspiration die and subtract from creature's roll
mechanics.timing: After roll, before DM determines success/failure or before damage
mechanics.immunity: Creature can't hear you OR immune to Charmed

features.name: Magical Discoveries
features.description: You learn two spells of your choice from any class. A spell you choose must be of a level you can cast, as shown in the Bard Features table, or a cantrip. The chosen spells count as Bard spells for you but don't count against the number of Bard spells you know.

mechanics.note: Do NOT count against Spells Known total

features.name: Peerless Skill
features.description: When you make an ability check and fail, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration, roll the die, and add the number rolled to the check, potentially turning a failure into a success. On a success, the Bardic Inspiration die is not expended.

mechanics.trigger: Fail an ability check
mechanics.effect: Roll die and add to the failed check (potentially turning to success)
mechanics.on_success: Bardic Inspiration die is not expended

subclasses.name: College of Valor
subclasses.description: Bards of the College of Valor are daring skalds whose tales keep alive the memory of the great heroes of the past, and thereby inspire a new generation of heroes. These Bards gather in mead halls or around great bonfires to sing the deeds of the mighty, both past and present.

features.name: Combat Inspiration
features.description: You can inspire others in battle. A creature that has a Bardic Inspiration die from you can use it in one of the following ways: Defense — When the creature is hit by an attack roll, it can use its Reaction to roll the Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to its Armor Class against that attack, potentially causing the attack to miss. Offense — Immediately after the creature hits a target with an attack roll, the creature can roll the Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to the attack's damage against the target.

options.name: Defense
options.effect: Roll Bardic Inspiration die and add to AC against that attack (potentially causing a miss)

options.name: Offense
options.trigger: Immediately after hitting a target with an attack roll
options.effect: Roll Bardic Inspiration die and add to damage

features.name: Martial Training
features.description: You gain proficiency with Martial weapons and Medium armor. In addition, you can use a Simple or Martial weapon as a spellcasting focus.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Battle Magic
features.description: After you cast a spell that has a casting time of an Action, you can make one attack with a weapon as a Bonus Action.

mechanics.trigger: Cast a spell with a casting time of an Action
mechanics.bonus: Make one weapon attack as a Bonus Action

features.name: Fighting Style
features.description: You have honed your martial prowess and gain a Fighting Style feat of your choice. Whenever you gain a Fighter level, you can replace the feat you chose with a different Fighting Style feat.

available_styles.name: Archery
available_styles.description: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with Ranged weapons.

available_styles.name: Blind Fighting
available_styles.description: You have Blindsight with a range of 10 feet.

available_styles.name: Defense
available_styles.description: While you are wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class.

available_styles.name: Dueling
available_styles.description: When you are wielding a Melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.

available_styles.name: Great Weapon Fighting
available_styles.description: When you roll damage for an attack you make with a Melee weapon that you are holding with two hands, you can treat any 1 or 2 on a damage die as a 3. The weapon must have the Two-Handed or Versatile property for you to gain this benefit.

available_styles.name: Interception
available_styles.description: When a creature you can see hits a target, other than you, within 5 feet of you with an attack, you can use your Reaction to reduce the damage the target takes by 1d10 plus your Proficiency Bonus (to a minimum of 0 damage). You must be wielding a Shield or a Simple or Martial weapon to use this Reaction.

available_styles.name: Protection
available_styles.description: When a creature you can see attacks a target other than you that is within 5 feet of you, you can use your Reaction to impose Disadvantage on the attack roll. You must be wielding a Shield.

available_styles.name: Thrown Weapon Fighting
available_styles.description: You can draw a weapon that has the Thrown property as part of the attack you make with the weapon. In addition, when you hit with a ranged attack using a Thrown weapon, you gain a +2 bonus to the damage roll.

available_styles.name: Two-Weapon Fighting
available_styles.description: When you make an extra attack as a result of using a weapon that has the Light property, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of that attack if you aren't already adding it to the damage.

available_styles.name: Unarmed Fighting
available_styles.description: Your Unarmed Strikes can deal Bludgeoning damage equal to 1d6 plus your Strength modifier on a hit. If you aren't holding any weapons or a Shield when you make the attack roll, the d6 becomes a d8. At the start of each of your turns, you can deal 1d4 Bludgeoning damage to one creature Grappled by you.

features.name: Second Wind
features.description: You have a limited well of physical and mental stamina that you can draw on. As a Bonus Action, you can use it to regain Hit Points equal to 1d10 plus your Fighter level. You can use this feature twice. You regain one expended use when you finish a Short Rest, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. When you reach certain Fighter levels, you gain more uses of this feature, as shown in the Second Wind column of the Fighter Features table.

mechanics.recharge: Short Rest restores 1 use; Long Rest restores all

features.name: Weapon Mastery
features.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of three kinds of Simple or Martial weapons of your choice. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change one of those weapon choices. When you reach certain Fighter levels, you gain the ability to use the mastery properties of more weapon types, as shown in the Weapon Mastery column of the Fighter Features table.

features.name: Action Surge
features.description: You can push yourself beyond your normal limits for a moment. On your turn, you can take one additional action, except the Magic action. Once you use this feature, you must finish a Short or Long Rest before you can use it again. Starting at level 17, you can use it twice before a rest, but only once per turn.

mechanics.effect: Take one additional action (not the Magic action)

features.name: Tactical Mind
features.description: You have a mind for tactics on and off the battlefield. When you fail an ability check, you can expend a use of your Second Wind to push yourself toward success. Rather than regaining Hit Points, you roll 1d10 and add the number rolled to the ability check, potentially turning it into a success. If the check still fails, this use of Second Wind isn't expended.

mechanics.trigger: Fail an ability check
mechanics.effect: Roll 1d10 and add to the failed check
mechanics.on_failure: Second Wind use is NOT expended

features.name: Fighter Subclass
features.description: You gain a Fighter subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Fighter levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Fighter level or lower.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Fighter levels 6, 8, 12, 14, and 16.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Fighter subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Indomitable
features.description: If you fail a saving throw, you can reroll it with a bonus equal to your Fighter level. If you do so, you must use the new roll, and you can't use this feature again until you finish a Long Rest. You can use this feature twice before a Long Rest starting at level 13, and three times before a Long Rest starting at level 17.

mechanics.trigger: Fail a saving throw
mechanics.effect: Reroll with bonus equal to Fighter level (must use new roll)

features.name: Tactical Shift
features.description: Whenever you activate your Second Wind, you can move up to half your Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

mechanics.trigger: Activate Second Wind
mechanics.bonus: Move up to half Speed (no Opportunity Attacks)

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Fighter subclass.

features.name: Extra Attack (2)
features.description: You can attack three times instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Indomitable (2 uses)
features.description: You can now use Indomitable twice before a Long Rest.

features.name: Studied Attacks
features.description: You study your enemies and learn from each attack you make. If you make an attack roll against a creature and miss, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

mechanics.trigger: Miss an attack roll against a creature
mechanics.benefit: Advantage on your next attack roll against that same creature
mechanics.window: Before the end of your next turn

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Fighter subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Action Surge (2 uses)
features.description: You can now use Action Surge twice before a rest, though still only once per turn.

features.name: Indomitable (3 uses)
features.description: You can now use Indomitable three times before a Long Rest.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Fighter subclass.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain the Epic Boon of Combat Prowess feat or another Epic Boon feat of your choice.

mechanics.type: Epic Boon feat (e.g. Epic Boon of Combat Prowess)

features.name: Extra Attack (3)
features.description: You can attack four times instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

subclasses.name: Battle Master
subclasses.description: Battle Masters are students of the art of battle, learning fighting techniques passed down through generations. They study not just how to fight, but how to think tactically in any situation, empowering their allies and exploiting every weakness of their foes.

features.name: Combat Superiority
features.description: Your experience on the battlefield has honed your fighting techniques. You learn maneuvers that are fueled by special dice called Superiority Dice. You have four Superiority Dice, which are d8s. A Superiority Die is expended when you use it. You regain all your expended Superiority Dice when you finish a Short or Long Rest. You use Superiority Dice to fuel your maneuvers. You know three maneuvers of your choice from the list below. You learn two additional maneuvers of your choice when you reach Fighter levels 7, 10, and 15. Each time you learn new maneuvers, you can also replace one maneuver you know with a different one. Saving Throws: Some maneuvers require your target to make a saving throw to resist the maneuver's effects. The saving throw DC equals 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice).

mechanics.saving_throw_dc: 8 + proficiency bonus + STR or DEX modifier (your choice)

maneuvers.name: Ambush
maneuvers.description: When you make a Dexterity (Stealth) check or an Initiative roll, you can expend one Superiority Die and add the die to the roll, provided you aren't Incapacitated.

maneuvers.name: Bait and Switch
maneuvers.description: When you're within 5 feet of a creature on your turn, you can expend one Superiority Die and switch places with that creature, provided you spend at least 5 feet of movement and the creature is willing and isn't Incapacitated. This movement doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks. Roll the Superiority Die. Until the start of your next turn, you or the other creature (your choice) gains a bonus to AC equal to the number rolled.

maneuvers.name: Commander's Strike
maneuvers.description: When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can forgo one of your attacks and use a Bonus Action to direct one of your companions to strike. When you do so, choose a friendly creature who can see or hear you and expend one Superiority Die. That creature can immediately use its Reaction to make one attack with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike, adding the Superiority Die to the attack's damage roll.

maneuvers.name: Commanding Presence
maneuvers.description: When you make a Charisma (Intimidation), Charisma (Performance), or Charisma (Persuasion) check, you can expend one Superiority Die and add the die to the ability check.

maneuvers.name: Disarming Attack
maneuvers.description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can expend one Superiority Die to attempt to disarm the target. Add the Superiority Die to the attack's damage roll. The target must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, it drops one object of your choice that it's holding. The object lands in its space.

maneuvers.name: Distracting Strike
maneuvers.description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can expend one Superiority Die to distract the target. Add the Superiority Die to the attack's damage roll. The next attack roll against the target by an attacker other than you has Advantage if the attack is made before the start of your next turn.

maneuvers.name: Evasive Footwork
maneuvers.description: When you move at least 5 feet on your turn, you can expend one Superiority Die, rolling it and adding the number rolled to your AC until you stop moving.

maneuvers.name: Feinting Attack
maneuvers.description: You can expend one Superiority Die and use a Bonus Action on your turn to feint, choosing one creature within 5 feet of you as your target. You have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature this turn. If that attack hits, add the Superiority Die to the attack's damage roll.

maneuvers.name: Goading Attack
maneuvers.description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can expend one Superiority Die to attempt to goad the target into attacking you. Add the Superiority Die to the attack's damage roll. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target has Disadvantage on all attack rolls against targets other than you until the end of your next turn.

maneuvers.name: Lunging Attack
maneuvers.description: When you make a melee attack on your turn, you can expend one Superiority Die to increase your reach for that attack by 5 feet. If you hit, you add the Superiority Die to the attack's damage roll.

maneuvers.name: Maneuvering Attack
maneuvers.description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can expend one Superiority Die to maneuver one of your comrades into a more advantageous position. Add the Superiority Die to the attack's damage roll, and choose a friendly creature who can see or hear you. That creature can use its Reaction to move up to half its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks from the target of your attack.

maneuvers.name: Menacing Attack
maneuvers.description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can expend one Superiority Die to attempt to frighten the target. Add the Superiority Die to the attack's damage roll. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it has the Frightened condition until the end of your next turn.

maneuvers.name: Parry
maneuvers.description: When another creature damages you with a melee attack, you can use your Reaction and expend one Superiority Die to reduce the damage by the number you roll on your Superiority Die plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice).

maneuvers.name: Precision Attack
maneuvers.description: When you make an attack roll against a creature, you can expend one Superiority Die to add it to the roll. You can use this maneuver before or after making the attack roll, but before any effects of the attack are applied.

maneuvers.name: Pushing Attack
maneuvers.description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can expend one Superiority Die to attempt to drive the target back. Add the Superiority Die to the attack's damage roll. If the target is Large or smaller, it must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, you push the target up to 15 feet away from you.

maneuvers.name: Quick Toss
maneuvers.description: As a Bonus Action, you can expend one Superiority Die and make a ranged attack with a weapon that has the Thrown property. You can draw the weapon as part of making this attack. If you hit, add the Superiority Die to the weapon's damage roll.

maneuvers.name: Rally
maneuvers.description: On your turn, you can use a Bonus Action and expend one Superiority Die to bolster the resolve of one of your companions. When you do so, choose a friendly creature who can see or hear you. That creature gains Temporary Hit Points equal to the Superiority Die roll plus your Charisma modifier.

maneuvers.name: Riposte
maneuvers.description: When a creature misses you with a melee attack, you can use your Reaction and expend one Superiority Die to make a melee attack against the creature. If you hit, add the Superiority Die to the attack's damage roll.

maneuvers.name: Sweeping Attack
maneuvers.description: When you hit a creature with a melee attack, you can expend one Superiority Die to attempt to damage another creature with the same attack. Choose another creature within 5 feet of the original target and within your reach. If the original attack roll would hit the second creature, it takes damage equal to the number you roll on your Superiority Die. The damage is of the same type dealt by the original attack.

maneuvers.name: Tactical Assessment
maneuvers.description: When you make an Intelligence (History), Intelligence (Investigation), or Wisdom (Insight) check, you can expend one Superiority Die and add the die to the ability check.

maneuvers.name: Trip Attack
maneuvers.description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can expend one Superiority Die to attempt to knock the target down. Add the Superiority Die to the attack's damage roll. If the target is Large or smaller, it must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the Prone condition.

features.name: Student of War
features.description: You gain proficiency with one type of Artisan's Tools of your choice.

mechanics.proficiency_gained: One type of Artisan's Tools (your choice)

features.name: Know Your Enemy
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can expend one Superiority Die to size up another creature you can see, making an Insight check contested by the target's Deception check. On a success, you learn two of the following characteristics of your choice about the target: Armor Class, Current Hit Points, Total Class Levels (if any), Fighter levels (if any), Strength score, Dexterity score, Constitution score.

features.name: Improved Combat Superiority
features.description: Your Superiority Die becomes a d10.

features.name: Relentless
features.description: Once per turn, when you use a maneuver, you can roll a d3 and recover that many expended Superiority Dice.

mechanics.trigger: Use a maneuver
mechanics.recovery: Roll 1d3 and recover that many Superiority Dice

features.name: Improved Combat Superiority (d12)
features.description: Your Superiority Die becomes a d12.

subclasses.name: Champion
subclasses.description: A Champion focuses on the development of raw physical power honed to deadly perfection. Those who model themselves on this archetype combine rigorous training with physical excellence to deal devastating blows.

features.name: Improved Critical
features.description: Your attack rolls with weapons and Unarmed Strikes can score a Critical Hit on a roll of 19 or 20 on the d20.

features.name: Remarkable Athlete
features.description: Thanks to your athleticism, you have Advantage on Initiative rolls and Strength (Athletics) checks. In addition, when you make a running long jump, the distance you can cover increases by a number of feet equal to your Strength modifier.

features.name: Additional Fighting Style
features.description: You gain another Fighting Style feat of your choice.

features.name: Heroic Warrior
features.description: The thrill of battle drives you toward victory. During combat, you can give yourself Heroic Inspiration whenever you start your turn without it.

mechanics.trigger: Start your turn without Heroic Inspiration (during combat)
mechanics.benefit: Gain Heroic Inspiration

features.name: Superior Critical
features.description: Your attack rolls with weapons and Unarmed Strikes can now score a Critical Hit on a roll of 18–20 on the d20.

features.name: Survivor
features.description: You attain the pinnacle of resilience in battle, giving you these benefits: Defy Death — You have Advantage on Death Saving Throws. Heroic Rally — At the start of each of your turns, if you have at least 1 Hit Point and are at half your Hit Point maximum or lower, you regain Hit Points equal to 5 plus your Constitution modifier.

heroic_rally.condition: At least 1 HP and at or below half your HP maximum

subclasses.name: Eldritch Knight
subclasses.description: The archetypal Eldritch Knight combines the martial mastery common to all Fighters with a careful study of magic. Eldritch Knights use magical techniques similar to those practiced by Wizards, focusing on spells of abjuration and evocation.

spellcasting.restriction: Abjuration and Evocation spells only (with exceptions at levels 3, 8, 14, 20)

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You have learned to cast spells. See the Spellcasting section for the full rules. You learn two cantrips of your choice from the Wizard spell list. Most spells you know must be from the Abjuration or Evocation school of magic. At levels 3, 8, 14, and 20 you can learn one spell from any school of magic.

features.name: War Bond
features.description: You learn a ritual that creates a magical bond between yourself and one weapon. You perform the ritual over the course of 1 hour, which can be done during a Short Rest. The weapon must be within your reach throughout the ritual. At the conclusion of the ritual, you touch the weapon and forge the bond. Once you have bonded a weapon to yourself, you can't be disarmed of that weapon unless you have the Incapacitated condition. If it is on the same plane of existence, you can summon that weapon as a Bonus Action on your turn, causing it to teleport instantly to your hand. You can have up to two bonded weapons, but can summon only one at a time with your Bonus Action. If you attempt to bond with a third weapon, you must break the bond with one of the other two.

mechanics.ritual_time: 1 hour (can be done during a Short Rest)
benefits: Bonus Action to summon bonded weapon to your hand (same plane)

features.name: War Magic
features.description: When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can replace one of the attacks with a casting of one of your Wizard cantrips that has a casting time of an action.

mechanics.trigger: Take the Attack action
mechanics.effect: Replace one attack with a Wizard cantrip (casting time: Action)

features.name: Eldritch Strike
features.description: You learn how to make your weapon strikes undercut a creature's resistance to your spells. When you hit a creature with an attack using a weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes against a spell you cast before the end of your next turn.

mechanics.trigger: Hit a creature with a weapon attack
mechanics.effect: Target has Disadvantage on the next saving throw against your spells before end of your next turn

features.name: Arcane Charge
features.description: When you use Action Surge, you can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You can teleport before or after the additional action.

mechanics.trigger: Use Action Surge
mechanics.effect: Teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see (before or after the extra action)

features.name: Improved War Magic
features.description: When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can replace two of the attacks with a casting of one of your Wizard spells that has a casting time of an action.

mechanics.trigger: Take the Attack action
mechanics.effect: Replace two attacks with one Wizard spell (casting time: Action)

subclasses.name: Psi Warrior
subclasses.description: Psi Warriors awaken the power of their minds to augment their physical might with psionic power. As a Psi Warrior, you could be a soldier gifted with latent psionic ability, an adept who has been trained to use psionic magic, or a warrior who has unlocked their psionic potential through a combination of meditation and combat.

resource.name: Psionic Energy Dice
resource.recharge: Long Rest; can recharge 1 die as Bonus Action (once per Short or Long Rest)

features.name: Psionic Power
features.description: You harbor a wellspring of psionic energy within yourself. This energy is represented by your Psionic Energy Dice, which are each a d6. You have a number of these dice equal to twice your Proficiency Bonus, and they fuel the following features: Protective Field, Psionic Strike, Telekinetic Movement. Any features that use Psionic Energy Dice are detailed below. When you use a Psionic Energy Die, it is expended. You regain all your expended Psionic Energy Dice when you finish a Long Rest. In addition, as a Bonus Action, you can regain one expended die, but you can't use this Bonus Action again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

mechanics.recharge: Long Rest; 1 die via Bonus Action (once per Short or Long Rest)

features.name: Protective Field
features.description: When you or another creature you can see within 30 feet of you takes damage, you can use your Reaction to expend one Psionic Energy Die, roll the die, and reduce the damage taken by the number rolled plus your Intelligence modifier (minimum reduction of 1), as you create a momentary shield of telekinetic force.

mechanics.trigger: You or a creature within 30 ft takes damage

features.name: Psionic Strike
features.description: You can propel your weapons with psionic force. Once on each of your turns, immediately after you hit a target within 30 feet of you with an attack and deal damage to it with a weapon, you can expend one Psionic Energy Die, rolling it and dealing Force damage to the target equal to the number rolled plus your Intelligence modifier.

mechanics.trigger: Hit a target within 30 feet with a weapon attack

features.name: Telekinetic Movement
features.description: You can move an object or a creature with your mind. As an Action, you target one loose object that is Large or smaller or one willing creature, other than yourself. If you can see the target and it is within 30 feet of you, you can move it up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Alternatively, if it is a Tiny object, you can move it to or from your hand. If you move a creature, the creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC 8 plus your Intelligence modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or be moved. Either way, you can't move the target if doing so would move it into a space occupied by another creature or object, and if the target is a creature, the movement doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks. Once you take this Action, you can't do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest, unless you expend a Psionic Energy Die to take it again.

mechanics.target: Loose Large-or-smaller object OR willing creature (not yourself) within 30 ft
mechanics.effect: Move target up to 30 ft to unoccupied space you can see
mechanics.tiny_objects: Move to or from your hand
mechanics.creature_save: Strength DC = 8 + INT modifier + PB (unwilling creatures)
mechanics.reuse: Once per Short/Long Rest, or expend a Psionic Energy Die

features.name: Telekinetic Adept
features.description: You have mastered new ways to use your telekinetic abilities, detailed below. Psi-Powered Leap — As a Bonus Action, you can propel your body with your mind. You gain a Fly Speed equal to twice your Speed until the end of the current turn. Once you take this Bonus Action, you can't do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest, unless you expend a Psionic Energy Die to take it again. Telekinetic Thrust — When you deal damage to a target with your Psionic Strike, you can force the target to make a Strength saving throw (DC 8 plus your Intelligence modifier and Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, you can knock the target Prone or move it up to 10 feet in any direction horizontally.

psi_powered_leap.effect: Fly Speed = 2× Speed until end of turn
psi_powered_leap.reuse: Once per Short/Long Rest, or expend a Psionic Energy Die

telekinetic_thrust.on_failure: Knock Prone OR move up to 10 ft horizontally (your choice)

features.name: Guarded Mind
features.description: The psionic power flowing through you has bolstered your mind. You have Resistance to Psychic damage. Moreover, if you start your turn Charmed or Frightened, you can expend a Psionic Energy Die and end every effect on yourself causing those conditions.

condition_removal.trigger: Start your turn Charmed or Frightened
condition_removal.effect: End all Charmed and Frightened effects on yourself

features.name: Bulwark of Force
features.description: You can shield yourself and others with telekinetic force. As a Bonus Action, you can choose creatures, which can include you, that you can see within 30 feet of you, up to a number of creatures equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of one creature). Each of the chosen creatures is protected by half cover for 1 minute or until you have the Incapacitated condition. Once you take this Bonus Action, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest, unless you expend a Psionic Energy Die to take it again.

mechanics.targets: Up to INT modifier creatures (min 1) within 30 ft (can include yourself)
mechanics.effect: Half cover for 1 minute or until you are Incapacitated
mechanics.reuse: Once per Long Rest, or expend a Psionic Energy Die

features.name: Telekinetic Master
features.description: Your ability to move creatures and objects with your mind is matched by few. You can cast the Telekinesis spell, requiring no spell slot or components, and your spellcasting ability for the spell is Intelligence. On each of your turns while you concentrate on the spell, including the turn you cast it, you can make one attack with a weapon as a Bonus Action. Once you cast the spell with this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest, unless you expend a Psionic Energy Die to cast it again.

mechanics.bonus: While concentrating on the spell, make one weapon attack as Bonus Action each turn
mechanics.reuse: Once per Long Rest, or expend a Psionic Energy Die

class.name: Rogue
weapon_proficiencies: Martial weapons that have the Finesse property
multiclass_proficiencies_gained: one skill from the Rogue list

features.name: Expertise
features.description: You gain Expertise in two of your skill proficiencies of your choice. Expertise means your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make using the chosen proficiency. At Rogue level 7, you gain Expertise in two more of your skill proficiencies.

mechanics.effect: Double proficiency bonus on chosen skills

features.name: Sneak Attack
features.description: You know how to strike subtly and exploit a foe's distraction. Once per turn, you can deal extra damage to one creature you hit with an attack roll if you have Advantage on the roll and the attack uses a Finesse or Ranged weapon. The extra damage's type is the same as the weapon's type. You don't need Advantage on the attack roll if at least one of your allies is within 5 feet of the target, the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition, and you don't have Disadvantage on the attack roll.

conditions: You have Advantage on the attack roll
OR: An ally without Incapacitated is within 5 feet of the target AND you don't have Disadvantage

features.name: Thieves' Cant
features.description: You picked up various languages in the communities where you plied your trade. You know Thieves' Cant and one other language of your choice. Thieves' Cant is a secret mix of dialect, jargon, and code that allows you to hide messages in seemingly normal conversation. Only another creature that knows Thieves' Cant understands such messages. It takes four times longer to convey such a message than it does to speak the same idea plainly. In addition, you understand a set of secret signs and symbols used to convey short, simple messages, such as whether an area is dangerous or the territory of a Thieves' Guild.

mechanics.secret_messages: Hidden in normal conversation; 4x longer to convey; only understood by others who know Thieves' Cant
mechanics.symbols: Understand secret signs for short messages

features.name: Weapon Mastery
features.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency, such as Daggers and Shortbows. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change one of those weapon choices. When you reach certain Rogue levels, you gain the ability to use the mastery properties of more weapon types, as shown in the Weapon Mastery column of the Rogue Features table.

features.name: Cunning Action
features.description: Your quick thinking and agility allow you to move and act quickly. On your turn, you can take a Bonus Action to take one of the following actions: Dash, Disengage, or Hide.

features.name: Rogue Subclass
features.description: You gain a Rogue subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Rogue levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Rogue level or lower.

features.name: Steady Aim
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you give yourself Advantage on your next attack roll on the current turn. You can use this feature only if you haven't moved during this turn, and after you use it, your Speed is 0 until the end of the current turn.

mechanics.benefit: Advantage on your next attack roll this turn
mechanics.requirement: Must not have moved this turn
mechanics.cost: Speed becomes 0 until end of turn

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Rogue levels 8, 10, 12, 16, and 19.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Cunning Strike
features.description: You have developed cunning ways to use your Sneak Attack. When you deal Sneak Attack damage, you can add one of the following Cunning Strike effects. Each effect has a die cost that reduces the number of Sneak Attack dice you roll: Disarm (Cost: 1d6) — The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus), or it drops one item of your choice that it's holding. Distract (Cost: 1d6) — The target has Disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes before the start of your next turn. Knock Prone (Cost: 1d6) — The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus), or it has the Prone condition. Poison (Cost: 1d6) — You add a toxin to your strike, forcing the target to make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the target has the Poisoned condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the Poisoned target repeats this save, ending the effect on itself on a success. Trip (Cost: 1d6) — If the target is Large or smaller, it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus), or it has the Prone condition.

mechanics.trigger: Deal Sneak Attack damage
mechanics.note: Each effect costs Sneak Attack dice (subtracted from total rolled)

effects.name: Disarm
effects.failure: Drops one held item of your choice

effects.name: Distract
effects.effect: Target has Disadvantage on next saving throw before start of your next turn

effects.name: Poison
effects.failure: Poisoned condition for 1 minute; repeats save at end of each turn

features.name: Uncanny Dodge
features.description: When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can use your Reaction to halve the attack's damage against you (round down).

mechanics.trigger: Attacker you can see hits you with an attack roll
mechanics.effect: Halve the attack's damage (round down)

features.name: Expertise (2 more)
features.description: You gain Expertise in two more of your skill proficiencies of your choice.

features.name: Evasion
features.description: You can nimbly dodge out of the way of certain dangers. When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if you fail. You can't use this feature if you have the Incapacitated condition.

mechanics.trigger: Subjected to effect requiring Dexterity saving throw for half damage

features.name: Reliable Talent
features.description: Whenever you make an ability check that uses one of your skill or tool proficiencies, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

mechanics.benefit: Treat any d20 roll of 9 or lower as 10 on proficient skill or tool checks

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Rogue subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Improved Cunning Strike
features.description: You can use up to two Cunning Strike effects when you deal Sneak Attack damage, paying the die cost for each effect.

mechanics.note: Each effect still costs its die amount from Sneak Attack dice

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Rogue subclass.

features.name: Devious Strikes
features.description: You have practiced these Cunning Strike effects so often that they have become more devious. The following effects are now available to you when using Cunning Strike, and the listed effects are improved: Daze (Cost: 2d6) — The target has the Dazed condition until the start of your next turn. Knock Out (Cost: 6d6) — The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus), or it has the Unconscious condition for 1 minute or until it takes any damage.
