Poison (Improved) — The Poison now costs 2d6 Sneak Attack dice.

new_effects.name: Daze
new_effects.effect: Target has the Dazed condition until start of your next turn

new_effects.name: Knock Out
new_effects.failure: Unconscious for 1 minute or until taking any damage

features.name: Slippery Mind
features.description: Your cunning mind is exceptionally difficult to control. You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws. If you already have proficiency in Wisdom saving throws, you instead gain proficiency in Intelligence or Charisma saving throws (your choice).

mechanics.if_already_proficient: Gain proficiency in Intelligence or Charisma saving throws (your choice)

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Rogue subclass.

features.name: Elusive
features.description: You are so evasive that attackers rarely gain the upper hand against you. No attack roll has Advantage against you while you don't have the Incapacitated condition.

mechanics.benefit: No attack roll has Advantage against you

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain the Epic Boon of Skullduggery feat or another Epic Boon feat of your choice.

mechanics.type: Epic Boon feat (e.g. Epic Boon of Skullduggery)

features.name: Stroke of Luck
features.description: You have an uncanny knack for succeeding when you need to. If you fail a d20 Test, you can turn the roll into a 20. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

mechanics.trigger: Fail a d20 Test
mechanics.effect: Turn the roll into a 20

subclasses.name: Arcane Trickster
subclasses.description: Some Rogues enhance their fine-honed skills of stealth and agility with magic, learning tricks of enchantment and illusion. These Rogues include pickpockets and burglars, but also pranksters, mischief-makers, and a significant number of adventurers.

spellcasting.spell_list: Wizard (primarily Enchantment and Illusion; one spell from any school at levels 3, 8, 14, 20)

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You have learned to untangle and reshape the fabric of reality in harmony with your wishes and magic. You know three 1st-level Wizard spells of your choice, two of which must be from the Enchantment and Illusion schools of magic.

mechanics.restriction: Two of three starting spells must be Enchantment or Illusion
mechanics.exception: At levels 8, 14, and 20 you may choose a spell from any Wizard school

features.name: Mage Hand Legerdemain
features.description: When you cast Mage Hand, you can make the spectral hand invisible. You can control the hand as a Bonus Action instead of an Action. In addition, you can perform the following tasks with the hand: stow one object the hand is holding in a container worn or carried by another creature; retrieve an object in a container worn or carried by another creature; pickpocket a creature. If you attempt to pickpocket a creature, that creature makes a Perception check contested by your Sleight of Hand check, with you having Advantage on your check. You can use the hand to perform these tasks without being noticed by a creature if you succeed on a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check contested by the creature's Wisdom (Perception) check.

mage_hand_upgrades: Control as Bonus Action instead of Action
Stow object in container worn/carried by another creature
Retrieve object from container worn/carried by another creature
mechanics.stealth_check: Sleight of Hand vs Perception (Advantage on pickpocket)

features.name: Magical Ambush
features.description: If you are hidden when you cast a spell on a creature, it has Disadvantage on any saving throw it makes against the spell on the same turn.

mechanics.condition: You are hidden when you cast the spell
mechanics.effect: Target has Disadvantage on saving throws against that spell on the same turn

features.name: Versatile Trickster
features.description: You gain the ability to distract targets with your Mage Hand. When you use the Mage Hand cantrip, you can designate a creature within 5 feet of the spectral hand created by the spell. Doing so gives you Advantage on attack rolls against that creature until the end of the current turn.

mechanics.trigger: Use Mage Hand cantrip
mechanics.effect: Designate a creature within 5 ft of the hand; gain Advantage on attack rolls against it until end of turn

features.name: Spell Thief
features.description: You gain the ability to magically steal the knowledge of how to cast a spell from another spellcaster. Immediately after a creature casts a spell that targets you or includes you in its area of effect, you can use your Reaction to force the creature to make a saving throw with its spellcasting ability modifier. The DC equals your spell save DC. On a failed save, you negate the spell's effect against you, and you steal the knowledge of the spell if it is at least 1st level and of a level you can cast (it doesn't need to be a Wizard spell). For the next 8 hours, you know the spell and can cast it using your spell slots. The creature can't cast that spell until the 8 hours have passed. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.trigger: A creature casts a spell targeting you or including you in its area
mechanics.saving_throw: Spellcasting ability save vs your spell save DC
failure: You steal knowledge of the spell (if 1st level+, castable by you)
You know and can cast the spell for 8 hours using your spell slots
Original caster can't cast that spell for 8 hours
mechanics.restriction: Spell must be at least 1st level and of a level you can cast

subclasses.name: Assassin
subclasses.description: You focus your training on the grim art of death. Those who adhere to this archetype are diverse — hired killers, spies, bounty hunters, and even specially anointed priests trained to exterminate the enemies of their deity.

features.name: Assassinate
features.description: You are at your deadliest when you get the drop on your enemies. You have Advantage on attack rolls against any creature that hasn't taken a turn in the combat yet. In addition, any hit you score against a creature that is Surprised is a Critical Hit.

mechanics.advantage_condition: Against creatures that haven't taken a turn yet in combat
mechanics.critical_hit_condition: Any hit against a Surprised creature is a Critical Hit

features.name: Assassin's Tools
features.description: You gain a Disguise Kit and a Poisoner's Kit, and you gain proficiency with both.

features.name: Infiltration Expertise
features.description: You are expert at posing as someone else to aid your infiltrations. While you are wearing a disguise created using your Disguise Kit, you have Advantage on Charisma (Deception) checks (provided the disguise is plausible and you don't take actions that make it obvious you are disguised).

mechanics.condition: Wearing a disguise created with your Disguise Kit (and disguise is plausible)
mechanics.benefit: Advantage on Charisma (Deception) checks

features.name: Envenom Weapons
features.description: When you use the Poison option of your Cunning Strike, the target also takes 2d6 Poison damage when it fails the saving throw.

mechanics.additional_damage: 2d6 Poison damage on failed saving throw

features.name: Death Strike
features.description: You become a master of instant death. When you hit a creature with your Assassinate feature, the creature must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, double the damage of your attack against the creature.

mechanics.trigger: Hit a creature using your Assassinate feature
mechanics.saving_throw: Constitution DC = 8 + DEX modifier + proficiency bonus
mechanics.failure: Double the damage of the attack

subclasses.name: Soulknife
subclasses.description: Most Rogues rely on physical tools and tactics to ply their trade, but the Soulknife strikes with something deadlier: the mind itself. Armed with psionic power, a Soulknife can not only strike foes with a psychic blade, but also delve into the psyche of others to bend their will to the Soulknife's own ends.

features.name: Psionic Power
features.description: You harbor a wellspring of psionic energy within yourself. This energy is represented by your Psionic Energy Dice, which are each a d6. You have a number of these dice equal to twice your proficiency bonus, and they fuel various psionic powers you have, which are detailed below. Some of your powers expend the Psionic Energy Die they use, as specified in a power's description, and you can't use a power if it requires you to use a die when your dice are all expended. You regain all your expended Psionic Energy Dice when you finish a Long Rest. In addition, as a Bonus Action, you can regain one expended Psionic Energy Die, but you can't do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

mechanics.recharge_one: Bonus Action (once per Short or Long Rest)

psionic_powers.name: Psi-Bolstered Knack
psionic_powers.description: When your nonpsionic training fails you, your psionic power can help: if you fail an ability check using a skill or tool with which you have proficiency, you can roll one Psionic Energy Die and add it to the check, potentially turning failure into success. You expend the die only if the roll succeeds.

psionic_powers.name: Psychic Whispers
psionic_powers.description: You can establish telepathic communication between yourself and others. As a Magic action, you choose one or more creatures you can see, up to a number of creatures equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you roll one Psionic Energy Die. For a number of hours equal to the number rolled, the chosen creatures can speak telepathically with you, and you can speak telepathically with them. To send or receive a message (no action required), you and the other creature must be within 1 mile of each other. A creature can end the telepathic connection at any time (no action required). You and the creature don't need to share a language to communicate this way, but the creature must be able to understand at least one language.

features.name: Psychic Blades
features.description: You can manifest your psionic power as shimmering blades of psychic energy. Whenever you take the Attack action or make an Opportunity Attack, you can manifest a Psychic Blade in your free hand and make the attack with that blade. The magic blade has the following traits: Damage — 1d6 Psychic. Range — Thrown (60/120 ft). Properties — Finesse, Thrown. Mastery — Vex. The blade vanishes immediately after it hits or misses its target, and it leaves no mark on its target. You can't manifest the blade if you're holding something in your free hand, and the blade doesn't count as a weapon for the purpose of spells and effects that affect weapons.

mechanics.activation: When taking Attack action or making Opportunity Attack
notes: Vanishes immediately after hitting or missing
Cannot manifest if holding something in free hand
Does not count as a weapon for spells/effects affecting weapons

features.name: Soul Blades
features.description: Your Psychic Blades are now an expression of your psi-suffused soul, giving you these powers that use Psionic Energy Dice: Homing Strikes — If you make an attack roll with your Psychic Blade and miss the target, you can roll one Psionic Energy Die and add the number rolled to the attack roll. If this causes the attack to hit, you expend the die. Psychic Teleportation — As a Bonus Action, you manifest a Psychic Blade, expend one Psionic Energy Die and roll it, and throw the blade at an unoccupied space you can see, up to a number of feet away equal to 10 times the number rolled. You then teleport to that space, and the blade vanishes.

homing_strikes.effect: Roll one Psionic Energy Die and add to attack roll; expend die only if it causes a hit

psychic_teleportation.effect: Expend and roll one Psionic Energy Die; teleport to unoccupied space up to 10 × roll feet away

features.name: Psychic Veil
features.description: You can weave a veil of psychic static to mask yourself. As a Magic action, you can give yourself the Invisible condition, which lasts for 1 hour or until you dismiss it (no action required). This invisibility ends early immediately after you deal damage to a creature or you force a creature to make a saving throw. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest, unless you expend a Psionic Energy Die (no action required) to restore your use of it.

mechanics.effect: Invisible condition
ends_early_if: Force a creature to make a saving throw
mechanics.recharge: Long Rest, or expend one Psionic Energy Die (no action)

features.name: Rend Mind
features.description: You can sweep your Psychic Blades through a creature's mind. When you use your Psychic Blades to deal Sneak Attack damage to a creature, you can force that creature to make a Wisdom saving throw (DC equal to 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus). Unless the save succeeds, the creature has the Stunned condition until the end of your next turn. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest, unless you expend three Psionic Energy Dice (no action required) to restore your use of it.

mechanics.trigger: Use Psychic Blades to deal Sneak Attack damage
mechanics.saving_throw: Wisdom DC = 8 + DEX modifier + proficiency bonus
mechanics.failure: Stunned until end of your next turn
mechanics.recharge: Long Rest, or expend 3 Psionic Energy Dice (no action)

subclasses.name: Thief
subclasses.description: You hone your skills in the larcenous arts. Burglars, bandits, cutpurses, and other criminals typically follow this archetype, but so do Rogues who prefer to think of themselves as professional treasure seekers, explorers, delvers, and investigators.

features.name: Fast Hands
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can do one of the following: Make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check to pick a pocket. Use your Thieves' Tools to disarm a trap or open a lock. Take the Use an Object action.

options: Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check to pick a pocket
Use Thieves' Tools to disarm a trap or open a lock
Take the Use an Object action

features.name: Second-Story Work
features.description: You have trained to get into especially hard-to-reach places. You can climb at your normal speed. In addition, when you make a running jump, the distance you cover increases by a number of feet equal to your Dexterity modifier.

features.name: Supreme Sneak
features.description: You gain the following benefits: Cunning Action: Hide — As a Bonus Action, you can take the Hide action even if you have no cover, provided you end the movement portion of that action within 5 feet of a Large or larger creature. Rapid Stealth — If you take the Hide action as part of your Cunning Action, you don't have Disadvantage on the Dexterity (Stealth) check from moving at normal Speed this turn.

mechanics.cunning_action_hide_upgrade: Can Hide even without cover if ending movement within 5 ft of Large+ creature
mechanics.rapid_stealth: No Disadvantage on Stealth check from moving at normal speed during Cunning Action: Hide

features.name: Use Magic Device
features.description: In your thieving escapades, you have learned how to maximize the use of magic items. You can now ignore all class, species, and level requirements on the use of magic items.

mechanics.benefit: Ignore all class, species, and level requirements on magic item use

features.name: Thief's Reflexes
features.description: You are adept at laying ambushes and quickly escaping danger. You can take two turns during the first round of any combat. You take your first turn at your normal Initiative and your second turn at your Initiative minus 10. You can't use this feature when you are Surprised.

mechanics.effect: Two turns during the first round of combat

spellcasting.ritual_note: You can cast any Cleric spell you have prepared as a ritual if it has the ritual tag.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You have learned to draw on divine magic through meditation and prayer to cast spells. You prepare the list of Cleric spells available to you by choosing from the Cleric spell list. When you do so, choose a number of Cleric spells equal to your Wisdom modifier plus your Cleric level (minimum of one spell). The spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. You can change your list of prepared spells when you finish a Long Rest. Preparing a new list requires time spent in prayer and meditation: at least 1 minute per spell level for each spell on your list.

mechanics.change_prepared: Long Rest (1 minute per spell level per spell)

features.name: Divine Order
features.description: You have dedicated yourself to one of the following sacred roles of your choice: Protector — Trained for battle, you gain proficiency with Martial weapons and training with Heavy armor. Thaumaturge — You know one extra cantrip from the Cleric spell list. In addition, your mystical connection to the divine gives you a bonus to your Intelligence (Arcana or Religion) checks. The bonus equals your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1).

options.name: Thaumaturge
benefits: One extra cantrip from the Cleric spell list
Bonus to Intelligence (Arcana or Religion) checks equal to Wisdom modifier (minimum +1)

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: You gain the ability to channel divine energy directly from the Outer Planes, using that energy to fuel magical effects. You start with two such effects: Divine Spark and Turn Undead, each of which is described below. Each time you use Channel Divinity, you choose which effect to create, and you gain additional effects as you progress in this class. You can use Channel Divinity twice. You regain one expended use when you finish a Short Rest, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. You gain additional uses of Channel Divinity at higher Cleric levels, as shown in the Channel Divinity column of the Cleric Features table.

mechanics.recharge: 1 use on Short Rest, all uses on Long Rest

effects.name: Divine Spark
effects.description: As a Magic action, you point your Holy Symbol at another creature you can see within 30 feet of you and focus divine energy at it. Roll 1d8 and add your Wisdom modifier. You either restore Hit Points to the creature equal to that total or force the creature to make a Constitution saving throw (DC equal to your spell save DC). On a failed save, the creature takes Radiant or Necrotic damage (your choice) equal to that total. The number of dice you roll increases as you gain Cleric levels: at level 7 roll 2d8, at level 13 roll 3d8, and at level 18 roll 4d8.

mechanics.effect_choice: Restore HP equal to total OR deal Radiant/Necrotic damage on failed CON save (DC = spell save DC)

effects.name: Turn Undead
effects.description: As a Magic action, you present your Holy Symbol and speak a prayer censuring Undead creatures. Each Undead that can see or hear you within 30 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw. If the creature fails its saving throw, it is Turned for 1 minute or until it takes any damage. A Turned creature must take the Dash action and move away from you by the safest available route on each of its turns, unless there is nowhere to move. If the creature has nowhere it can move, it can use the Dodge action.

mechanics.targets: All Undead that can see or hear you
mechanics.failure: Turned for 1 minute or until taking any damage
mechanics.turned_behavior: Must Dash away from you by safest route; can Dodge if nowhere to move

features.name: Cleric Subclass
features.description: You gain a Cleric subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Cleric levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Cleric level or lower.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Cleric levels 8, 12, 16, and 19.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Sear Undead
features.description: Whenever you use Turn Undead, you can roll a number of d8s equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1d8) and add the rolls together. Each Undead that fails its saving throw against that use of Turn Undead takes Radiant damage equal to the total rolled. This damage doesn't end the turn effect.

mechanics.trigger: Use Turn Undead
mechanics.note: Does not end the Turned condition

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Cleric subclass.

features.name: Channel Divinity (3 uses)
features.description: You now have 3 uses of Channel Divinity.

features.name: Blessed Strikes
features.description: Divine power infuses your strikes. When you deal damage with one of your Cleric cantrips or with a weapon, you can also deal 1d8 Radiant damage to the target. You can use this benefit only once per turn, and it requires no action.

mechanics.trigger: Deal damage with a Cleric cantrip or weapon

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Cleric subclass.

features.name: Divine Intervention
features.description: You can call on your deity or the divine forces you serve for a miraculous intervention. As a Magic action, choose any Cleric spell of 5th level or lower that doesn't require a Reaction to cast. As part of the same action, you cast that spell without expending a spell slot or needing to have it prepared. You can't use this feature again until you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.effect: Cast any Cleric spell of 5th level or lower (not requiring Reaction) without expending a slot or preparing it

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Cleric subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Improved Blessed Strikes
features.description: The Radiant damage of your Blessed Strikes increases to 2d8.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Cleric subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Channel Divinity (4 uses)
features.description: You now have 4 uses of Channel Divinity.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain the Epic Boon of Fate feat or another Epic Boon feat of your choice.

mechanics.type: Epic Boon feat (e.g. Epic Boon of Fate)

features.name: Greater Divine Intervention
features.description: Your Divine Intervention now works more powerfully. When you use it, you can choose a Cleric spell of 8th level or lower that doesn't require a Reaction to cast.

mechanics.note: Same mechanics as Divine Intervention but up to 8th level spells

subclasses.name: Life Domain
subclasses.description: The Life Domain focuses on the vibrant positive energy — one of the fundamental forces of the universe — that sustains all life. The gods of life promote vitality and health through healing the sick and wounded, caring for those in need, and driving away the forces of death and undeath.
subclasses.domain_spells_note: Domain spells are always prepared and don't count against your prepared spells total.

features.name: Disciple of Life
features.description: Your healing spells are empowered by divine vitality. When a spell you cast with a spell slot restores Hit Points to a creature, that creature regains additional Hit Points on the turn you cast the spell. The additional Hit Points equal 2 plus the spell slot's level.

mechanics.trigger: Cast a spell with a spell slot that restores HP

features.name: Preserve Life
features.description: As a Magic action, you present your Holy Symbol and evoke healing energy that can restore a total number of Hit Points equal to five times your Cleric level. Choose any creatures within 30 feet of you (you can choose yourself), and divide those Hit Points among the chosen creatures. This feature can restore a creature to no more than half of its Hit Point maximum. You can use this feature only on a creature that has no more than half of its Hit Point maximum in current Hit Points. You can't use this feature on an Undead creature or a Construct.

mechanics.cap_per_creature: Cannot restore above half HP maximum
mechanics.eligibility: Target must be at or below half HP maximum

features.name: Blessed Healer
features.description: The healing spells you cast on others heal you as well. When you cast a spell with a spell slot that restores Hit Points to one or more creatures other than yourself, you regain Hit Points equal to 2 plus the spell slot's level.

mechanics.trigger: Cast a healing spell (with slot) that restores HP to others (not just yourself)

features.name: Supreme Healing
features.description: When you would normally roll one or more dice to restore Hit Points with a spell, you instead use the highest number possible for each die. For example, instead of restoring 2d6 Hit Points to a creature, you restore 12.

mechanics.effect: Use maximum value of each healing die instead of rolling

features.name: Subclass Feature — Holy Radiance
features.description: You gain the ability to channel holy energy to purify. When you use Turn Undead, the effect also functions as a Mass Cure Wounds spell cast at 3rd level targeting all allies within 30 feet, using your Wisdom as the spellcasting ability. You don't expend a spell slot for this effect.
features.note: This entry approximates the level 11 feature within the SRD framework.

mechanics.trigger: Use Turn Undead
mechanics.bonus: Mass Cure Wounds (3rd level) targeting all allies within 30 ft (no slot expended)

features.name: Subclass Feature — Divine Strike (Life)
features.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend a use of Channel Divinity to deal an extra 2d8 Radiant damage as part of the attack. The weapon is also treated as magical for the duration of combat.
features.note: This entry approximates the level 15 feature within the SRD framework.

mechanics.trigger: Hit a creature with a weapon attack (once per turn)

subclasses.name: Light Domain
subclasses.description: Gods of light — including Helm, Lathander, Pholtus, Branchala, the Silver Flame, Belenus, Apollo, and Re-Horakhty — promote the ideals of rebirth and renewal, truth, vigilance, and the pursuit of beauty, and the light domain clerics are those who follow them.
subclasses.domain_spells_note: Domain spells are always prepared and don't count against your prepared spells total.

features.name: Radiance of the Dawn
features.description: As a Magic action, you present your Holy Symbol, and any magical darkness within 30 feet of you is dispelled. Additionally, each Hostile creature within 30 feet of you must make a Constitution saving throw, taking Radiant damage equal to 2d10 plus your Cleric level on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful save. A creature that has Total Cover from you is not affected.

effect_2.targets: Hostile creatures within 30 ft (no Total Cover)

features.name: Warding Flare
features.description: When a creature that you can see within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll, you can use your Reaction to impose Disadvantage on the attack roll, causing light to flare before the attacker before it hits or misses. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.trigger: Creature within 30 ft makes an attack roll
mechanics.effect: Impose Disadvantage on the attack roll

features.name: Improved Warding Flare
features.description: You regain all expended uses of Warding Flare when you finish a Short or Long Rest. In addition, whenever you use Warding Flare, you can extend the benefit to a creature within 30 feet of you rather than imposing Disadvantage on the attacker directly.

mechanics.new_option: Extend Warding Flare benefit to a nearby ally within 30 ft instead

features.name: Corona of Light
features.description: As a Magic action, you cause yourself to emit an aura of sunlight that lasts for 1 minute or until you dismiss it using a Bonus Action. You emit Bright Light in a 60-foot radius and Dim Light 30 feet beyond that. Your enemies in the Bright Light have Disadvantage on saving throws against any spell that deals Fire or Radiant damage.

mechanics.light: Bright Light 60 ft, Dim Light additional 30 ft
mechanics.effect: Enemies in Bright Light have Disadvantage on saves vs Fire and Radiant spells

features.name: Subclass Feature — Searing Vengeance
features.description: When you or an ally within 30 feet of you is reduced to 0 Hit Points, you can use your Reaction to have that creature regain Hit Points equal to 2d8 plus your Wisdom modifier. If the creature was downed, it stands up. Each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you takes 2d8 + Wisdom modifier Radiant damage and is Blinded until the end of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, and you regain all uses on a Long Rest.

mechanics.trigger: You or ally within 30 ft reduced to 0 HP
mechanics.healing: 2d8 + Wisdom modifier (target regains HP and stands up if downed)
mechanics.area_damage: 2d8 + Wisdom modifier Radiant to chosen creatures within 30 ft
mechanics.area_effect: Blinded until end of your next turn

features.name: Subclass Feature — Blaze of Glory
features.description: As an action on your turn, you can expend a spell slot of 5th level or lower to cause a blazing column of divine fire to descend from the sky to a point within 60 feet that you can see. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius, 40-foot-high cylinder centered on that point must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 5d6 Fire damage plus 5d6 Radiant damage. A creature takes half as much damage on a successful save.

mechanics.cost: Spell slot of 5th level or lower

subclasses.name: Trickery Domain
subclasses.description: Gods of trickery — such as Tymora, Beshaba, Olidammara, the Traveler, Garl Glittergold, and Loki — are mischief-makers and instigators who stand as a constant challenge to the accepted order among both gods and mortals.
subclasses.domain_spells_note: Domain spells are always prepared and don't count against your prepared spells total.

features.name: Blessing of the Trickster
features.description: As a Magic action, you can choose yourself or a willing creature within 30 feet of you. You grant the chosen creature Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks. This blessing lasts for 1 hour or until you use this feature again.

mechanics.effect: Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks

features.name: Invoke Duplicity
features.description: As a Magic action, you create a perfect illusory duplicate of yourself that lasts for 1 minute (Channel Divinity). The illusion appears in an unoccupied space that you can see within 30 feet of you. As a Bonus Action, you can move the illusion up to 30 feet to a space you can see, but it must remain within 120 feet of you. For the duration, you can cast spells as though you were in the illusion's space, but you must use your own senses. In addition, when both you and your illusion are within 5 feet of a creature that can see the illusion, you have Advantage on attack rolls against that creature.

mechanics.move_illusion: Bonus Action: move up to 30 ft (must stay within 120 ft)
mechanics.spellcasting: Cast spells as if in illusion's space (using your own senses)
mechanics.combat: Advantage on attacks vs creatures within 5 ft of both you and your illusion

features.name: Trickster's Magic
features.description: When you cast an Enchantment or Illusion spell using a spell slot, you can add your Wisdom modifier to one damage roll of that spell. In addition, you can use your Invoke Duplicity to take the Hide action as a Bonus Action even when creatures can see you, as long as you are within 5 feet of your illusion.

mechanics.spell_bonus: Add Wisdom modifier to one damage roll of Enchantment or Illusion spells (cast with slot)
mechanics.hide_upgrade: Can take Hide as Bonus Action while within 5 ft of illusion, even if visible

features.name: Improved Duplicity
features.description: The power of your illusions grows. When you use Invoke Duplicity, you can create two duplicates of yourself instead of one. As a Bonus Action, you can move any number of these duplicates. In addition, each duplicate now lasts until you dismiss it or until you create a new set of duplicates.

mechanics.move: Bonus Action: move any number of duplicates
mechanics.duration_update: Until dismissed or new duplicates created

features.name: Subclass Feature — Opportunist
features.description: When a creature within 5 feet of you is hit by an attack made by a creature other than you, you can use your Reaction to make a melee attack against the attacker. On a hit you deal weapon damage plus your Wisdom modifier in Psychic damage.

mechanics.trigger: Creature within 5 ft is hit by an attack from another creature
mechanics.effect: Melee attack against the attacker; on hit: weapon damage + Wisdom modifier Psychic

features.name: Subclass Feature — Master of Deceit
features.description: Your deceptive mastery reaches its peak. You always have the Mislead spell prepared and can cast it without expending a spell slot once per Long Rest. In addition, while your Invoke Duplicity illusion is active, you are immune to the Charmed and Frightened conditions.

subclasses.name: War Domain
subclasses.description: War has many manifestations. It can make heroes of ordinary people. It can be desperate and horrific, pitting good against evil. Gods of war — including Tempus, the Silver Flame, Hextor, Maglubiyet, Nuada, the Red Knight, Selûne, and Wilder — watch over warriors and reward them for their great deeds.
subclasses.domain_spells_note: Domain spells are always prepared and don't count against your prepared spells total.

features.name: War Priest
features.description: As a Bonus Action immediately after you take the Attack action, you can make one additional weapon attack. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.effect: Make one additional weapon attack

features.name: Guided Strike
features.description: When you or a creature within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll and misses, you can use your Reaction to expend one use of Channel Divinity and grant a +10 bonus to the roll, potentially causing it to hit.

mechanics.trigger: You or ally within 30 ft misses an attack roll
mechanics.effect: +10 bonus to the missed attack roll (potentially turning to a hit)

features.name: War God's Blessing
features.description: You can use your Guided Strike to grant the +10 bonus to attack rolls made by any creature, not just yourself. When a creature within 30 feet of you (including yourself) misses with an attack roll, you can use your Reaction to expend a use of Channel Divinity and grant that creature a +10 bonus to the roll.

mechanics.effect: +10 bonus to their missed attack roll

features.name: Avatar of Battle
features.description: You gain Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage from nonmagical attacks.

features.name: Subclass Feature — Divine Strike (War)
features.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 damage of the same type dealt by the weapon. When you reach level 14, this increases to 2d8.

mechanics.trigger: Hit with a weapon attack (once per turn)

features.name: Subclass Feature — Holy Weapon
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can imbue one weapon you are holding with positive energy. For 1 hour, the weapon deals an extra 2d8 Radiant damage on each hit and is considered magical. You can dismiss this effect as a Bonus Action. If the weapon leaves your grasp, the effect ends.

mechanics.duration: 1 hour (or until dismissed or weapon leaves hand)

spellcasting.ritual_note: You can cast any Wizard spell in your spellbook as a ritual if it has the ritual tag, without having it prepared.

spellbook.note: Each time you gain a Wizard level, you can add two Wizard spells of your choice to your spellbook for free. Each of these spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. On your adventures, you might find other spells that you can add to your spellbook.
spellbook.copying_cost: 50 GP and 2 hours per spell level
spellbook.copying_note: When you find a Wizard spell of 1st level or higher, you can add it to your spellbook if it is of a spell level you can prepare and if you can spare the time to decipher and copy it.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: As a student of arcane magic, you have a spellbook containing spells that show the first glimmerings of your true power. You prepare the list of Wizard spells available to cast by choosing from your spellbook. When you do so, choose a number of Wizard spells equal to your Intelligence modifier plus your Wizard level (minimum of one spell). The spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. You can change your list of prepared spells when you finish a Long Rest. Preparing a new list of Wizard spells requires time spent studying your spellbook and memorizing the incantations and gestures you must make to cast the spell: at least 1 minute per spell level for each spell on your list.

mechanics.change_prepared: Long Rest (1 minute per spell level per spell)
mechanics.ritual_note: Can cast any Wizard spell in spellbook as ritual (no preparation needed)

features.name: Spellbook
features.description: The spells that you add to your spellbook as you gain levels reflect the arcane research you conduct on your own, as well as intellectual breakthroughs you have had about the nature of the multiverse. You might find other spells during your adventures. You could discover a spell recorded on a scroll in an evil cultist's chest, for example, or in a dusty tome in an ancient library. Copying a Spell into the Book — When you find a Wizard spell of 1st level or higher, you can add it to your spellbook if it is of a spell level you can prepare and if you can spare the time to decipher and copy it. Copying that spell into your spellbook involves reproducing the basic form of the spell, then deciphering the unique system of notation used by the wizard who wrote it. You must practice the spell until you understand the sounds or gestures required, then transcribe it into your spellbook using your own notation. For each level of the spell, the process takes 2 hours and costs 50 GP. The cost represents material components you expend as you experiment with the spell to master it, as well as the fine inks you need to record it. Once you have spent this time and money, you can prepare the spell just like your other spells.

mechanics.replacement_on_loss: You can use the Replace Spellbook downtime activity (8 hours) to replace a lost spellbook

features.name: Arcane Recovery
features.description: You have learned to regain some of your magical energy by studying your spellbook. When you finish a Short Rest, you can choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level that is equal to or less than half your Wizard level (rounded up), and none of the slots can be of 6th level or higher. For example, if you're a 4th-level Wizard, you can recover up to two levels worth of spell slots. You can recover either a 2nd-level spell slot or two 1st-level spell slots. You can't use this feature again until you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.trigger: Finish a Short Rest
mechanics.recovery_pool: Combined slot levels up to half Wizard level (rounded up)

features.name: Scholar
features.description: While studying magic, you also specialized in another field of study. Choose one of the following skills in which you have proficiency: Arcana, History, Investigation, Medicine, Nature, or Religion. You have Expertise in the chosen skill.

mechanics.benefit: Expertise in chosen skill (double proficiency bonus)

features.name: Wizard Subclass
features.description: You gain a Wizard subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Wizard levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Wizard level or lower.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Wizard levels 8, 12, 16, and 19.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Memorize Spell
features.description: Whenever you finish a Short Rest, you can study your spellbook and replace one of the spells you have prepared for your Spellcasting feature with another spell from your spellbook.

mechanics.trigger: Finish a Short Rest
mechanics.effect: Replace one prepared spell with another from your spellbook

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Wizard subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Wizard subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Wizard subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Spell Mastery
features.description: You have achieved such mastery over certain spells that you can cast them at will. Choose a 1st-level Wizard spell and a 2nd-level Wizard spell that are in your spellbook. You can cast those spells at their lowest level without expending a spell slot when you have them prepared. If you want to cast either spell at a higher level, you must expend a spell slot as normal. By spending 8 hours in study, you can exchange one or both of the spells you chose for different spells of the same levels.

mechanics.requirement: Must be in your spellbook and prepared
mechanics.benefit: Cast at lowest level without expending a spell slot
mechanics.higher_level: Must expend a spell slot as normal
mechanics.change: 8 hours of study to exchange choices

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain the Epic Boon of Spell Recall feat or another Epic Boon feat of your choice.

mechanics.type: Epic Boon feat (e.g. Epic Boon of Spell Recall)

features.name: Signature Spells
features.description: You gain mastery over two powerful spells and can cast them with little effort. Choose two 3rd-level Wizard spells in your spellbook as your signature spells. You always have these spells prepared, they don't count against the number of spells you have prepared, and you can cast each of them once at 3rd level without expending a spell slot. When you do so, you can't cast it this way again until you finish a Short Rest. If you want to cast a signature spell at a higher level, you must expend a spell slot as normal.

mechanics.free_cast: Once each per Short Rest at 3rd level (no spell slot)
mechanics.higher_level: Must expend a spell slot as normal

subclasses.name: Abjurer
subclasses.description: Your study of magic is focused on spells that block, banish, or protect — ending harmful effects, banishing evil influences, and protecting the weak. Called Abjurers, members of this school are sought when baleful spirits require exorcism, when important locations must be guarded against magical spying, and when portals to the Outer Planes must be closed.

features.name: Abjuration Savant
features.description: Choose two Abjuration spells from the Wizard spell list, each of which must be of a level you can prepare. Add them to your spellbook for free. In addition, whenever you gain a Wizard level, you can add one Abjuration spell from the Wizard spell list to your spellbook for free. The spell must be of a level you can prepare.

mechanics.free_spells_condition: Abjuration spells of a level you can prepare
mechanics.ongoing_condition: One Abjuration spell per Wizard level gained

features.name: Arcane Ward
features.description: You can weave magic around yourself for protection. When you cast an Abjuration spell with a spell slot, you can simultaneously use a strand of the spell's magic to create a magical ward on yourself that lasts until you finish a Long Rest. The ward has Hit Points equal to twice your Wizard level plus your Intelligence modifier. Whenever you take damage, the ward takes the damage instead. If this damage reduces the ward to 0 Hit Points, you take any remaining damage. While the ward has 0 Hit Points, it can't absorb damage, but its magic remains. Whenever you cast an Abjuration spell with a spell slot, the ward regains a number of Hit Points equal to twice the level of the spell slot. Once you create the ward, you can't create it again until you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.trigger: Cast an Abjuration spell with a spell slot
mechanics.damage_absorption: Ward takes damage before you do; excess damage hits you
mechanics.recharge: Cast Abjuration spell with slot: ward regains HP equal to 2 × slot level

features.name: Projected Ward
features.description: When a creature that you can see within 30 feet of you takes damage, you can use your Reaction to cause your Arcane Ward to absorb that damage. If this damage reduces the ward to 0 Hit Points, the warded creature takes any remaining damage.

mechanics.trigger: Creature within 30 ft you can see takes damage
mechanics.effect: Arcane Ward absorbs the damage instead; excess damage hits the target

features.name: Improved Abjuration
features.description: When you cast an Abjuration spell that requires you to make an ability check as part of casting that spell (as in counterspell and dispel magic), you add your Proficiency Bonus to that ability check.

mechanics.trigger: Cast Abjuration spell requiring an ability check (e.g., Counterspell, Dispel Magic)
mechanics.bonus: Add Proficiency Bonus to the ability check

features.name: Spell Resistance
features.description: You have Advantage on saving throws against spells. Furthermore, you have Resistance to the damage of spells.

mechanics.saving_throw_benefit: Advantage on saving throws against spells

subclasses.name: Diviner
subclasses.description: The counsel of a Diviner is sought by royalty and commoners alike, for all seek a clearer understanding of the past, present, and future. As a Diviner, you strive to part the veils of space, time, and consciousness so that you can see clearly. You work to master spells of discernment, remote viewing, supernatural knowledge, and foresight.

features.name: Divination Savant
features.description: Choose two Divination spells from the Wizard spell list, each of which must be of a level you can prepare. Add them to your spellbook for free. In addition, whenever you gain a Wizard level, you can add one Divination spell from the Wizard spell list to your spellbook for free. The spell must be of a level you can prepare.

mechanics.free_spells_condition: Divination spells of a level you can prepare
mechanics.ongoing_condition: One Divination spell per Wizard level gained

features.name: Portent
features.description: Glimpses of the future begin to press in on your awareness. When you finish a Long Rest, roll two d20s and record the numbers rolled. You can replace any attack roll, saving throw, or ability check made by you or a creature that you can see with one of these foretelling rolls. You must choose to do so before the roll, and you can replace a roll in this way only once per turn. Each foretelling roll can be used only once. When you finish a Long Rest, you lose any unused foretelling rolls.

mechanics.trigger: Finish a Long Rest
mechanics.usage: Replace any attack roll, saving throw, or ability check (yours or visible creature)
mechanics.frequency: Once per turn per foretelling die
mechanics.expiry: Unused dice lost on next Long Rest

features.name: Expert Divination
features.description: Casting Divination spells comes so easily to you that it expends only a fraction of your spellcasting efforts. When you cast a Divination spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you regain one expended spell slot. The slot you regain must be of a level lower than the spell you cast and can't be higher than 5th level.

mechanics.trigger: Cast a Divination spell using a slot of 2nd level or higher
mechanics.benefit: Regain one expended spell slot
recovered_slot_conditions: Lower level than the cast spell

features.name: The Third Eye
features.description: You can use your action to increase your powers of perception. When you do so, choose one of the following benefits, which lasts until you are Incapacitated or you take a Short or Long Rest: Darkvision — You gain Darkvision with a range of 60 feet. Ethereal Sight — You can see into the Ethereal Plane within 60 feet of you. Greater Comprehension — You can read any language. See Invisibility — You can see invisible creatures and objects within 10 feet of you that are within line of sight.

options.name: Ethereal Sight
options.effect: See into the Ethereal Plane within 60 feet

options.name: See Invisibility
options.effect: See invisible creatures and objects within 10 feet (line of sight)

features.name: Greater Portent
features.description: The visions in your dreams intensify and paint a more accurate picture in your mind of what is to come. You roll three d20s for your Portent feature, rather than two.

subclasses.name: Evoker
subclasses.description: You focus your study on magic that creates powerful elemental effects such as bitter cold, searing flame, rolling thunder, crackling lightning, and burning acid. Some Evokers find employment in military forces, serving as artillery to blast enemy armies from afar. Others use their spectacular power to protect the weak, while some seek their own gain as bandits, adventurers, or aspiring tyrants.

features.name: Evocation Savant
features.description: Choose two Evocation spells from the Wizard spell list, each of which must be of a level you can prepare. Add them to your spellbook for free. In addition, whenever you gain a Wizard level, you can add one Evocation spell from the Wizard spell list to your spellbook for free. The spell must be of a level you can prepare.

mechanics.free_spells_condition: Evocation spells of a level you can prepare
mechanics.ongoing_condition: One Evocation spell per Wizard level gained

features.name: Potent Cantrip
features.description: Your damaging cantrips affect even creatures that avoid the brunt of the effect. When a creature succeeds on a saving throw against one of your cantrips that deals damage, the creature takes half the cantrip's damage (if any) but suffers no other effects of the cantrip.

mechanics.trigger: Creature succeeds saving throw against your damaging cantrip
mechanics.effect: Creature takes half damage instead of none

features.name: Sculpt Spells
features.description: You can create pockets of relative safety within the effects of your Evocation spells. When you cast an Evocation spell that affects other creatures that you can see, you can choose a number of them equal to 1 plus the spell's level. The chosen creatures automatically succeed on their saving throws against the spell, and they take no damage if they would normally take half damage on a successful save.

mechanics.trigger: Cast an Evocation spell affecting visible creatures
mechanics.effect: Chosen creatures auto-succeed on saves; take no damage even on half-damage success

features.name: Empowered Evocation
features.description: Whenever you cast a Wizard spell that deals damage, you can add your Intelligence modifier to one damage roll of that spell.

mechanics.trigger: Cast any Wizard spell that deals damage
mechanics.bonus: Add Intelligence modifier to one damage roll

features.name: Overchannel
features.description: You can increase the power of your simpler spells. When you cast a Wizard spell with a spell slot of 1st through 5th level that deals damage, you can deal maximum damage with that spell. The first time you do so, you suffer no adverse effect. If you use this feature again before you finish a Long Rest, you take 2d12 Necrotic damage for each level of the spell slot immediately after you cast it. Each time you use this feature again before finishing a Long Rest, the Necrotic damage per spell level increases by 1d12. This damage ignores Resistance and Immunity.

mechanics.trigger: Cast a damage-dealing Wizard spell of 1st-5th level
mechanics.effect: Deal maximum damage with the spell
mechanics.subsequent_uses: 2d12 Necrotic damage per spell slot level (immediately after casting; increases by 1d12 per additional use before Long Rest)
mechanics.note: This damage ignores Resistance and Immunity

subclasses.name: Illusionist
subclasses.description: You focus your studies on magic that dazzles the senses, befuddles the mind, and tricks even the wisest folk. Your magic is subtle, but the illusions crafted by your keen mind make the impossible seem real. Some Illusionists — including many gnome Wizards — are benign tricksters who use their spells to entertain. Others are more sinister masters of deception, using their illusions to frighten and fool.

features.name: Illusion Savant
features.description: Choose two Illusion spells from the Wizard spell list, each of which must be of a level you can prepare. Add them to your spellbook for free. In addition, whenever you gain a Wizard level, you can add one Illusion spell from the Wizard spell list to your spellbook for free. The spell must be of a level you can prepare.

mechanics.free_spells_condition: Illusion spells of a level you can prepare
mechanics.ongoing_condition: One Illusion spell per Wizard level gained

features.name: Improved Minor Illusion
features.description: You know the Minor Illusion cantrip. If you already know this cantrip, you learn a different Wizard cantrip of your choice. The cantrip doesn't count against your number of cantrips known. When you cast Minor Illusion, you can create both a sound and an image with a single casting of the spell.

mechanics.cantrip_bonus: Know Minor Illusion (or different cantrip if already known); does not count against cantrips known
mechanics.upgrade: Single cast of Minor Illusion can create both a sound and an image simultaneously

features.name: Malleable Illusions
features.description: When you cast an Illusion spell that has a duration of 1 minute or longer, you can use your action to change the nature of that illusion (using the spell's normal parameters for the illusion), provided you can see the illusion.

mechanics.trigger: You have an active Illusion spell with duration 1 minute or longer
mechanics.effect: Change the nature of the illusion (within the spell's normal parameters)
mechanics.requirement: Must be able to see the illusion

features.name: Illusory Self
features.description: You can create an illusory duplicate of yourself as an instant, almost instinctual reaction to danger. When a creature makes an attack roll against you, you can use your Reaction to interpose the illusory duplicate between the attacker and yourself. The attack automatically misses you, then the illusion dissipates. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

mechanics.trigger: A creature makes an attack roll against you
mechanics.effect: Attack automatically misses; illusion dissipates after

features.name: Illusory Reality
features.description: You have learned the secret of weaving shadow magic into your illusions to give them a semi-reality. When you cast an Illusion spell of 1st level or higher, you can choose one inanimate, nonmagical object that is part of the illusion and make that object real. You can do this on your turn as a Bonus Action while the spell is ongoing. The object remains real for 1 minute. For example, you can create an illusion of a bridge over a chasm and then make it real enough to walk on. The object can't deal damage or otherwise directly harm anyone.

mechanics.trigger: Cast an Illusion spell of 1st level or higher
mechanics.effect: One inanimate, nonmagical object in the illusion becomes real for 1 minute
mechanics.restriction: Object cannot deal damage or directly harm anyone

notes: All Pact Magic slots are the same level.
All Pact Magic slots are regained on a Short or Long Rest.
Warlocks know a fixed number of spells; one known spell can be replaced each level up.

eldritch_invocations.description: Eldritch Invocations are special abilities fueled by your patron's power. You gain a number of invocations shown in the Invocations Known column of the Warlock Features table. You can replace one invocation with another each time you gain a Warlock level. Invocations with a prerequisite level must be taken at or after that level.

invocations_list.name: Agonizing Blast
invocations_list.description: When you cast Eldritch Blast, add your Charisma modifier to the damage it deals on a hit.

invocations_list.name: Armor of Shadows
invocations_list.description: You can cast Mage Armor on yourself at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.

invocations_list.name: Ascendant Step
invocations_list.description: You can cast Levitate on yourself at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.

invocations_list.name: Beast Speech
invocations_list.description: You can cast Speak with Animals at will, without expending a spell slot.

invocations_list.name: Beguiling Influence
invocations_list.description: You gain proficiency in the Deception and Persuasion skills. If you are already proficient in either skill, you gain Expertise in it.

invocations_list.name: Bewitching Whispers
invocations_list.description: You can cast Compulsion once using a Pact Magic spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

invocations_list.name: Bond of the Talisman
invocations_list.prerequisite: Level 12 Warlock, Pact of the Talisman boon
invocations_list.description: While someone else is wearing your talisman, you can use your action to teleport to the unoccupied space closest to them, provided the two of you are on the same plane of existence. The wearer can do the same thing. The teleportation can be used a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and all expended uses are restored when you finish a Long Rest.

invocations_list.name: Book of Ancient Secrets
invocations_list.description: You can now inscribe magical rituals in your Book of Shadows. Choose two 1st-level spells that have the ritual tag from any class spell list; they appear in the book and do not count against your spells known. You can cast them as rituals. You can also add other ritual spells to the book during your adventures (spell level up to half your Warlock level rounded up; costs 2 hours and 50 GP per spell level).

invocations_list.name: Chains of Carceri
invocations_list.prerequisite: Level 15 Warlock, Pact of the Chain boon
invocations_list.description: You can cast Hold Monster at will targeting a Celestial, Fiend, or Elemental, without expending a spell slot or material components. You must finish a Long Rest before you can use this invocation on the same creature again.

invocations_list.name: Devil's Sight
invocations_list.description: You can see normally in Darkness, both magical and nonmagical, to a distance of 120 feet.

invocations_list.name: Dreadful Word
invocations_list.description: You can cast Confusion once using a Pact Magic spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

invocations_list.name: Eldritch Mind
invocations_list.description: You have Advantage on Constitution saving throws that you make to maintain Concentration.

invocations_list.name: Eldritch Smite
invocations_list.prerequisite: Level 5 Warlock, Pact of the Blade boon
invocations_list.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with your pact weapon, you can expend a Pact Magic spell slot to deal an extra 1d8 Force damage to the target, plus another 1d8 per level of the spell slot, and you can knock the target Prone if it is Huge or smaller.

invocations_list.name: Eldritch Spear
invocations_list.description: When you cast Eldritch Blast, its range is 300 feet.

invocations_list.name: Eyes of the Rune Keeper
invocations_list.description: You can read all writing.

invocations_list.name: Fiendish Vigor
invocations_list.description: You can cast False Life on yourself at will as a 1st-level spell, without expending a spell slot or material components.

invocations_list.name: Gaze of Two Minds
invocations_list.description: You can use your action to touch a willing humanoid and perceive through its senses until the end of your next turn. As long as the creature is on the same plane of existence as you, you can use your action on subsequent turns to maintain this connection, extending the duration until the end of your next turn. While perceiving through the other creature's senses, you benefit from any special senses possessed by that creature, and you are Blinded and Deafened to your own surroundings.

invocations_list.name: Gift of the Depths
invocations_list.description: You can breathe underwater, and you gain a Swim Speed equal to your Speed. You can also cast Water Breathing once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to do so when you finish a Long Rest.

invocations_list.name: Gift of the Ever-Living Ones
invocations_list.description: Whenever you regain Hit Points while your familiar is within 100 feet of you, treat any dice rolled to determine the Hit Points you regain as having rolled their maximum value for you.

invocations_list.name: Grasp of Hadar
invocations_list.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with Eldritch Blast, you can move that creature in a straight line 10 feet closer to yourself.

invocations_list.name: Improved Pact Weapon
invocations_list.prerequisite: Level 5 Warlock, Pact of the Blade boon
invocations_list.description: You can use any weapon summoned with your Pact of the Blade feature as a spellcasting focus for your Warlock spells. In addition, the weapon gains a +1 bonus to its attack and damage rolls, unless it is a magic weapon that already has a bonus to those rolls. Finally, the weapon you conjure can be a Shortbow, Longbow, Light Crossbow, or Heavy Crossbow.

invocations_list.name: Investment of the Chain Master
invocations_list.description: When you cast Find Familiar, you infuse the summoned familiar with a measure of your eldritch power, granting it the following benefits: the familiar gains either a Fly Speed or Swim Speed (your choice) of 40 feet; as a Bonus Action, you can command the familiar to take the Attack action; the familiar's weapon attacks are considered magical for the purpose of overcoming immunity and resistance; if the familiar forces a creature to make a saving throw, it uses your spell save DC; and when the familiar takes damage, you can use your Reaction to grant it Resistance against that damage.

invocations_list.name: Lance of Lethargy
invocations_list.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with Eldritch Blast, you can reduce that creature's Speed by 10 feet until the end of your next turn.

invocations_list.name: Lessons of the First Ones
invocations_list.description: You have received knowledge from an elder entity of the multiverse, allowing you to gain one feat of your choice that has the Origin category.

invocations_list.name: Lifedrinker
invocations_list.prerequisite: Level 12 Warlock, Pact of the Blade boon
invocations_list.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with your pact weapon, you deal an extra 1d6 Necrotic damage to the target, and you regain Hit Points equal to the Necrotic damage dealt.

invocations_list.name: Mask of Many Faces
invocations_list.description: You can cast Disguise Self at will, without expending a spell slot.

invocations_list.name: Master of Myriad Forms
invocations_list.description: You can cast Alter Self at will, without expending a spell slot.

invocations_list.name: Minions of Chaos
invocations_list.description: You can cast Conjure Elemental once using a Pact Magic spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

invocations_list.name: Mire the Mind
invocations_list.description: You can cast Slow once using a Pact Magic spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

invocations_list.name: Misty Visions
invocations_list.description: You can cast Silent Image at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.

invocations_list.name: One with Shadows
invocations_list.description: When you are in an area of Dim Light or Darkness, you can use your action to become Invisible until you move, take an action, or take a Reaction.

invocations_list.name: Otherworldly Leap
invocations_list.description: You can cast Jump on yourself at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.

invocations_list.name: Repelling Blast
invocations_list.description: When you hit a creature with Eldritch Blast, you can push the creature up to 10 feet away from you in a straight line.

invocations_list.name: Sculptor of Flesh
invocations_list.description: You can cast Polymorph once using a Pact Magic spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

invocations_list.name: Shroud of Shadow
invocations_list.description: You can cast Invisibility at will, without expending a spell slot.

invocations_list.name: Sign of Ill Omen
invocations_list.description: You can cast Bestow Curse once using a Pact Magic spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

invocations_list.name: Thief of Five Fates
invocations_list.description: You can cast Bane once using a Pact Magic spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

invocations_list.name: Thirsting Blade
invocations_list.prerequisite: Level 5 Warlock, Pact of the Blade boon
invocations_list.description: When you take the Attack action, you can attack with your pact weapon twice instead of once.

invocations_list.name: Tomb of Levistus
invocations_list.description: As a Reaction when you take damage, you can entomb yourself in ice, which melts away at the end of your next turn. You gain 10 Temporary Hit Points per Warlock level, which take the triggering damage first. You also gain Vulnerability to Fire damage and your Speed is reduced to 0 until the ice melts. You can use this invocation a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

invocations_list.name: Trickster's Escape
invocations_list.description: You can cast Freedom of Movement on yourself once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to do so when you finish a Long Rest.

invocations_list.name: Undying Servitude
invocations_list.description: You can cast Animate Dead once without expending a spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

invocations_list.name: Visions of Distant Realms
invocations_list.description: You can cast Arcane Eye at will, without expending a spell slot.

invocations_list.name: Voice of the Chain Master
invocations_list.description: You can communicate telepathically with your familiar and perceive through your familiar's senses as long as you are on the same plane of existence. Additionally, while perceiving through your familiar's senses, you can also speak through your familiar in your own voice, even if your familiar is normally incapable of speech.

invocations_list.name: Whispers of the Grave
invocations_list.description: You can cast Speak with Dead at will, without expending a spell slot.

invocations_list.name: Witch Sight
invocations_list.description: You can see the true form of any Shapechanger or creature concealed by illusion or transmutation magic while the creature is within 30 feet of you and within line of sight.

features.name: Eldritch Invocations
features.description: You have unearthed Eldritch Invocations, fragments of forbidden knowledge that imbue you with an abiding magical ability or other lessons. You gain one invocation of your choice. Invocations are described in the Eldritch Invocations section. A level prerequisite in an invocation refers to your Warlock level. Whenever you gain a Warlock level, you can replace one invocation you know with another invocation for which you qualify.

features.name: Pact Magic
features.description: Through occult ceremony, you have formed a pact with a mysterious entity to gain magical powers. The entity is known as your patron. Your arcane research and the magic bestowed on you by your patron have given you facility with spells. You know two cantrips of your choice from the Warlock spell list. Whenever you gain a Warlock level, you can replace one of your cantrips from this feature with another cantrip from the Warlock spell list.

features.name: Magical Cunning
features.description: You can perform an esoteric rite for 1 minute. At the end of it, you regain expended Pact Magic spell slots, but no more than a number equal to half your maximum (round up). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.recovery: Regain up to half your maximum Pact Magic slots (rounded up)

features.name: Warlock Subclass
features.description: You gain a Warlock subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Warlock levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Warlock level or lower.

features.name: Pact Boon
features.description: Your otherworldly patron grants you a fanciful gift in acknowledgment of your loyal service. You gain one of the following Pact Boon options of your choice.

options.name: Pact of the Blade
options.description: As a Bonus Action, you can conjure a pact weapon in your hand — a Simple or Martial melee weapon of your choice with which you are proficient and that lacks the Two-Handed property. You are proficient with it, and you can use your Charisma modifier for its attack and damage rolls. The weapon disappears if you use this feature again, sheathe the weapon, or are Incapacitated. The weapon counts as magical for the purpose of overcoming Resistance and Immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

mechanics.weapon_type: Simple or Martial melee weapon (no Two-Handed property) of your choice
mechanics.ability_modifier: Charisma for attack and damage rolls

options.name: Pact of the Chain
options.description: You learn the Find Familiar spell and can cast it as a Magic action without expending a spell slot. You can cast it in this way a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. The spell doesn't count against your number of spells known. When you cast the spell, you choose one of the normal familiar forms for that spell or one of the following special forms: Imp, Pseudodragon, Quasit, Skeleton, Slaad Tadpole, Sphinx of Wonder, Sprite, or Venomous Snake.

options.name: Pact of the Tome
options.description: Your patron gives you a magical grimoire called a Book of Shadows. When you gain this feature, choose three cantrips from any class's spell list. While the book is on your person, you can cast those cantrips at will; they don't count against your number of cantrips known and are Warlock spells for you. If you lose the book, you can perform a 1-hour ceremony to receive a replacement from your patron. This ceremony can be performed during a Short or Long Rest, and it destroys the previous book.

mechanics.requirement: Book must be on your person to cast them

options.name: Pact of the Talisman
options.description: Your patron gives you an amulet, a talisman that can aid the wearer when the need is great. When the wearer fails an ability check, they can add a d4 to the roll, potentially turning the failure into a success. This benefit can be used a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and all expended uses are restored when you finish a Long Rest. You can give the talisman to others to use. If the talisman is lost, you can perform a 1-hour ceremony to receive a replacement from your patron. This ceremony can be performed during a Short or Long Rest, and it destroys the previous talisman.

mechanics.trigger: Wearer fails an ability check
mechanics.effect: Add a d4 to the roll (potentially turning failure to success)

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Warlock levels 8, 12, 16, and 19.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Contact Patron
features.description: In the past, you have had to dream or use occult means to contact your otherworldly patron. Now you can call out to your patron more easily. As a Magic action, you can expend one Pact Magic spell slot to contact your patron. The patron appears to you as a vision or voice for up to 1 minute, and you can ask it one question per Pact Magic slot expended. The patron answers each question with one or two sentences. The DM plays the role of the patron.

mechanics.cost: One Pact Magic spell slot per question
mechanics.effect: Patron appears (vision or voice) for up to 1 minute; one question per slot expended
mechanics.answer: One or two sentences per question (DM roleplays)

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Warlock subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Warlock subclass.

features.name: Mystic Arcanum
features.description: Your patron grants you a magical secret called an arcanum. Choose one 6th-level spell from the Warlock spell list as this arcanum. You can cast your arcanum spell once without expending a spell slot, and you must finish a Long Rest before you can do so again. At higher Warlock levels, you gain more Warlock spells of your choice that can be cast in this way: one 7th-level spell at level 13, one 8th-level spell at level 15, and one 9th-level spell at level 17. You regain all uses of your Mystic Arcanum when you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.note: Arcanum spells do not expend Pact Magic slots

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Mystic Arcanum (7th level)
features.description: You gain a Mystic Arcanum spell of 7th level from the Warlock spell list.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Warlock subclass.

features.name: Mystic Arcanum (8th level)
features.description: You gain a Mystic Arcanum spell of 8th level from the Warlock spell list.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Mystic Arcanum (9th level)
features.description: You gain a Mystic Arcanum spell of 9th level from the Warlock spell list.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain the Epic Boon of Fate feat or another Epic Boon feat of your choice.

mechanics.type: Epic Boon feat (e.g. Epic Boon of Fate)

features.name: Eldritch Master
features.description: When you use your Magical Cunning feature, you regain all your expended Pact Magic spell slots instead of only half.

mechanics.new_effect: Regain ALL expended Pact Magic slots (instead of half)

subclasses.name: Archfey Patron
subclasses.description: You have made a pact with a lord or lady of the Feywild. Such beings include the Prince of Frost; the Queen of Air and Darkness, ruler of the Gloaming Court; Titania of the Summer Court; Hyrsam, the Prince of Fools; and ancient hags. Your patron's motivations are often inscrutable, and sometimes whimsical, and might involve a striving for more power, collecting mortal experiences, or seeking entertainment.

expanded_spell_list.note: These spells are always available to you and do not count against your spells known.

features.name: Steps of the Fey
features.description: Your patron grants you the ability to move between the boundaries of the mortal and fey realms. You can cast Misty Step without expending a spell slot a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. In addition, whenever you cast that spell, you can choose one of the following additional effects: Refreshing Step — Immediately after you teleport, one creature you can see within 10 feet of you gains 1d10 Temporary Hit Points.
