Taunting Step — Creatures within 5 feet of the space you left must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or have Disadvantage on attack rolls against creatures other than you until the start of your next turn.

additional_effect_options.name: Refreshing Step
additional_effect_options.effect: One creature within 10 ft gains 1d10 Temporary HP after you teleport

additional_effect_options.name: Taunting Step
additional_effect_options.effect: Creatures within 5 ft of your origin space make Wisdom save (DC = spell save DC) or have Disadvantage on attacks against creatures other than you until start of your next turn

features.name: Misty Escape
features.description: You can cast Misty Step as a Reaction in response to taking damage. In addition, the following effects are now among the options for your Steps of the Fey: Dreadful Step — Creatures within 5 feet of the space you left or the space you appear in (your choice) must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or be Frightened of you until the end of your next turn. Disappearing Step — You have the Invisible condition until the start of your next turn or until immediately after you make an attack roll or cast a spell.

mechanics.reaction_cast: Cast Misty Step as a Reaction when you take damage

new_step_options.name: Dreadful Step
new_step_options.effect: Creatures within 5 ft of origin or destination (your choice) make Wisdom save or are Frightened until end of your next turn

new_step_options.name: Disappearing Step
new_step_options.effect: Become Invisible until start of your next turn or until you attack or cast a spell

features.name: Beguiling Defenses
features.description: Your patron teaches you how to guard your mind and how to use your otherworldly allure against your enemies. You are immune to the Charmed condition. In addition, immediately after a creature rolls the die for an attack roll against you, you can use your Reaction to impose your Charisma modifier (minimum of +1) as a penalty on that roll. If the attack misses, you can teleport the creature to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of yourself.

reaction.trigger: Creature rolls attack roll against you
reaction.effect: Impose -Charisma modifier (min -1) penalty on the roll
reaction.on_miss: Optional teleport: move the attacker to unoccupied space within 30 ft

features.name: Bewitching Magic
features.description: Your patron grants you the ability to weave fey magic into your spells. Immediately after you cast an Enchantment or Illusion spell using a Pact Magic spell slot, you can cast Misty Step as part of the same action without expending a spell slot.

mechanics.trigger: Cast an Enchantment or Illusion spell using a Pact Magic slot
mechanics.bonus: Cast Misty Step as part of the same action (no slot expended)

subclasses.name: Fiend Patron
subclasses.description: You have made a pact with a fiend whose power derives from the Lower Planes of existence. Such patrons desire the corruption or destruction of all things. You might have made a bargain to ensure your own survival, to exact revenge on an enemy, or simply out of a desire for power. Whatever the reason, the forces of the Lower Planes now have a direct conduit through you into the world.

expanded_spell_list.note: These spells are always available to you and do not count against your spells known.

features.name: Dark One's Blessing
features.description: When you reduce an enemy to 0 Hit Points, you gain Temporary Hit Points equal to your Charisma modifier plus your Warlock level (minimum of 1 Temporary Hit Point).

mechanics.trigger: Reduce an enemy to 0 HP

features.name: Dark One's Own Luck
features.description: You can call on your patron to alter fate in your favor. When you make an ability check or a saving throw, you can use this feature to add a d10 to your roll. You can do so after seeing the initial roll but before any of the effects of the roll occur. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

mechanics.trigger: Make an ability check or saving throw
mechanics.timing: After seeing the initial roll, before effects occur
mechanics.effect: Add 1d10 to the roll

features.name: Fiendish Resilience
features.description: Choose one damage type when you finish a Short or Long Rest. You gain Resistance to that damage type until you choose a different one with this feature. Damage from magical weapons or silver weapons ignores this Resistance.

mechanics.choose_on: Finish a Short or Long Rest
mechanics.effect: Resistance to the chosen damage type
mechanics.exceptions: Magical weapons and silver weapons bypass this Resistance

features.name: Hurl Through Hell
features.description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can try to temporarily banish it to a hellish realm. The creature must succeed on a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC or be Banished until the end of your next turn. When the creature returns, if it is not a Fiend, it takes 8d10 Psychic damage as it reels from its horrific experience.

mechanics.trigger: Hit a creature with an attack roll
mechanics.failure: Banished until end of your next turn
mechanics.on_return: 8d10 Psychic damage (only if creature is not a Fiend)

features.name: Devilish Deal
features.description: You can make a pact with a lesser fiend to empower your magic. As a Bonus Action, you summon a Devil of CR 3 or lower. The fiend is friendly to you and your companions, acts on its own initiative, and obeys your verbal commands. It remains for 1 minute or until it is reduced to 0 HP. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once), and regain all uses on a Long Rest.

mechanics.summons: Devil of CR 3 or lower (friendly, obeys verbal commands)
mechanics.duration: 1 minute or until reduced to 0 HP

subclasses.name: Great Old One Patron
subclasses.description: Your patron is a mysterious entity whose nature is utterly foreign to the fabric of reality. It might come from the Far Realm, the space beyond reality, or it could be one of the elder gods known only in legends. Its motives are incomprehensible to mortals, and its knowledge so immense and ancient that even the greatest libraries pale in comparison to the vast secrets it holds.

expanded_spell_list.note: These spells are always available to you and do not count against your spells known.

features.name: Awakened Mind
features.description: You can communicate telepathically with any creature you can see within 30 feet of you. You don't need to share a language with the creature, but the creature must be able to use at least one language. Communication is two-way once contact is established.

mechanics.requirement: Creature must be visible and able to use at least one language

features.name: Psychic Spells
features.description: When you cast a Warlock spell that deals damage, you can change its damage type to Psychic. In addition, when you cast a Warlock spell that requires a saving throw, you can give one target of the spell Disadvantage on the first saving throw it makes against the spell.

mechanics.option_1: Change damage type of a Warlock spell to Psychic
mechanics.option_2: Give one target Disadvantage on their first save against the spell

features.name: Clairvoyant Combatant
features.description: When you forge a telepathic bond with a creature using your Awakened Mind, you can force the creature to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature has Disadvantage on attack rolls against you, and you have Advantage on attack rolls against it, until your Concentration ends or until you use this feature on a different creature. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

mechanics.trigger: Forge telepathic bond using Awakened Mind

failure.creature: Disadvantage on attack rolls against you
failure.you: Advantage on attack rolls against the creature

mechanics.duration: Until Concentration ends or used on different creature

features.name: Eldritch Hex
features.description: Your alien patron grants you a powerful curse. You always have the Hex spell prepared. When you cast Hex and choose an ability, the target also has Disadvantage on saving throws using the chosen ability.

mechanics.upgrade: Target also has Disadvantage on saving throws using the hexed ability

features.name: Create Thrall
features.description: You gain the ability to infect a creature's mind with the alien magic of your patron. As a Magic action, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The target must make a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC or become Charmed by you until you cast this spell again or until you die. The Charmed creature obeys your commands as best it can. Whenever the Charmed creature takes damage, it repeats the saving throw. On a success, the effect ends. You can have only one creature Charmed in this way at a time.

mechanics.duration: Until you cast again, you die, or the creature succeeds on a repeated save (triggered by taking damage)

class.name: Monk
weapon_proficiencies: Martial weapons that have the Light property
tool_proficiencies: Choose one type of Artisan's Tools or Musical Instrument
multiclass_proficiencies_gained: Martial weapons that have the Light property

martial_arts.description: Your practice of Martial Arts gives you mastery of combat styles that use your Unarmed Strike and Monk weapons, which are Simple Melee weapons and Martial Melee weapons that have the Light property. You gain the following benefits while you are unarmed or wielding only Monk weapons and you aren't wearing armor or wielding a Shield.

focus_points.description: Your training allows you to harness the mystic energy of Focus, represented by Focus Points. The Monk Features table shows how many Focus Points you have at each level. You can spend these points to fuel various Focus features. You start knowing three Focus features: Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind. You learn more Focus features as you gain levels in this class. When you spend a Focus Point, it is unavailable until you finish a Short or Long Rest, at the end of which you regain all expended Focus Points.

features.name: Martial Arts
features.description: Your practice of Martial Arts gives you mastery of combat styles that use your Unarmed Strike and Monk weapons (Simple Melee weapons and Martial Melee weapons with the Light property). You gain the following benefits while unarmed or wielding only Monk weapons and not wearing armor or wielding a Shield: Bonus Unarmed Strike — You can make one Unarmed Strike as a Bonus Action. Dexterous Attacks — You can use Dexterity instead of Strength for the attack and damage rolls of your Unarmed Strikes and Monk weapons. When you use Dexterity in this way, you use your Martial Arts die for the damage die of such attacks. Martial Arts Die — You can roll a d6 in place of the normal damage of your Unarmed Strike or Monk weapons. This die changes as you gain Monk levels (d8 at level 5, d10 at level 11, d12 at level 17).

requirements: Unarmed or wielding only Monk weapons
mechanics.bonus_unarmed_strike: One Unarmed Strike as a Bonus Action
mechanics.dexterous_attacks: Use Dexterity for attack and damage rolls of Unarmed Strikes and Monk weapons

features.name: Unarmored Defense
features.description: While you are not wearing any armor and not wielding a Shield, your Armor Class equals 10 plus your Dexterity modifier plus your Wisdom modifier.

features.name: Monk's Focus
features.description: Your focus and martial training allow you to harness a well of extraordinary energy within yourself. This energy is represented by Focus Points. You have 2 Focus Points, and you regain all expended Focus Points when you finish a Short or Long Rest. You can spend these points to fuel the following Focus features.

features.name: Flurry of Blows
features.description: You can spend 1 Focus Point to make two Unarmed Strikes as a Bonus Action.

mechanics.effect: Make two Unarmed Strikes

features.name: Patient Defense
features.description: You can take the Dodge action as a Bonus Action. When you do so, you can spend 1 Focus Point to also take the Disengage action as part of that Bonus Action.

features.name: Step of the Wind
features.description: You can take the Disengage action as a Bonus Action. When you do so, you can spend 1 Focus Point to also take the Dash action as part of that Bonus Action, and your jump distance is doubled for the turn.

mechanics.optional_effect: Also take the Dash action; jump distance doubled for the turn

features.name: Unarmored Movement
features.description: Your speed increases by 10 feet while you are not wearing armor or wielding a Shield. This bonus increases when you reach certain Monk levels.

features.name: Uncanny Metabolism
features.description: When you roll Initiative, you can regain all expended Focus Points. When you do so, roll your Martial Arts die, and regain a number of Hit Points equal to your Monk level plus the number rolled. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.trigger: Roll Initiative
mechanics.effect_2: Roll Martial Arts die; regain HP equal to Monk level + roll

features.name: Deflect Attacks
features.description: When an attack roll hits you and its damage includes Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage, you can take a Reaction to reduce that damage. When you do so, the total damage is reduced by 1d10 plus your Dexterity modifier plus your Monk level. If you reduce the damage to 0, you can spend 1 Focus Point to redirect it: make a Ranged attack roll with a range of 20/60 feet using your Unarmed Strike bonus. On a hit, the target takes the damage you reduced to 0 plus 1d10 of the same type.

mechanics.trigger: Attack roll hits you dealing Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage

redirect_on_zero.effect: Ranged attack (20/60 ft) using Unarmed Strike bonus; on hit deals reduced damage + 1d10 (same type)

features.name: Monk Subclass
features.description: You gain a Monk subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Monk levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Monk level or lower.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Monk levels 8, 12, 16, and 19.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Slow Fall
features.description: You can take a Reaction when you fall to reduce any Falling damage you take by an amount equal to five times your Monk level.

mechanics.trigger: You fall

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Stunning Strike
features.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with a Monk weapon or an Unarmed Strike, you can spend 1 Focus Point to attempt a stunning strike. The target must make a Constitution saving throw (DC equal to 8 plus your Wisdom modifier and Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the target has the Stunned condition until the start of your next turn.

mechanics.trigger: Hit a creature with a Monk weapon or Unarmed Strike
mechanics.saving_throw: Constitution DC = 8 + WIS modifier + proficiency bonus
mechanics.failure: Stunned until start of your next turn

features.name: Martial Arts Die upgrade (d8)
features.description: Your Martial Arts die becomes a d8.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Monk subclass.

features.name: Empowered Strikes
features.description: Whenever you deal damage with your Unarmed Strike, it can deal your choice of Force damage or its normal damage type.

damage_type_choice: Normal damage type of the Unarmed Strike

features.name: Unarmored Movement upgrade (+15 ft)
features.description: Your Unarmored Movement bonus increases to +15 feet.

features.name: Evasion
features.description: When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and only half damage if you fail. You can't use this feature if you have the Incapacitated condition.

mechanics.trigger: Subjected to effect requiring Dexterity save for half damage

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Acrobatic Movement
features.description: While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a Shield, you gain the ability to move along vertical surfaces and across liquids on your turn without falling during the movement.

benefits: Move along vertical surfaces without falling (during movement)
Move across liquids without falling (during movement)

features.name: Heightened Focus
features.description: Your Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind gain the following additional benefits. Flurry of Blows — One of the Unarmed Strikes you make with this feature can impose a special condition on the target if it fails a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + WIS modifier + proficiency bonus): Dazed, Prone, or Push 15 feet (your choice each time). Patient Defense — You gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die when you use this feature. Step of the Wind — A creature within 5 feet of you that is Medium or smaller must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + WIS modifier + proficiency bonus) or be moved to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you when you use this feature (no action required).

flurry_of_blows_upgrade.saving_throw: Constitution DC = 8 + WIS modifier + proficiency bonus

step_of_the_wind_upgrade.saving_throw: Dexterity DC = 8 + WIS modifier + proficiency bonus
step_of_the_wind_upgrade.target: Medium or smaller creature within 5 feet
step_of_the_wind_upgrade.failure: Moved to unoccupied space within 5 feet of you

features.name: Unarmored Movement upgrade (climb and swim)
features.description: You gain a Climb Speed and a Swim Speed equal to your Speed.

features.name: Self-Restoration
features.description: Through sheer force of will, you can remove one of the following conditions from yourself at the end of each of your turns: Charmed, Frightened, or Poisoned. In addition, forgoing food and drink doesn't give you levels of Exhaustion.

mechanics.trigger: End of each of your turns
mechanics.bonus: Forgoing food and drink does not cause Exhaustion levels

features.name: Unarmored Movement upgrade (+20 ft)
features.description: Your Unarmored Movement bonus increases to +20 feet.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Monk subclass.

features.name: Martial Arts Die upgrade (d10)
features.description: Your Martial Arts die becomes a d10.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Deflect Energy
features.description: You can now use your Deflect Attacks feature against attacks that deal any damage type, not just Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing.

mechanics.new_damage_types: All damage types (not just Bludgeoning, Piercing, Slashing)

features.name: Disciplined Survivor
features.description: Your physical and mental discipline make you extraordinarily resilient. You gain proficiency in all saving throws. Additionally, whenever you make a saving throw and fail, you can spend 1 Focus Point to reroll it and take the second result.

reroll.effect: Reroll and use the new result

features.name: Unarmored Movement upgrade (+25 ft)
features.description: Your Unarmored Movement bonus increases to +25 feet.

features.name: Perfect Focus
features.description: When you roll Initiative and have no Focus Points remaining, you regain Focus Points equal to your Proficiency Bonus.

mechanics.trigger: Roll Initiative with 0 Focus Points
mechanics.effect: Regain Focus Points equal to Proficiency Bonus

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Monk subclass.

features.name: Martial Arts Die upgrade (d12)
features.description: Your Martial Arts die becomes a d12.

features.name: Superior Defense
features.description: At the start of your turn, you can spend 3 Focus Points to perfectly bolster yourself against harm for 1 minute or until you are Incapacitated. During that time, you have Resistance to all damage except Force damage.

mechanics.effect: Resistance to all damage types except Force

features.name: Unarmored Movement upgrade (+30 ft)
features.description: Your Unarmored Movement bonus increases to +30 feet.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain the Epic Boon of Irresistible Offense feat or another Epic Boon feat of your choice.

mechanics.type: Epic Boon feat (e.g. Epic Boon of Irresistible Offense)

features.name: Body and Mind
features.description: You have fully integrated your body and mind. Your Dexterity and Wisdom scores increase by 4. Your maximum for those scores is now 25.

subclasses.name: Warrior of Mercy
subclasses.description: Warriors of Mercy manipulate the life force of others. These Monks are wandering physicians who see a parity between the forces of life and death — choosing to harness both in service of their calling.

features.name: Hand of Harm
features.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike and spend a Focus Point for the hit, you can also deal Necrotic damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die plus your Wisdom modifier.

mechanics.trigger: Hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike and spend a Focus Point

features.name: Hand of Healing
features.description: As a Magic action, you can spend 1 Focus Point to touch a creature and restore a number of Hit Points equal to a roll of your Martial Arts die plus your Wisdom modifier. When you use Flurry of Blows, you can replace one of the Unarmed Strikes with a use of this feature, without spending a Focus Point for the healing.

mechanics.flurry_integration: Can replace one Flurry of Blows strike with Hand of Healing (no extra Focus Point cost)

features.name: Implements of Mercy
features.description: You gain proficiency in the Insight and Medicine skills and with the Herbalism Kit. You also gain a special mask that you can don or doff as a Bonus Action. While wearing the mask, you are immune to the Poisoned condition, have Darkvision out to 60 feet (or your existing Darkvision range increases by 60 feet if already present), and are not subject to the negative effects of the Poisoned condition.

mask_benefits: Immunity to Poisoned condition while worn
Darkvision 60 ft (or +60 ft if already present)

features.name: Physician's Touch
features.description: Your Hand of Healing and Hand of Harm improve. Hand of Healing — When you use Hand of Healing, you can also end one of the following conditions affecting the creature: Blinded, Deafened, Paralyzed, Poisoned, or Stunned. Hand of Harm — When you use Hand of Harm, you can also subject the creature to the Poisoned condition until the end of your next turn.

hand_of_healing_upgrade.bonus: End one condition: Blinded, Deafened, Paralyzed, Poisoned, or Stunned

hand_of_harm_upgrade.bonus: Subject target to Poisoned condition until end of your next turn

features.name: Flurry of Healing and Harm
features.description: When you use Flurry of Blows, you can replace any number of the Unarmed Strikes with a use of your Hand of Healing, without spending Focus Points for the healing. In addition, when you make an Unarmed Strike with Flurry of Blows, you can use Hand of Harm with that strike without spending a Focus Point for the Hand of Harm. Each creature can be affected by one Hand of Harm per Flurry of Blows.

mechanics.healing_upgrade: Replace any number of Flurry strikes with Hand of Healing (no Focus Point cost)
mechanics.harm_upgrade: Use Hand of Harm with a Flurry strike without spending a Focus Point (once per creature per Flurry)

features.name: Hand of Ultimate Mercy
features.description: Your mastery of life energy opens the door to the ultimate mercy. As a Magic action, you can touch the corpse of a creature that died within the past 24 hours and expend 5 Focus Points. The creature then returns to life, regaining a number of Hit Points equal to 4d10 plus your Wisdom modifier. If the creature died while subject to any of the following conditions, it revives with them removed: Blinded, Deafened, Paralyzed, Poisoned, and Stunned.

mechanics.target: Corpse of creature that died within the past 24 hours
mechanics.effect: Creature returns to life with 4d10 + Wisdom modifier HP

subclasses.name: Warrior of Shadow
subclasses.description: Warriors of Shadow practice stealth and subterfuge, channeling their focus into a marvel of mystic shadow arts. Originally they trained in secret temples as assassins, and some still serve as assassins and spies. Others apply their gifts to fight against injustice or evil.

features.name: Shadow Arts
features.description: You have learned to draw on the power of shadow to bolster your Monk abilities and cast certain spells. Darkness — You can spend 1 Focus Point to cast Darkness without a spell slot. You can see within the Darkness created by this casting, unlike normal Darkness. Darkvision — You gain Darkvision out to a range of 60 feet. If you already have Darkvision, its range increases by 60 feet. Pass Without Trace — You can spend 2 Focus Points to cast Pass Without Trace without a spell slot. Silence — You can spend 2 Focus Points to cast Silence without a spell slot. You can concentrate on both Silence and another spell simultaneously.

spells.name: Darkness
spells.note: You can see within your own Darkness

spells.name: Silence
spells.note: Can concentrate on Silence and another spell simultaneously

features.name: Shadow Step
features.description: While in Dim Light or Darkness, you can use a Bonus Action to teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see that is also in Dim Light or Darkness. You then have Advantage on the first melee attack you make before the end of the turn.

mechanics.requirement: Must be in Dim Light or Darkness
mechanics.destination_requirement: Destination must also be Dim Light or Darkness
mechanics.bonus: Advantage on first melee attack before end of turn

features.name: Improved Shadow Step
features.description: You can now use your Shadow Step feature while in any lighting condition, not just Dim Light or Darkness. In addition, you can spend 1 Focus Point to remove the requirement that the destination space must be in Dim Light or Darkness.

destination_upgrade.effect: Destination no longer needs to be in Dim Light or Darkness

features.name: Cloak of Shadows
features.description: As a Magic action while in Dim Light or Darkness, you can spend 3 Focus Points to shroud yourself in magical darkness for 8 hours. While in this shroud, you have the Invisible condition and shed no light. The shroud ends early if you spend Focus Points or if you end your turn in Bright Light.

mechanics.requirement: Must be in Dim Light or Darkness
mechanics.effect: Invisible condition, shed no light
ends_early_if: End your turn in Bright Light

subclasses.name: Warrior of the Elements
subclasses.description: Warriors of the Elements tap into the power of the Elemental Planes. Some of these Monks travel to one such plane to train with the denizens there, while others manipulate elemental energy closer to home.

features.name: Manipulate Elements
features.description: You know the Elementalism cantrip. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.

features.name: Elemental Attunement
features.description: At the start of your turn, you can spend 1 Focus Point to imbue yourself with elemental energy. The energy lasts for 10 minutes or until you are Incapacitated. You gain the following benefits for the duration: Reach — Your Unarmed Strikes have a reach of 10 feet, as elemental energy extends from you. Elemental Strikes — Once per turn when you hit with your Unarmed Strike, you can cause it to deal your choice of Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage instead of its normal damage type, and you can cause the following effect: Push 10 feet (same direction), or impose a condition (Blinded, Deafened, or Prone) on a failed Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + WIS modifier + proficiency bonus).

elemental_strikes.frequency: Once per turn on Unarmed Strike hit
effects: On failed Constitution save (DC = 8 + WIS modifier + PB): Blinded, Deafened, or Prone (your choice)

features.name: Environmental Burst
features.description: As a Magic action, you can spend 2 Focus Points to cause elemental energy to burst from you. Choose a damage type: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. Each other creature within 20 feet of you must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + WIS modifier + proficiency bonus). On a failed save, a creature takes 2d10 damage of the chosen type. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage.

mechanics.saving_throw: Dexterity DC = 8 + WIS modifier + proficiency bonus

features.name: Stride of the Elements
features.description: When you use your Step of the Wind, you gain a Fly Speed and a Swim Speed equal to your Speed for 10 minutes.

mechanics.trigger: Use Step of the Wind
mechanics.effect: Fly Speed and Swim Speed equal to your Speed

features.name: Elemental Epitome
features.description: When you use your Elemental Attunement, you also gain the following benefits for the duration: Damage Resistance — You gain Resistance to one of the following damage types of your choice: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. Destructive Stride — When you use Step of the Wind, you can cause elemental damage to each creature of your choice within 5 feet of the spaces you move through. The damage equals one Martial Arts die roll of the type chosen for your Damage Resistance. A creature can be damaged this way only once per turn.

mechanics.resistance: One of: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder (chosen on attunement)

destructive_stride.damage: One Martial Arts die (same type as chosen Resistance)
destructive_stride.targets: Chosen creatures within 5 ft of spaces moved through
destructive_stride.limit: Each creature can be damaged only once per turn

subclasses.name: Warrior of the Open Hand
subclasses.description: Warriors of the Open Hand are masters of unarmed combat. They learn techniques that push and trip their opponents, and they manipulate their own life energy to nourish and heal themselves.

features.name: Open Hand Technique
features.description: Whenever you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike that uses Flurry of Blows, you can impose one of the following effects on the target: Addle — The target can't make Opportunity Attacks until the start of its next turn. Push — The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC = 8 + WIS modifier + proficiency bonus) or be pushed up to 15 feet away from you. Topple — The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + WIS modifier + proficiency bonus) or have the Prone condition.

mechanics.trigger: Hit a creature with a Flurry of Blows Unarmed Strike

choose_one_effect.name: Addle
choose_one_effect.effect: Target can't make Opportunity Attacks until start of its next turn

features.name: Wholeness of Body
features.description: You gain the ability to heal yourself. As a Bonus Action, you can roll your Martial Arts die. You regain a number of Hit Points equal to the number rolled plus your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1 Hit Point). You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.healing: Martial Arts die + Wisdom modifier (minimum 1 HP)

features.name: Fleet Step
features.description: When you take a Bonus Action other than Step of the Wind, you can also use Step of the Wind as part of that Bonus Action.

mechanics.trigger: Take any Bonus Action other than Step of the Wind
mechanics.effect: Also use Step of the Wind as part of the same Bonus Action

features.name: Quivering Palm
features.description: You gain the ability to set up lethal vibrations in someone's body. When you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike, you can spend 4 Focus Points to start these imperceptible vibrations, which last for a number of days equal to your Monk level. The vibrations are harmless unless you use your action to end them. To do so, you and the target must be on the same plane of existence. When you use this action, the creature must make a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + WIS modifier + proficiency bonus). On a failed save, the creature is reduced to 0 Hit Points. On a successful save, the creature takes 10d10 Necrotic damage. You can have only one creature under the effect of this feature at a time. You can end the vibrations harmlessly without using an action.

mechanics.trigger: Hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike
mechanics.activation_to_trigger: Action (you and target must be on same plane)
mechanics.saving_throw: Constitution DC = 8 + WIS modifier + proficiency bonus
mechanics.safe_dismissal: Can end vibrations harmlessly (no action required)

notes: Sorcerers know a fixed number of spells and can replace one known spell each time they gain a Sorcerer level.
Sorcerers use Charisma as their spellcasting ability.
Sorcerers can spend Sorcery Points to create additional spell slots or to fuel Metamagic options.

sorcery_points.description: You have an internal wellspring of magic called Sorcery Points. You can spend these points to modify your spells or to create additional spell slots. You regain all spent Sorcery Points when you finish a Long Rest.

converting_slots_to_points.description: As a Bonus Action, you can expend a spell slot and gain a number of Sorcery Points equal to the slot's level.

creating_slots_from_points.description: As a Bonus Action, you can transform unexpended Sorcery Points into one spell slot. The Creating Spell Slots table shows the cost of creating a spell slot of a given level. You can create spell slots no higher than 5th level. Any spell slot you create with this feature vanishes when you finish a Long Rest.

metamagic.description: You gain the ability to twist your spells to suit your needs. You gain two Metamagic options of your choice from the list below. You gain another one at Sorcerer level 10 and one more at level 17. You can use only one Metamagic option on a spell when you cast it, unless the option says otherwise.

options.name: Careful Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that forces other creatures to make a saving throw, you can protect some of those creatures from the spell's full force. To do so, you spend 1 Sorcery Point and choose a number of those creatures up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). A chosen creature automatically succeeds on its saving throw against the spell.

options.name: Distant Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that has a range of 5 feet or greater, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to double the spell's range. When you cast a spell that has a range of Touch, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to make the range of the spell 30 feet.

options.name: Empowered Spell
options.description: When you roll damage for a spell, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to reroll a number of the damage dice up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one). You must use the new rolls. You can use Empowered Spell even if you have already used a different Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.

options.name: Extended Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that has a duration of 1 minute or longer, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to double its duration, to a maximum duration of 24 hours.

options.name: Heightened Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that forces a creature to make a saving throw to resist its effects, you can spend 2 Sorcery Points to give one target of the spell Disadvantage on its first saving throw made against the spell.

options.name: Quickened Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that has a casting time of an Action, you can spend 2 Sorcery Points to change the casting time to a Bonus Action for this casting. You can't modify a spell in this way if you've already cast a spell of 1st level or higher on the current turn, nor can you cast a spell of 1st level or higher on this turn after modifying a spell in this way.

options.name: Seeking Spell
options.description: If you make an attack roll for a spell and miss, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to reroll the d20, and you must use the new roll. You can use Seeking Spell even if you have already used a different Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.

options.name: Subtle Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to cast it without any Verbal or Somatic components.

options.name: Transmuted Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that deals a type of damage from the following list, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to change that damage type to one of the other listed types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, Thunder.

options.name: Twinned Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that can target only one creature and doesn't have a range of Self, you can spend a number of Sorcery Points equal to the spell's level to target a second creature in range with the same spell (1 Sorcery Point if the spell is a cantrip). To be eligible, a spell must be incapable of targeting more than one creature at the spell's current level. For example, Magic Missile and Scorching Ray aren't eligible, but Ray of Frost and Chromatic Orb are.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: Drawing from your inner wellspring of magic, you can cast spells. The magic you command flows from your innate power rather than from books or prayer.

features.name: Innate Sorcery
features.description: An event in your past, or in the life of a parent or ancestor, left an indelible mark on you, infusing you with a mote of wild magic. This power is a font of raw magic and is the origin of your sorcery. Choose your Sorcerous Origin. As a Bonus Action, you can unleash the magic of your origin to heighten your spells. When you do so, the spells you cast deal extra damage equal to your Charisma modifier if they deal damage, and you add your Charisma modifier to Constitution saving throws for maintaining Concentration. This lasts until the start of your next turn. You can use this feature twice, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.duration: Until start of your next turn
effects: Spells that deal damage deal extra damage equal to Charisma modifier
Add Charisma modifier to Constitution saves for Concentration

features.name: Font of Magic
features.description: You tap into the deep wellspring of magic within yourself. You have 2 Sorcery Points, and you gain more as you reach higher levels. You regain all spent Sorcery Points when you finish a Long Rest. You can use your Sorcery Points in the following ways.

converting_slots_to_points.effect: Expend a spell slot to gain Sorcery Points equal to the slot's level

creating_slots_from_points.note: Created slots vanish on Long Rest

features.name: Metamagic
features.description: You gain the ability to twist your spells to suit your needs. You gain two Metamagic options of your choice. You can use only one Metamagic option on a spell when you cast it, unless the option says otherwise. You gain another Metamagic option at levels 10 and 17.

features.name: Sorcerer Subclass
features.description: You gain a Sorcerer subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Sorcerer levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Sorcerer level or lower.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Sorcerer levels 8, 12, 16, and 19.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Sorcerous Restoration
features.description: When you finish a Short Rest, you can regain expended Sorcery Points, but no more than a number equal to half your Sorcerer level (round down). Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.trigger: Finish a Short Rest
mechanics.recovery: Up to half Sorcerer level (rounded down) Sorcery Points

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Sorcerer subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Metamagic (3rd option)
features.description: You gain one additional Metamagic option of your choice.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Sorcerer subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Sorcerer subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Metamagic (4th option)
features.description: You gain one additional Metamagic option of your choice.

features.name: Arcane Apotheosis
features.description: While your Innate Sorcery feature is active, you can use one Metamagic option on each of your turns without spending Sorcery Points on it.

mechanics.effect: Use one Metamagic option per turn for free (no Sorcery Point cost)

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain the Epic Boon of Dimensional Travel feat or another Epic Boon feat of your choice.

mechanics.type: Epic Boon feat (e.g. Epic Boon of Dimensional Travel)

features.name: Arcane Apotheosis upgrade
features.description: Your Innate Sorcery now lasts for 1 minute instead of until the start of your next turn.

mechanics.innate_sorcery_duration_upgrade: 1 minute (up from until start of your next turn)

subclasses.name: Draconic Sorcery
subclasses.description: Your innate magic comes from the gift of a dragon. Perhaps an ancient dragon facing death transferred some of its magical power to you or your ancestor. You might have absorbed magic from a site infused with a dragon's power, or perhaps you simply have draconic blood running through your veins.

features.name: Draconic Resilience
features.description: The magic in your body manifests physical traits of your draconic gift. Your Hit Point maximum increases by 3, and it increases by 1 whenever you gain another Sorcerer level. Parts of you are also covered by a thin sheen of dragon-like scales. When you aren't wearing armor, your base Armor Class equals 10 plus your Dexterity modifier plus your Charisma modifier.

features.name: Draconic Origin
features.description: Choose a type of dragon as the origin of your power. The dragon type you choose determines the damage type associated with many of your features. You also learn the language Draconic.

features.name: Elemental Affinity
features.description: When you cast a spell that deals damage of the type associated with your Draconic Origin, you can add your Charisma modifier to one damage roll of that spell. At the same time, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to gain Resistance to that damage type for 1 hour.

mechanics.trigger: Cast a spell dealing your Draconic Origin damage type
mechanics.bonus_damage: Add Charisma modifier to one damage roll

optional.effect: Gain Resistance to your origin damage type for 1 hour

features.name: Dragon Wings
features.description: You gain the ability to sprout a pair of dragon wings from your back, gaining a Fly Speed equal to your Speed. You can create these wings as a Bonus Action on your turn. They last until you dismiss them using another Bonus Action on your turn. You can't manifest your wings while wearing armor unless the armor is made to accommodate them, and clothing might be destroyed when you manifest them.

mechanics.effect: Sprout dragon wings; gain Fly Speed equal to Speed
mechanics.restriction: Cannot manifest while wearing armor (unless armor accommodates wings)

features.name: Draconic Presence
features.description: You can channel the dread presence of your dragon ancestor, causing those around you to become Awestruck or Frightened. As a Bonus Action, you can spend 5 Sorcery Points to draw on this power and exude an aura of awe or fear (your choice) to a distance of 60 feet. For 1 minute or until you lose Concentration (as if you were concentrating on a spell), each Hostile creature that starts its turn in this aura must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be Charmed (if you chose awe) or Frightened (if you chose fear) until the aura ends. A creature that succeeds on this saving throw is immune to your aura for 24 hours.

mechanics.on_success: Immune to your aura for 24 hours

subclasses.name: Wild Magic Sorcery
subclasses.description: Your innate magic comes from the wild forces of chaos that underlie the order of creation. You might have endured exposure to some form of raw magic, perhaps through a planar portal leading to Limbo, the Elemental Planes, or the mysterious Far Realm. Perhaps you were blessed by a powerful fey creature or marked by a demon. Or your magic could be a fluke of your birth, with no apparent cause or reason. However it came to be, this chaotic magic churns within you, waiting for any outlet.

features.name: Wild Magic Surge
features.description: Your spellcasting can unleash surges of untamed magic. Immediately after you cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot, the DM can have you roll a d20. If you roll a 1, roll on the Wild Magic Surge table to create a magical effect. If that effect is a spell, it is too wild to be affected by your Metamagic, and if it normally requires Concentration, it doesn't require Concentration in this case; the spell lasts for its full duration.

mechanics.trigger: Cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot
mechanics.effect: Roll on Wild Magic Surge table
mechanics.surge_spells: No Concentration required; last full duration

features.name: Tides of Chaos
features.description: You can manipulate the forces of chance and chaos to gain Advantage on one attack roll, ability check, or saving throw. Once you do so, you must finish a Long Rest before you can use this feature again. Any time before you regain the use of this feature, the DM can have you roll on the Wild Magic Surge table immediately after you cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot. You then regain the use of this feature.

mechanics.effect: Advantage on one attack roll, ability check, or saving throw
mechanics.dm_trigger: Before recharge, DM can trigger a Wild Magic Surge after casting a Sorcerer spell with a slot; doing so restores the use of Tides of Chaos

features.name: Bend Luck
features.description: You have the ability to twist fate using your wild magic. When another creature you can see makes an attack roll, an ability check, or a saving throw, you can use your Reaction and spend 2 Sorcery Points to roll 1d4 and apply the number rolled as a bonus or penalty (your choice) to the creature's roll. You can do this after the creature rolls but before any effects of the roll occur.

mechanics.trigger: Creature you can see makes an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw
mechanics.effect: Roll 1d4; apply as bonus or penalty to the creature's roll

features.name: Controlled Chaos
features.description: You gain a modicum of control over the surges of your wild magic. Whenever you roll on the Wild Magic Surge table, you can roll twice and use either number.

mechanics.trigger: Roll on the Wild Magic Surge table
mechanics.effect: Roll twice; choose which result to use

features.name: Spell Bombardment
features.description: The harmful energy of your spells intensifies. When you roll damage for a spell and roll the highest number possible on any of the dice, choose one of those dice, roll it again, and add that roll to the damage. You can use the feature only once per turn.

mechanics.trigger: Roll damage for a spell and roll the maximum on any die
mechanics.effect: Choose one of those max-rolled dice, roll it again, and add to damage

wild_magic_surge_table.note: Roll a d100 when a Wild Magic Surge occurs. This is a condensed version of the SRD table.

entries.effect: Roll on this table at the start of each of your turns for the next minute, ignoring this result on subsequent rolls.
For the next minute, you can see any invisible creature if you have line of sight to it.
A modron chosen and controlled by the DM appears in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you, then disappears 1 minute later.
You cast Fireball as a 3rd-level spell centered on yourself.
You cast Magic Missile as a 5th-level spell.
Roll a d10. Your height changes by a number of inches equal to the roll. If the roll is odd, you shrink. If the roll is even, you grow.
You cast Confusion centered on yourself.
For the next minute, you regain 5 Hit Points at the start of each of your turns.
You grow a long beard made of feathers that remains until you sneeze, at which point the feathers explode out from your face.
You cast Grease centered on yourself.
Creatures have Disadvantage on saving throws against the next spell you cast in the next minute that involves a saving throw.
Your skin turns a vibrant shade of blue. A Remove Curse spell can end this effect.
An eye appears on your forehead for the next minute. During that time, you have Advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight.
For the next minute, all your spells with a casting time of 1 action have a casting time of 1 Bonus Action.
You teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space of your choice that you can see.
You are transported to the Astral Plane until the end of your next turn, after which time you return to the space you previously occupied or the nearest unoccupied space if that space is occupied.
Maximize the damage of the next damaging spell you cast within the next minute.
Roll a d10. Your age changes by a number of years equal to the roll. If the roll is odd, you get younger (minimum 1 year old). If the roll is even, you get older.
1d6 Flumphs controlled by the DM appear in unoccupied spaces within 60 feet of you and are frightened of you. They vanish after 1 minute.
You regain 2d10 Hit Points.

You turn into a potted plant until the start of your next turn. While a plant, you are Incapacitated and have Vulnerability to all damage. If you drop to 0 Hit Points, your pot breaks, and your form reverts.
For the next minute, you can teleport up to 20 feet as a Bonus Action on each of your turns.
You cast Levitate on yourself.
A unicorn controlled by the DM appears in a space within 5 feet of you, then disappears 1 minute later.
You can't speak for the next minute. Whenever you try, pink bubbles float out of your mouth.
A spectral shield hovers near you for the next minute, granting you a +2 bonus to AC and immunity to Magic Missile.
You are immune to being intoxicated by alcohol for the next 5d6 days.
Your hair falls out but grows back within 24 hours.
For the next minute, any flammable object you touch that isn't being worn or carried by anyone else bursts into flame.
You regain your lowest-level expended spell slot.
For the next minute, you must shout when you speak.
You cast Fog Cloud centered on yourself.
Up to three creatures you choose within 30 feet of you take 4d10 Lightning damage.
You are frightened by the nearest creature until the end of your next turn.
Each creature within 30 feet of you becomes invisible for the next minute. The invisibility ends on a creature when it attacks or casts a spell.
You gain Resistance to all damage for the next minute.
A random creature within 60 feet of you becomes Poisoned for 1d4 hours.
You glow with bright light in a 30-foot radius for the next minute. Any creature that ends its turn within 5 feet of you is Blinded until the end of its next turn.
You cast Polymorph on yourself. If you fail the saving throw, you turn into a sheep for the spell's duration.
Illusory butterflies and flower petals flutter in the air within 10 feet of you for the next minute.
You can take one additional action immediately.
Each creature within 30 feet of you takes 1d10 Necrotic damage. You regain Hit Points equal to the sum of the Necrotic damage dealt.
You cast Mirror Image.
You cast Fly on a random creature within 60 feet of you.
You become Invisible for the next minute. During that time, other creatures can't hear you. The invisibility ends if you attack or cast a spell.
If you die within the next minute, you immediately come back to life as if by the Reincarnate spell.
Your size increases by one size category for the next minute.
You and all creatures within 30 feet of you gain Vulnerability to Piercing damage for the next minute.
You are surrounded by faint, ethereal music for the next minute.
You regain all expended Sorcery Points.

class.name: Druid
class.armor_notes: Druids will not wear armor or use shields made of metal.

spellcasting.ritual_note: You can cast any Druid spell you have prepared as a ritual if it has the ritual tag.

wild_shape.description: A central feature of the Druid, Wild Shape allows you to magically transform into animals. The rules for Wild Shape are detailed in each level entry where it is gained or upgraded.

uses_per_level.note: You regain all expended uses of Wild Shape when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

notes: You retain your personality, memories, and ability to speak while transformed.
You can't cast spells while transformed, but can maintain Concentration on spells already cast.
You revert when you run out of Hit Points, or choose to revert as a Bonus Action.
Gear merges with your form or falls to the ground; your choice.
You use the beast's stat block, replacing its INT/WIS/CHA with your own.
You retain your skill and saving throw proficiencies unless the beast has higher ones.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: Drawing on the divine essence of nature itself, you can cast spells to shape that essence to your will. You prepare the list of Druid spells available to you by choosing from the Druid spell list. When you do so, choose a number of Druid spells equal to your Wisdom modifier plus your Druid level (minimum of one spell). The spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. You can change your list of prepared spells when you finish a Long Rest. Preparing a new list of Druid spells requires time spent in communion with nature: at least 1 minute per spell level for each spell on your list.

mechanics.change_prepared: Long Rest (1 minute per spell level per spell)

features.name: Druidic
features.description: You know Druidic, the secret language of Druids. You can speak the language and use it to leave hidden messages. You and others who know this language automatically spot such a message. Others spot the message's presence with a successful DC 15 Wisdom (Perception) check but can't decipher it without magic.

mechanics.hidden_messages: Can leave hidden messages; auto-spotted by other Druids; DC 15 Perception to notice by others; requires magic to decipher

features.name: Primal Order
features.description: You have dedicated yourself to one of the following sacred roles of your choice: Magician — You know one extra cantrip from the Druid spell list. In addition, your mystical connection to nature gives you a bonus to your Intelligence (Arcana or Nature) checks. The bonus equals your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1). Warden — Trained for battle, you gain proficiency with Martial weapons and training with Medium armor.

options.name: Magician
benefits: One extra cantrip from the Druid spell list
Bonus to Intelligence (Arcana or Nature) checks equal to Wisdom modifier (minimum +1)

features.name: Wild Shape
features.description: The power of nature allows you to assume the form of an animal. As a Bonus Action, you can shape-shift into a Beast form that you have learned for this feature. You start with two Beast forms of your choice that have a Challenge Rating of 1/4 or lower and no Fly Speed or Swim Speed. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can replace one of your known forms with a different eligible form. You can use Wild Shape twice, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

mechanics.hp_in_form: Beast's HP; revert when reduced to 0
mechanics.revert: Bonus Action to revert; auto-reverts at 0 HP
retain: Skill and saving throw proficiencies (unless beast's are higher)
mechanics.equipment: Gear merges with new form or falls to ground (your choice)

features.name: Wild Companion
features.description: You can summon a nature spirit that assumes an animal form to aid you. As a Magic action, you can expend a spell slot to cast the Find Familiar spell without material components. When you cast the spell in this way, the familiar is a Fey instead of a Beast, Celestial, or Fiend, and the familiar disappears when you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.effect: Cast Find Familiar without material components

features.name: Druid Subclass
features.description: You gain a Druid subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Druid levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Druid level or lower.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Druid levels 8, 12, 16, and 19.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Wild Shape Improvement (CR 1/2)
features.description: Your Wild Shape improves, allowing you to transform into Beasts with a Challenge Rating of 1/2 or lower. Beasts with a Fly Speed are still excluded.

features.name: Wild Resurgence
features.description: Once on each of your turns, if you have no uses of Wild Shape left, you can give yourself one use by expending a spell slot (no action required). In addition, you can expend one use of Wild Shape (no action required) to give yourself a 1st-level spell slot, but you can do this no more than once per turn.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Druid subclass.

features.name: Elemental Fury
features.description: The power of the elements flows through you. You gain one of the following options of your choice: Potent Spellcasting — Add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any Druid cantrip. Primal Strike — Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon or a Beast form's attack in Wild Shape, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage (choose each time).

options.name: Potent Spellcasting
options.effect: Add Wisdom modifier to damage of Druid cantrips

options.name: Primal Strike
options.effect: Once per turn on a hit with a weapon or Wild Shape attack: deal extra 1d8 Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage (chosen each time)

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Wild Shape Improvement (CR 1)
features.description: Your Wild Shape improves, allowing you to transform into Beasts with a Challenge Rating of 1 or lower. Beasts with a Fly Speed are still excluded.

features.name: Wild Shape Improvement (CR 2)
features.description: Your Wild Shape improves, allowing you to transform into Beasts with a Challenge Rating of 2 or lower, including Beasts with a Swim Speed.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Druid subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Wild Shape Improvement (CR 3)
features.description: Your Wild Shape improves, allowing you to transform into Beasts with a Challenge Rating of 3 or lower.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Druid subclass.

features.name: Improved Elemental Fury
features.description: The option you chose for Elemental Fury grows more powerful: Potent Spellcasting — When you cast a Druid cantrip with a range of 10 feet or greater, the spell's range increases by 300 feet. Primal Strike — The extra damage of your Primal Strike increases to 2d8.

mechanics.potent_spellcasting_upgrade: Druid cantrips with range 10 ft or greater gain +300 ft range

features.name: Wild Shape Improvement (CR 4)
features.description: Your Wild Shape improves, allowing you to transform into Beasts with a Challenge Rating of 4 or lower.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Beast Spells
features.description: While using Wild Shape, you can cast spells in Beast form. You can perform the Somatic and Verbal components of a Druid spell while in a Beast form, but you aren't able to provide Material components that require being held.

mechanics.effect: Can cast Druid spells while in Beast form (Wild Shape)
components_not_allowed: Material components that require being held

features.name: Wild Shape Improvement (CR 6)
features.description: Your Wild Shape improves, allowing you to transform into Beasts with a Challenge Rating of 6 or lower, and Beasts with a Fly Speed are now permitted.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain the Epic Boon of Luck feat or another Epic Boon feat of your choice.

mechanics.type: Epic Boon feat (e.g. Epic Boon of Luck)

features.name: Archdruid
features.description: Your mastery of druidic magic and oneness with nature is complete. Whenever you roll Initiative, you can regain all your expended uses of Wild Shape. In addition, the Primal Order and Elemental Fury features are replaced by enhanced versions. You can ignore Verbal and Somatic components of Druid spells, as well as any Material components that lack a cost and aren't consumed by a spell. You must still provide Material components that have a cost or are consumed.

mechanics.initiative_trigger: Regain all expended Wild Shape uses when rolling Initiative

spellcasting_upgrade.still_required: Material components with a cost or that are consumed

subclasses.name: Circle of the Land
subclasses.description: The Circle of the Land is made up of mystics and sages who safeguard ancient knowledge and rites through a vast oral tradition. These Druids meet within sacred circles of trees or standing stones to whisper primal secrets in Druidic. The circle's wisest members preside as the chief priests of communities that hold to the Old Faith and serve as advisors to the rulers of such places.

features.name: Land Spell
features.description: Your mystical connection to the land infuses you with the ability to cast certain spells. When you finish a Long Rest, choose one type of land from the options below. Until your next Long Rest, you always have prepared the spells associated with that land (they don't count against your prepared spells). Arid (Desert) — Blur, Burning Hands, Fire Shield, Firewall, Wall of Sand. Arctic — Cone of Cold, Hold Person, Ice Storm, Sleet Storm, Spike Growth. Coast — Misty Step, Mirror Image, Shore, Water Breathing, Water Walk. Forest — Barkskin, Insect Plague, Speak with Plants, Spike Growth, Tree Stride. Grassland — Daylight, Haste, Pass Without Trace, Shatter, Wind Walk. Mountain — Meld into Stone, Passwall, Stoneskin, Stone Shape, Wall of Stone. Swamp — Freedom of Movement, Gaseous Form, Locate Creature, Scrying, Stinking Cloud. Underdark — Cloudkill, Gaseous Form, Greater Invisibility, Stone Shape, Web.

features.name: Natural Recovery
features.description: You can cast one of your prepared Druid spells without expending a spell slot, and you regain the use of this ability when you finish a Long Rest. In addition, when you finish a Short Rest, you can choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level that is equal to or less than half your Druid level (rounded up), and none of the slots can be of 6th level or higher.

free_spell_cast.effect: Cast one prepared Druid spell without expending a slot

short_rest_recovery.recovery_pool: Combined slot levels up to half Druid level (rounded up)

features.name: Land's Aid
features.description: As a Magic action, you can expend one use of Wild Shape and choose a point within 60 feet of yourself. Vitality-giving energy flows from that point in a 10-foot-radius sphere. Each creature of your choice in that area regains 1d6 Hit Points per level of the highest-level Druid spell slot you have expended today (minimum of 1d6). In addition, one creature of your choice in that area takes 2d6 Necrotic damage.

mechanics.healing: 1d6 per level of highest expended spell slot today (min 1d6), to chosen creatures
mechanics.damage: 2d6 Necrotic to one chosen creature in area

features.name: Nature's Ward
features.description: You are immune to the Poisoned condition. You also have Resistance to a damage type associated with your current Land Spell choice: Arid — Fire; Arctic — Cold; Coast — Lightning; Forest — Piercing; Grassland — Thunder; Mountain — Bludgeoning; Swamp — Poison; Underdark — Necrotic.

mechanics.resistance: Damage type tied to current Land Spell choice

features.name: Nature's Sanctuary
features.description: The natural world responds to your magic with protective energy. When a Beast or Plant creature attacks you, that creature must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature must choose a different target, or the attack automatically misses. On a successful save, the creature is immune to this effect for 24 hours. The creature is aware of this effect before it makes its attack against you.

mechanics.trigger: Beast or Plant creature attacks you
mechanics.failure: Must choose a different target or attack misses automatically
mechanics.success: Immune to this effect for 24 hours
mechanics.note: Creature is aware of the effect before attacking

subclasses.name: Circle of the Moon
subclasses.description: Druids of the Circle of the Moon are fierce guardians of the wilds. Their order gathers under the full moon to share news and trade warnings. They haunt the deepest parts of the wilderness, where they might go for weeks on end before crossing paths with another humanoid creature, let alone another Druid.

features.name: Combat Wild Shape
features.description: You gain the ability to use Wild Shape in more aggressive ways. You can use Wild Shape as a Bonus Action. In addition, while in a Wild Shape form, you can use a Bonus Action to expend one spell slot and regain 1d8 Hit Points per level of the spell slot expended.

mechanics.activation_upgrade: Wild Shape can now be activated as a Bonus Action

healing.activation: Bonus Action while in Wild Shape
healing.healing: 1d8 HP per level of expended spell slot

features.name: Circle Forms
features.description: The rites of your circle grant you the ability to transform into more dangerous animal forms. You can use Wild Shape to transform into a Beast with a Challenge Rating as high as 1 (you ignore the CR 1/4 limit of the base Wild Shape feature). Starting at level 6, your CR limit increases to match the limits in the Wild Shape table.

mechanics.note: From level 6 onward follows standard Wild Shape CR table

features.name: Elemental Wild Shape
features.description: You can expend two uses of Wild Shape at the same time to transform into an Air Elemental, an Earth Elemental, a Fire Elemental, or a Water Elemental.

features.name: Thousand Forms
features.description: You have learned to use magic to alter your physical form in more subtle ways. You can cast Alter Self at will, without expending a spell slot.

features.name: Beast Spells
features.description: You can cast many of your Druid spells in any shape you assume using Wild Shape. You can perform the Somatic and Verbal components of a Druid spell while in a Beast form, but you aren't able to provide Material components that require being held.

mechanics.effect: Cast Druid spells while in any Wild Shape form
components_not_allowed: Material components that require being held
mechanics.note: Circle of the Moon gets this at level 14; base class gets it at level 18

subclasses.name: Circle of the Sea
subclasses.description: Druids of the Circle of the Sea draw on the tides and storms of the ocean. Residing on coasts and aboard ships, these Druids are uniquely suited to work with sailors and pirates. Their magic draws on elemental connections between ocean and sky.

features.name: Wrath of the Sea
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can expend one use of Wild Shape to manifest the power of the sea around you for 10 minutes. During this time, when you finish casting a spell with a spell slot, the following things can happen: Each creature of your choice within 5 feet of you takes Cold damage equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1); and one creature of your choice within 60 feet that took the Cold damage or was hit by the spell must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC or be Pushed up to 15 feet away from you and have the Prone condition.

mechanics.trigger_during: Finish casting a spell with a spell slot

push_prone.target: One chosen creature within 60 ft that took cold damage or was hit by the spell
push_prone.failure: Pushed up to 15 ft and Prone

features.name: Gift of the Sea
features.description: You gain a Swim Speed of 30 feet and the ability to breathe underwater.

features.name: Aquatic Affinity
features.description: The aquatic beasts of the sea heed your call. You can cast Conjure Animals without expending a spell slot, but when you do so, the only beasts you can summon with it are those that have a Swim Speed. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. In addition, while underwater, you have Resistance to Fire damage.

mechanics.bonus: Resistance to Fire damage while underwater

features.name: Stormborn
features.description: You gain a Fly Speed equal to your Speed whenever you are not underground or indoors.

features.name: Oceanic Gift
features.description: Instead of you, one willing creature you can see within 60 feet of you gains the benefit of Wrath of the Sea. Any uses of Wrath of the Sea still expend your Wild Shape uses, but the triggered effects use your Wisdom modifier and spell save DC.

mechanics.target: One willing creature within 60 ft (instead of yourself)
mechanics.cost: Still uses your Wild Shape uses

subclasses.name: Circle of the Stars
subclasses.description: The Circle of the Stars allows Druids to draw on the power of moonlight, the stars, and the constellations. Druids of this circle have kept records of the night sky spanning generations and have observed how patterns in the stars affect the world below.

features.name: Star Map
features.description: You've created a star map that is a Tiny object and can serve as a Spellcasting Focus for your Druid spells. While holding this map, you have these benefits: You know the Guidance cantrip. You always have the Guiding Bolt spell prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare. You can cast Guiding Bolt without expending a spell slot a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Starry Form
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape feature to take on a starry form, rather than transforming into a Beast. While in your starry form, you retain your game statistics but your body becomes luminous, shedding Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet. The form lasts for 10 minutes. When you assume the form, choose its name from Archer, Chalice, or Dragon, each of which grants a different benefit for the duration. Archer — When you activate this form and as a Bonus Action on each turn, you can make a ranged spell attack, hurling a luminous arrow targeting one creature within 60 feet. On a hit, the arrow deals Radiant damage equal to 1d8 + your Wisdom modifier. Chalice — Whenever you cast a healing spell using a spell slot, you or one creature within 30 feet of you can regain Hit Points equal to 1d8 + your Wisdom modifier. Dragon — Immediately after you use your Concentration to maintain a spell, you can make one attack with your Unarmed Strike as a Bonus Action. If it hits, it deals Radiant damage equal to 1d6 + your Wisdom modifier.

mechanics.light: Bright Light 10 ft, Dim Light additional 10 ft

forms.name: Archer
forms.effect: On activation and as Bonus Action each turn: ranged spell attack (60 ft), 1d8 + WIS modifier Radiant damage on hit

forms.name: Chalice
forms.effect: When casting a healing spell with a slot: you or a creature within 30 ft regains 1d8 + WIS modifier HP

forms.name: Dragon
forms.effect: After using Concentration to maintain a spell: one Unarmed Strike as Bonus Action; on hit 1d6 + WIS modifier Radiant

features.name: Cosmic Omen
features.description: You learn to use your star map to divine the will of the cosmos. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can consult your Star Map for omens and roll a die. Until you finish your next Long Rest, you gain access to a special Reaction based on whether you rolled an odd or even number on the die: Weal (even) — When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll, an ability check, or a saving throw, you can use your Reaction to roll a d6 and add it to the result. Woe (odd) — When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll, an ability check, or a saving throw, you can use your Reaction to roll a d6 and subtract it from the result. You can use the Reaction a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.trigger: Finish a Long Rest

weal.trigger: Creature within 30 ft makes attack roll, ability check, or saving throw
weal.effect: Roll 1d6 and ADD to the result

woe.trigger: Creature within 30 ft makes attack roll, ability check, or saving throw
woe.effect: Roll 1d6 and SUBTRACT from the result

features.name: Twinkling Constellations
features.description: The constellations of your Starry Form improve. The 1d8 of the Archer and the 1d8 of the Chalice each become 2d8. Moreover, while in your Starry Form, you have a Fly Speed of 20 feet and can hover.

features.name: Full of Stars
features.description: While in your Starry Form, you become partially incorporeal, giving you Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

spellcasting.prepared_spells_formula: Charisma modifier + half Paladin level (rounded down, minimum 1)
spellcasting.note: Paladin is a half-caster. Spell slots begin at level 1 but the Paladin does not formally gain Spellcasting until level 1 via the Channel Divinity feature. Spell slots are shared with the Spellcasting feature gained at level 1.

features.name: Lay on Hands
features.description: Your blessed touch can heal wounds. You have a pool of healing power that replenishes when you finish a Long Rest. With that pool, you can restore a total number of Hit Points equal to five times your Paladin level. As a Magic action, you can touch a creature and draw power from the pool to restore a number of Hit Points to that creature, up to the maximum amount remaining in your pool. Alternatively, you can expend 5 Hit Points from your pool of healing to cure the target of one disease or neutralize one poison affecting it. You can cure multiple diseases and neutralize multiple poisons with a single use of Lay on Hands, expending Hit Points separately for each one. This feature has no effect on Undead or Constructs.

mechanics.pool: 5 × Paladin level HP (replenished on Long Rest)
mechanics.heal: Restore any number of HP from pool to one creature
mechanics.cure_disease_or_poison: Expend 5 HP from pool per disease or poison cured

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You have learned to draw on divine magic through meditation and prayer to cast spells as a Paladin. You prepare the list of Paladin spells available to you by choosing from the Paladin spell list. When you do so, choose a number of Paladin spells equal to your Charisma modifier plus half your Paladin level, rounded down (minimum of one spell). The spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. You can change your list of prepared spells when you finish a Long Rest. Preparing a new list of Paladin spells requires time spent in prayer and meditation: at least 1 minute per spell level for each spell on your list.

mechanics.prepared_spells: Charisma modifier + half Paladin level (rounded down, minimum 1)
mechanics.change_prepared: Long Rest (1 minute per spell level per spell)

features.name: Weapon Mastery
features.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of Simple or Martial weapons of your choice. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change one of those weapon choices.
