When you reach certain Paladin levels, you gain the ability to use the mastery properties of more weapon types, as shown in the Weapon Mastery column of the Paladin Features table.

features.name: Fighting Style
features.description: You gain a Fighting Style feat of your choice. Whenever you gain a Paladin level, you can replace the feat you chose with a different Fighting Style feat.

options.name: Blessed Warrior
options.description: You learn two cantrips of your choice from the Cleric spell list. They count as Paladin spells for you, and Charisma is your spellcasting ability for them. Whenever you gain a Paladin level, you can replace one of these cantrips with another cantrip from the Cleric spell list.

options.name: Blind Fighting
options.description: You have Blindsight with a range of 10 feet.

options.name: Defense
options.description: While you're wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class.

options.name: Dueling
options.description: When you're wielding a Melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.

options.name: Great Weapon Fighting
options.description: When you roll damage for an attack you make with a Melee weapon that you are holding with two hands, you can reroll any of the damage dice that show a 1 or 2, and you must use the new roll. The weapon must have the Two-Handed or Versatile property to gain this benefit.

options.name: Interception
options.description: When a creature you can see hits a target, other than you, within 5 feet of you with an attack, you can use your Reaction to reduce the damage the target takes by 1d10 plus your Proficiency Bonus. You must be wielding a Shield or a Simple or Martial weapon to use this Reaction.

options.name: Protection
options.description: When a creature you can see attacks a target other than you that is within 5 feet of you, you can use your Reaction to impose Disadvantage on the attack roll. You must be wielding a Shield.

features.name: Paladin's Smite
features.description: You always have the Divine Smite spell prepared. In addition, you can cast it without expending a spell slot, but you must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it in this way again.

mechanics.note: Divine Smite: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can expend one spell slot to deal 2d8 Radiant damage plus 1d8 per slot level above 1st. Undead and Fiends take 1d8 extra.

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: You can channel divine energy to fuel magical effects. You start with two uses per Long Rest and gain an additional use at Paladin levels 7 and 15. You regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. Each Channel Divinity option is described in its relevant subclass or class feature.

features.name: Paladin Subclass
features.description: You gain a Paladin subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Paladin levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Paladin level or lower.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Paladin levels 8, 12, 16, and 19.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Weapon Mastery (3 weapons)
features.description: You can now use the mastery properties of three kinds of weapons.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Faithful Steed
features.description: You can call on the aid of an otherworldly steed. You always have the Find Steed spell prepared. You can cast it once without expending a spell slot, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Aura of Protection
features.description: You radiate a protective, divine aura. You and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you gain a bonus to saving throws equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum bonus of +1). You must be conscious to grant this bonus. The aura's radius increases to 30 feet at Paladin level 18.

mechanics.bonus: Charisma modifier (minimum +1) to all saving throws
mechanics.targets: You and friendly creatures within range

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Paladin subclass.

features.name: Channel Divinity (3 uses)
features.description: You now have 3 uses of Channel Divinity.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Abjure Foes
features.description: As a Magic action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to overwhelm foes with awe. As you present your Holy Symbol or weapon, you can target a number of creatures of your choice that you can see within 60 feet of you, up to a number equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). Each target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the Frightened condition for 1 minute or until it takes any damage. On a successful save, the target's Speed is halved until the end of its next turn.

mechanics.targets: Up to Charisma modifier creatures (minimum 1) you can see
mechanics.failure: Frightened for 1 minute or until taking any damage
mechanics.success: Speed halved until end of its next turn

features.name: Aura of Courage
features.description: You and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you can't be Frightened while you are conscious. The aura's radius increases to 30 feet at Paladin level 18.

mechanics.effect: You and friendly creatures within range can't be Frightened

features.name: Weapon Mastery (4 weapons)
features.description: You can now use the mastery properties of four kinds of weapons.

features.name: Radiant Strikes
features.description: You are so suffused with righteous might that all your strikes carry divine power with them. When you hit a target with an attack roll using a Melee weapon or an Unarmed Strike, the target takes an extra 1d8 Radiant damage.

mechanics.trigger: Hit a target with a melee weapon or Unarmed Strike

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Restoring Touch
features.description: When you use Lay on Hands on a creature, you can also remove one or more of the following conditions from the creature: Blinded, Charmed, Deafened, Frightened, Paralyzed, or Stunned. To remove each condition, you must expend 5 Hit Points from your Lay on Hands healing pool.

mechanics.trigger: Use Lay on Hands
mechanics.cost_per_condition: 5 HP from Lay on Hands pool

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Paladin subclass.

features.name: Channel Divinity (4 uses)
features.description: You now have 4 uses of Channel Divinity.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Aura Expansion
features.description: Your Aura of Protection and Aura of Courage now have a radius of 30 feet instead of 10 feet.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain the Epic Boon of Truesight feat or another Epic Boon feat of your choice.

mechanics.type: Epic Boon feat (e.g. Epic Boon of Truesight)

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Paladin subclass.

subclasses.name: Oath of Devotion
subclasses.description: The Oath of Devotion binds a Paladin to the loftiest ideals of justice, virtue, and order. Sometimes called cavaliers, white knights, or holy warriors, these Paladins meet the ideal of the knight in shining armor, acting with honor in pursuit of justice and the greater good. They hold themselves to the highest standards of conduct, and some, for better or worse, hold the rest of the world to the same standards.
tenets: Honesty: Don't lie or cheat. Let your word be your promise.
Courage: Never fear to act, though caution is wise.
Compassion: Aid others, protect the weak, and punish those who threaten them.
Honor: Treat others with fairness, and let your honorable deeds be an example.
Duty: Be responsible for your actions and their consequences, protect those in your care.

oath_spells.note: These spells are always prepared and do not count against your prepared spells total.

features.name: Sacred Weapon
features.description: When you take the Attack action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to imbue one Melee weapon that you are holding with positive energy. For 10 minutes or until you use this feature again, the weapon emits Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light 20 feet beyond that. You can also use your Charisma modifier for the weapon's attack and damage rolls. The weapon's damage is Radiant, and the weapon is considered magical for overcoming Resistance and Immunity.

mechanics.light: Bright Light 20 ft, Dim Light additional 20 ft

features.name: Aura of Devotion
features.description: You and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you can't be Charmed while you are conscious. At Paladin level 18, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

mechanics.effect: You and friendly creatures within range can't be Charmed

features.name: Smite of Protection
features.description: Your Divine Smite now also shields its target or an ally. Whenever you cast Divine Smite, you can choose yourself or a creature within 30 feet of you. The chosen creature gains a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to your Paladin level.

mechanics.trigger: Cast Divine Smite
mechanics.effect: You or a creature within 30 ft gains Temp HP equal to Paladin level

features.name: Holy Nimbus
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can emanate an aura of sunlight for 1 minute. For the duration: you emit Bright Light in a 30-foot radius and Dim Light 30 feet beyond that; hostile creatures in the bright light have Disadvantage on saving throws against your spells and Channel Divinity options; and when a hostile creature starts its turn in the bright light, it takes 10 Radiant damage. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.light: Bright Light 30 ft, Dim Light additional 30 ft
effect_on_hostiles_in_light: Disadvantage on saving throws against your spells and Channel Divinity
10 Radiant damage at start of their turn

subclasses.name: Oath of Glory
subclasses.description: Paladins who take the Oath of Glory believe they and their companions are destined to achieve glory through deeds of heroism. They train diligently and encourage their companions, so that they're all ready when destiny calls.
tenets: Actions over Words: Demonstrate your ideals through your actions.
Glorious Acts: Perform deeds worthy of legend.
No Regrets: Take every opportunity to do good, even if it causes hardship.
Destiny: Believe in the strength of the stars, and accept your greater destiny.

oath_spells.note: These spells are always prepared and do not count against your prepared spells total.

features.name: Inspiring Smite
features.description: Immediately after you cast Divine Smite, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity and distribute Temporary Hit Points to creatures of your choice within 30 feet of you, which can include you. The total number of Temporary Hit Points equals 2d8 plus your Paladin level, divided among the chosen creatures however you like.

mechanics.trigger: Immediately after casting Divine Smite
mechanics.distribution: Divided among chosen creatures however you like

features.name: Peerless Athlete
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to augment yourself with divine athleticism. For 1 hour, you have Advantage on Strength (Athletics) and Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks, and the distance of your long and high jumps increases by 10 feet.

features.name: Aura of Alacrity
features.description: Your speed increases by 10 feet. In addition, if you aren't Incapacitated, the speed of any friendly creature that starts its turn within 10 feet of you increases by 10 feet until the end of that turn. At Paladin level 18, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

mechanics.aura_effect: Friendly creatures starting turn within range gain +10 feet Speed until end of their turn

features.name: Glorious Defense
features.description: You can turn the might of your foes into a glory for yourself or your allies. When you or another creature you can see within 10 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you can use your Reaction to grant a bonus to the target's AC against that attack, potentially causing it to miss. The bonus equals your Charisma modifier (minimum of +1). If the attack misses, you can make one weapon attack against the attacker as part of this Reaction if the attacker is within your weapon's range. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.trigger: You or creature within 10 ft is hit by an attack roll
mechanics.bonus: +Charisma modifier to target's AC against that attack (minimum +1)
mechanics.on_miss: Make one weapon attack against the attacker (if within weapon range) as part of the Reaction

features.name: Living Legend
features.description: You can empower yourself with the legends — whether true or exaggerated — of your deeds. As a Bonus Action, you gain the following benefits for 1 minute: Charisma Saving Throws — You are blessed with an otherworldly presence, and can add your Charisma modifier to your saving throws. Inspiring Strikes — Once on each of your turns when you miss a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause that attack to hit instead. Retributive Smite — If you fail a saving throw, you can use your Reaction to succeed on it instead. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

effects: Add Charisma modifier to all saving throws
Once per turn: a missed weapon attack hits instead
Reaction: succeed on a failed saving throw instead

subclasses.name: Oath of the Ancients
subclasses.description: The Oath of the Ancients is as old as the race of elves and the rituals of the druids. Sometimes called fey knights, green knights, or horned knights, Paladins who swear this oath cast their lot with the side of the light in the cosmic struggle against darkness because they love the beautiful and life-giving things of the world, not necessarily because they seek to preserve law and order.
tenets: Kindle the Light: Through your acts of mercy, kindness, and forgiveness, kindle the light of hope in the world.
Shelter the Light: Where there is good, beauty, love, and laughter in the world, stand against the wickedness that would swallow it.
Preserve Your Own Light: Delight in song and laughter, in beauty and art. Hold to the things that bring you joy.
Be the Light: Be a glorious beacon for all who live in despair.

oath_spells.note: These spells are always prepared and do not count against your prepared spells total.

features.name: Nature's Wrath
features.description: As a Magic action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to conjure spectral vines around nearby creatures. Each creature of your choice that you can see within 15 feet of you must make a Strength saving throw or have the Restrained condition for 1 minute. A Restrained creature repeats the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

mechanics.failure: Restrained for 1 minute (repeats save at end of each turn)

features.name: Aura of Warding
features.description: Ancient magic lies so heavily upon you that it forms an eldritch ward. You and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you have Resistance to damage from spells. At Paladin level 18, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

mechanics.effect: You and friendly creatures within range have Resistance to damage from spells

features.name: Undying Sentinel
features.description: When you are reduced to 0 Hit Points and are not killed outright, you can choose to drop to 1 Hit Point instead. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest. Additionally, you suffer none of the drawbacks of old age, and you can't be aged magically.

mechanics.trigger: Reduced to 0 HP without being killed outright
mechanics.effect: Drop to 1 HP instead

features.name: Elder Champion
features.description: You can assume the form of an ancient force of nature, taking on an appearance you choose: a towering green-clad knight, a fierce elf with bark-like skin, or an ancient knight covered in holly and trees. Using your Bonus Action, you undergo a transformation. For 1 minute, you gain the following benefits: Regeneration — At the start of each of your turns, you regain 10 Hit Points. Speak with Nature — Beasts and Plant creatures have Disadvantage on attack rolls against you. Nature's Resilience — When you cast a spell with a spell slot, you can expend a use of your Channel Divinity (no action required) to have the spell affect a creature as if it were cast with a spell slot two levels higher. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

effects: Regeneration: Regain 10 HP at start of each turn
Beasts and Plant creatures have Disadvantage on attacks against you
When casting a spell with a slot, can expend Channel Divinity (no action) to treat it as 2 slot levels higher

subclasses.name: Oath of Vengeance
subclasses.description: The Oath of Vengeance is a solemn commitment to punish those who have committed a grievous sin. When evil forces slaughter helpless villagers, when an entire people turns against the will of the gods, when a thieves' guild grows too powerful and reckless, when a dragon rampages through the countryside — at times like these, Paladins arise and swear an Oath of Vengeance to set right that which has gone wrong.
tenets: Fight the Greater Evil: Faced with a choice of fighting my sworn foes or combating a lesser evil, I choose the greater evil.
No Mercy for the Wicked: Ordinary foes might win my mercy, but my sworn enemies do not.
By Any Means Necessary: My qualms can't get in the way of exterminating my foes.
Restitution: If my foes wreak ruin on the world, it is because I failed to stop them.

oath_spells.note: These spells are always prepared and do not count against your prepared spells total.

features.name: Vow of Enmity
features.description: When you take the Attack action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to utter a vow of enmity against a creature you can see within 30 feet of you. You gain Advantage on attack rolls against the creature for 1 minute or until it drops to 0 Hit Points or falls Unconscious.

mechanics.effect: Advantage on attack rolls against the target
mechanics.duration: 1 minute, or until target drops to 0 HP or becomes Unconscious

features.name: Relentless Avenger
features.description: Your supernatural focus helps you close off a foe's retreat. When you hit a creature with an Opportunity Attack, you can reduce the creature's Speed to 0 until the end of the current turn. You can then move up to half your Speed as part of the same Reaction.

mechanics.trigger: Hit a creature with an Opportunity Attack
mechanics.effect_1: Reduce target's Speed to 0 until end of current turn
mechanics.effect_2: Move up to half your Speed as part of the same Reaction

features.name: Soul of Vengeance
features.description: The authority with which you speak your Vow of Enmity gives you greater power over your foe. When a creature under the effect of your Vow of Enmity makes an attack, you can use your Reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature if it is within range.

mechanics.trigger: Creature under your Vow of Enmity makes an attack
mechanics.effect: Make one melee weapon attack against that creature (if within range)

features.name: Avenging Angel
features.description: You can assume the form of an angelic avenger. Using your Bonus Action, you undergo a transformation. For 1 hour, you gain the following benefits: Flight — You sprout wings and gain a Fly Speed of 60 feet. Frightful Aura — Whenever an enemy creature starts its turn within 30 feet of you, it must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC equals your spell save DC). On a failed save, the creature has the Frightened condition for 1 minute or until it takes any damage. Attack rolls against a Frightened creature have Advantage. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

frightful_aura.trigger: Enemy starts its turn within 30 feet
frightful_aura.failure: Frightened for 1 minute or until taking damage
frightful_aura.note: Attack rolls against Frightened creatures have Advantage

spellcasting.prepared_spells_formula: Wisdom modifier + half Ranger level (rounded down, minimum 1)
spellcasting.note: Ranger is a half-caster. Spell slots begin at level 2.

features.name: Expertise
features.description: You gain Expertise in two of your skill proficiencies of your choice. Expertise means your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make using the chosen proficiency.

mechanics.effect: Double proficiency bonus on chosen skills

features.name: Favored Enemy
features.description: You are adept at focusing your ire on a single foe. You always have the Hunter's Mark spell prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare. Moreover, you don't have to concentrate on the spell once you cast it; it lasts for its full duration. You can cast it a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once) without expending a spell slot, and you regain all expended uses of this casting when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Weapon Mastery
features.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change one of those weapon choices. When you reach certain Ranger levels, you gain the ability to use the mastery properties of more weapon types, as shown in the Weapon Mastery column of the Ranger Features table.

features.name: Deft Explorer
features.description: You are a master of navigating the natural world, and you react with swift and decisive action when the situation demands it. You gain the following benefits: Expertise — You gain Expertise in one more skill of your choice from your class skill list. Roving — Your Speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing Heavy armor. You also have a Climb Speed and a Swim Speed equal to your Speed.

mechanics.expertise_bonus: One additional skill from Ranger class list

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You have learned to cast spells through studying the natural world. You prepare the list of Ranger spells available to you by choosing from the Ranger spell list. When you do so, choose a number of Ranger spells equal to your Wisdom modifier plus half your Ranger level, rounded down (minimum of one spell). You can change your list of prepared spells when you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.prepared_spells: Wisdom modifier + half Ranger level (rounded down, minimum 1)

features.name: Ranger Subclass
features.description: You gain a Ranger subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Ranger levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Ranger level or lower.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Ranger levels 8, 12, 16, and 19.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Ranger subclass.

features.name: Conjure Barrage
features.description: You always have the Conjure Barrage spell prepared.

features.name: Weapon Mastery (3 weapons)
features.description: You can now use the mastery properties of three kinds of weapons.

features.name: Relentless Hunter
features.description: Taking damage can't break your Concentration on Hunter's Mark.

mechanics.effect: Concentration on Hunter's Mark cannot be broken by taking damage

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Stoic
features.description: When you are subjected to an effect that would give you the Frightened or Poisoned condition, you can use a Reaction to give yourself Resistance to the triggering effect's damage type (if any) and Immunity to the Frightened and Poisoned conditions until the end of your next turn.

mechanics.trigger: Effect would give you the Frightened or Poisoned condition
effects: Resistance to the triggering effect's damage type (if any)
Immunity to Frightened condition until end of next turn
Immunity to Poisoned condition until end of next turn

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Ranger subclass.

features.name: Tireless
features.description: Primal forces now help fuel your treks. As a Magic action, you can give yourself a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to 1d8 plus your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1 Temporary Hit Point). You can use this action a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. In addition, whenever you finish a Short Rest, your Exhaustion level, if any, decreases by 1.

features.name: Conjure Volley
features.description: You always have the Conjure Volley spell prepared.

features.name: Weapon Mastery (4 weapons)
features.description: You can now use the mastery properties of four kinds of weapons.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Ranger subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Ranger subclass.

features.name: Weapon Mastery (4 weapons — confirmed)
features.description: Your Weapon Mastery count is confirmed at 4 weapons.

features.name: Feral Senses
features.description: Your connection to the natural world gives you Blindsight with a range of 10 feet.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Precise Hunter
features.description: You have Advantage on attack rolls against the creature currently marked by your Hunter's Mark.

mechanics.effect: Advantage on attack rolls against the Hunter's Mark target

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain the Epic Boon of Dimensional Travel feat or another Epic Boon feat of your choice.

mechanics.type: Epic Boon feat (e.g. Epic Boon of Dimensional Travel)

features.name: Foe Slayer
features.description: You become an unparalleled hunter. Your Wisdom modifier is now added to the attack rolls of your weapons and Unarmed Strikes.

mechanics.effect: Add Wisdom modifier to attack rolls with weapons and Unarmed Strikes

subclasses.name: Beast Master
subclasses.description: The Beast Master archetype embodies a friendship between the civilized races and the beasts of the world. United in focus, beast and ranger work as one to fight the monstrous foes that threaten civilization and the natural world alike.

features.name: Primal Companion
features.description: You magically summon a primal beast, which draws strength from your bond with nature. The beast is friendly to you and your companions and obeys your commands. Choose its stat block: Beast of the Land, Beast of the Sea, or Beast of the Sky. The beast appears in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. In combat, the beast acts on your Initiative. It can move and use its Reaction on its own, but takes the Dodge action unless you use a Bonus Action to command it to take an action from its stat block or take another action. If the beast dies, you can use a Magic action to touch a willing beast, and your Primal Beast magically transforms into that beast. A new Primal Beast appears at the start of your next turn with all its HP, unless you expend a spell slot of 1st level or higher — in which case it returns with half its HP instead.

mechanics.activation: Summoned using a Magic action; first appears at 3rd level automatically

command.effect: Beast takes an action from its stat block or another action

on_death.recovery_magic_action: Touch a willing beast; your Primal Beast transforms into it
on_death.respawn: New Primal Beast appears at start of your next turn with all HP
on_death.with_spell_slot: Expend 1st-level+ slot: returns with half HP instead

actions.name: Maul
actions.description: Melee attack: PB + STR to hit, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d8 + STR Slashing. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC = 8 + PB + STR) or have the Prone condition.

traits: Charge: If the beast moves at least 20 ft straight toward a target and then hits it, the target takes an extra 1d6 damage and must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC = 8 + PB + STR) or be knocked Prone.

actions.name: Binding Strike
actions.description: Melee attack: PB + STR or DEX to hit, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d6 + STR or DEX Piercing or Slashing (your choice). If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC = 8 + PB + STR or DEX).

actions.name: Shred
actions.description: Melee attack: PB + DEX to hit, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d4 + DEX Slashing.

traits: Flyby: The beast doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

features.name: Exceptional Training
features.description: When you use your Bonus Action to command your Primal Companion to take an action, you can also command it to take the Dash, Disengage, Dodge, or Help action using that same Bonus Action. The beast's attacks now count as magical for the purpose of overcoming Resistance and Immunity.

features.name: Bestial Fury
features.description: When you command your Primal Companion to use the Maul, Binding Strike, or Shred action, the beast can use the action twice.

mechanics.effect: Primal Companion's attack action can be used twice when commanded

features.name: Share Spells
features.description: When you cast a spell with a range of Self, you can also affect your Primal Companion with the spell if the beast is within 30 feet of you.

mechanics.trigger: Cast a spell with range of Self
mechanics.effect: Primal Companion is also affected (if within 30 ft)

features.name: Superior Beast's Defense
features.description: Whenever you cast a spell that targets only you, you can also target your Primal Companion with the spell, provided it is within 30 feet of you. In addition, whenever you are the target of a spell or effect that grants Resistance or Immunity to a damage type, your Primal Companion gains the same benefit for the same duration.

mechanics.spell_sharing: Spells targeting only you can also target Primal Companion (within 30 ft)
mechanics.resistance_immunity_sharing: Resistance or Immunity you gain is also shared with Primal Companion for same duration

subclasses.name: Fey Wanderer
subclasses.description: A fey mystique surrounds you, thanks to the boon of an archfey or a location steeped in fey power. This mystique grants you abilities that are otherworldly and beguiling.

features.name: Dreadful Strikes
features.description: You can augment your weapon strikes with mind-scarring magic drawn from the murky twilight of the Feywild. When you hit a creature with a weapon, you can deal an extra 1d4 Psychic damage to the target, which can take this extra damage only once per turn. The extra damage increases to 1d6 when you reach 11th level in this class.

mechanics.trigger: Hit a creature with a weapon

features.name: Fey Wanderer Magic
features.description: You always have certain spells prepared. These spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare.

features.name: Otherworldly Glamour
features.description: Your fey qualities give you a supernatural charm. As a result, you gain proficiency in the Deception, Performance, or Persuasion skill (your choice). In addition, whenever you make a Charisma check, you gain a bonus to the check equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1).

features.name: Beguiling Twist
features.description: The magic of the Feywild guards your mind. You have Advantage on saving throws against the Charmed and Frightened conditions. In addition, whenever you or a creature you can see within 120 feet of you succeeds on a saving throw against being Charmed or Frightened, you can use your Reaction to force a different creature you can see within 120 feet of you to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. If it fails, the target is Charmed or Frightened by you (your choice) for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

mechanics.personal_benefit: Advantage on saves against Charmed and Frightened

reaction_effect.trigger: You or visible creature within 120 ft succeeds on save vs Charmed or Frightened
reaction_effect.target: A different visible creature within 120 ft
reaction_effect.failure: Charmed or Frightened by you (your choice) for 1 minute; repeats save at end of each turn

features.name: Fey Reinforcements
features.description: You can cast Summon Fey without a spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Whenever you start casting the spell, you can modify it so that it doesn't require Concentration. If you do so, the spell's duration becomes 1 minute for that casting.

features.name: Misty Wanderer
features.description: You can cast Misty Step without expending a spell slot. You can do so a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. In addition, whenever you cast Misty Step, you can bring along one willing creature you can see within 5 feet of you. That creature teleports to an unoccupied space of your choice within 5 feet of your destination space.

bring_ally.destination: Unoccupied space within 5 ft of your destination

features.name: Fey Wanderer Capstone
features.description: Your mastery over fey magic intensifies. You can use your Beguiling Twist reaction without expending a Reaction if the triggering save succeeds by a margin you choose. In addition, the Charmed and Frightened conditions you impose through Beguiling Twist last until the end of your next turn as a default, but can still extend as noted.

mechanics.note: Subclass feature at level 14 per SRD framework; details reflect progression of Beguiling Twist and expanded Fey Wanderer abilities

subclasses.name: Gloom Stalker
subclasses.description: Gloom Stalkers are at home in the darkest places: deep under the earth, in gloomy alleyways, in primeval forests, and wherever else the light dims. Most folk enter such places with trepidation, but a Gloom Stalker ventures boldly into the darkness, seeking to ambush threats before they can reach the broader world.

features.name: Dread Ambusher
features.description: You have mastered the art of the ambush. You can give yourself a bonus to your Initiative rolls equal to your Wisdom modifier. At the start of the first turn of each combat, your walking Speed increases by 10 feet, which lasts until the end of that turn. If you take the Attack action on that first turn, you can make one additional weapon attack as part of that action. If that attack hits, the target takes an extra 2d6 damage of the weapon's type.

mechanics.first_turn_speed_bonus: +10 feet (lasts until end of first turn)

first_turn_extra_attack.trigger: Take the Attack action on your first turn of combat

features.name: Gloom Stalker Magic
features.description: You always have certain spells prepared. These spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare.

features.name: Umbral Sight
features.description: You gain Darkvision with a range of 60 feet. If you already have Darkvision, its range increases by 60 feet. You are also adept at evading creatures that rely on Darkvision. While entirely in Darkness, you are Invisible to any creature that relies on Darkvision to see you in that Darkness.

mechanics.darkness_invisibility: Invisible to creatures relying on Darkvision while you are entirely in Darkness

features.name: Iron Mind
features.description: You have honed your ability to resist the mind-altering powers of your prey. You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws. If you already have this proficiency, you instead gain proficiency in Intelligence or Charisma saving throws (your choice).

mechanics.if_already_proficient: Proficiency in Intelligence or Charisma saving throws (your choice)

features.name: Stalker's Flurry
features.description: You learn to attack with such unexpected speed that you can turn a miss into another strike. Once on each of your turns when you miss with a weapon attack, you can make another weapon attack as part of the same action.

mechanics.trigger: Miss with a weapon attack
mechanics.effect: Make one additional weapon attack as part of the same action

features.name: Shadowy Dodge
features.description: You can dodge in unforeseen ways, with wisps of supernatural shadow around you. Whenever a creature makes an attack roll against you and doesn't have Advantage on the roll, you can use your Reaction to impose Disadvantage on it. You must use this feature before you know the outcome of the attack roll.

mechanics.trigger: Creature makes an attack roll against you without Advantage
mechanics.timing: Must use before knowing the outcome
mechanics.effect: Impose Disadvantage on the attack roll

features.name: Shadowy Dodge Improvement
features.description: You can use your Shadowy Dodge feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclasses.name: Hunter
subclasses.description: You stalk prey in the wilds and elsewhere, using your abilities as a Hunter to protect the border between civilization and the terrors of the untamed world.

features.name: Hunter's Lore
features.description: You can call on the forces of nature to reveal certain strengths and weaknesses of your prey. While a creature is marked by your Hunter's Mark, you know whether that creature has any damage Immunities, Resistances, or Vulnerabilities and what they are.

mechanics.trigger: Creature is marked by your Hunter's Mark

features.name: Hunter's Prey
features.description: You gain one of the following feature options of your choice. Colossus Slayer — Your tenacity can wear down even the most resilient foes. When you hit a creature with a weapon, the weapon deals an extra 1d8 damage to the target if it's missing any of its Hit Points. You can deal this extra damage only once per turn. Giant Killer — When a Large or larger creature within 5 feet of you misses you with an attack roll, you can use your Reaction to attack that creature immediately after its attack, provided that you can see the creature. Horde Breaker — Once on each of your turns when you make a weapon attack, you can make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the original target and within range of your weapon.

options.name: Colossus Slayer
options.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon, deal an extra 1d8 damage if it's missing any HP. Once per turn.

options.name: Giant Killer
options.description: When a Large or larger creature within 5 ft misses you with an attack roll, use your Reaction to attack it immediately after.

options.name: Horde Breaker
options.description: Once per turn when you make a weapon attack, make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature within 5 ft of the first target and within range.

features.name: Defensive Tactics
features.description: You gain one of the following feature options of your choice. Escape the Horde — Opportunity Attacks against you are made with Disadvantage. Multiattack Defense — When a creature hits you with an attack roll, you gain a +4 bonus to AC against all subsequent attacks from that creature for the rest of the current turn. Steel Will — You have Advantage on saving throws against the Frightened condition.

options.name: Escape the Horde
options.description: Opportunity Attacks against you are made with Disadvantage.

options.name: Multiattack Defense
options.description: When hit by an attack, gain +4 AC against all subsequent attacks from the same creature for the rest of the turn.

options.name: Steel Will
options.description: Advantage on saving throws against the Frightened condition.

features.name: Superior Hunter's Prey
features.description: You gain one of the following feature options of your choice. Brutal Critical — When you score a Critical Hit with an attack roll using a weapon, roll the weapon's damage dice twice more and add them to the extra damage of the Critical Hit. Slayer's Counter — If the target of your Hunter's Mark forces you to make a saving throw, you can use your Reaction to make one weapon attack against the quarry. You make this attack immediately before making the saving throw. If your attack hits, your saving throw automatically succeeds. Slayer's Prey — Once on each of your turns, you can add 1d6 damage to one of your attacks against the target of your Hunter's Mark.

options.name: Brutal Critical
options.description: On a Critical Hit with a weapon, roll the weapon's damage dice two more times and add to the Critical Hit's extra damage.

options.name: Slayer's Counter
options.description: When Hunter's Mark target forces a saving throw on you, use Reaction to attack it first; if you hit, your save automatically succeeds.

options.name: Slayer's Prey
options.description: Once per turn, add 1d6 damage to one attack against your Hunter's Mark target.

features.name: Superior Hunter's Defense
features.description: You gain one of the following feature options of your choice. Evasion — When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and half damage if you fail. Stanch — You have Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage while not wearing Heavy armor. Uncanny Dodge — When an attacker you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can use your Reaction to halve the attack's damage against you.

options.name: Evasion
options.description: On Dex saves for half damage: no damage on success, half on failure.

options.name: Stanch
options.description: Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage while not wearing Heavy armor.

options.name: Uncanny Dodge
options.description: When an attacker you can see hits you, use Reaction to halve the attack's damage.

features.name: Hunter's Capstone
features.description: Your hunting skills reach their peak. Whenever you hit a creature marked by Hunter's Mark with a weapon attack, you can choose to deal maximum damage with that weapon instead of rolling. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum once), and regain all uses on a Long Rest.

mechanics.trigger: Hit a Hunter's Mark target with a weapon attack
mechanics.effect: Deal maximum damage instead of rolling

class.name: Tinkerer
class.flavor: Where others see iron and copper, the Tinkerer sees potential. Through study, ingenuity, and an unshakeable belief that magic is just another tool, these inventor-scholars craft solutions to problems that spellcasters and warriors alike cannot solve. They are not born with power — they build it.
tool_proficiencies: One additional Artisan's Tools of your choice

spellcasting.focus: Tinker's tools or a crafted arcane device
spellcasting.prepared_spells_formula: Intelligence modifier + half Tinkerer level (rounded down, minimum 1)
spellcasting.ritual_note: You can cast any Tinkerer spell you have prepared as a ritual if it has the ritual tag.
spellcasting.note: Tinkerer is a half-caster. Spell slots begin at level 1.

tinkerer_spells.note: The following spells are always prepared for the Tinkerer and do not count against the number of spells you can prepare. They represent the Tinkerer's unique magical repertoire.

infusions.description: You have learned to channel arcane energy into objects through a process called Infusion. Infusions are temporary magical enhancements you apply to nonmagical items during a Long Rest. An infusion lasts until you infuse another item or until you finish a Long Rest without re-applying it. You can have a number of active infusions equal to your Infusions Known divided by 2 (rounded down). Infused items count as magical for the purpose of overcoming Resistance and Immunity.

infusion_list.name: Arcane Propulsion
infusion_list.item: A pair of boots or shoes
infusion_list.description: The wearer's Speed increases by 5 feet. The wearer can use a Bonus Action to cause the boots to emit a brief burst of force, jumping up to 10 feet horizontally without expending movement.

infusion_list.name: Bolstering Armor
infusion_list.description: While wearing this armor, the wearer gains a +1 bonus to AC. This bonus increases to +2 at Tinkerer level 10.

infusion_list.name: Crafted Ammunition
infusion_list.description: The ammunition becomes magical and deals an additional 1d4 Force damage on a hit.

infusion_list.name: Deployable Shield
infusion_list.description: While holding this shield, the wielder can use a Bonus Action to extend a magical barrier: until the start of their next turn, all ranged attack rolls against them are made with Disadvantage.

infusion_list.name: Focused Focus
infusion_list.description: This item can serve as a spellcasting focus for any class. When the wielder casts a spell through it that requires Concentration, they gain a +2 bonus to Constitution saving throws to maintain Concentration on that spell.

infusion_list.name: Homunculus Vessel
infusion_list.item: A gem or crystal worth at least 100 GP
infusion_list.description: You craft a Homunculus Servant (see Companion section) that is bound to this gem. If the homunculus is destroyed, you can spend a Long Rest and 100 GP in materials to recreate it. The gem must remain intact.

infusion_list.name: Iron Mind Helm
infusion_list.description: The wearer gains Advantage on Intelligence saving throws and cannot be Surprised while wearing this helm.

infusion_list.name: Radiant Weapon
infusion_list.description: This weapon becomes a +1 magic weapon that sheds Bright Light in a 5-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 5 feet. As a Bonus Action, the wielder can cause the weapon to flare: the next attack with it has Advantage.

infusion_list.name: Repeating Mechanism
infusion_list.item: A crossbow or other ranged weapon that uses ammunition
infusion_list.description: The weapon gains a built-in magazine. The wielder ignores the Loading property of this weapon and can make two attacks with it when taking the Attack action (if they normally only make one).

infusion_list.name: Resistant Cloak
infusion_list.description: Choose a damage type: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison. The wearer gains Resistance to the chosen damage type.

infusion_list.name: Sending Stone
infusion_list.description: You create a pair of Sending Stones. A creature holding one stone can use an Action to speak a message of up to 25 words; the holder of the other stone hears the message. The stones only work within 1 mile of each other, and each stone can send 3 messages per Long Rest.

infusion_list.name: Sovereign Glue Gloves
infusion_list.description: The wearer gains a Climb Speed equal to their Speed and Advantage on Strength (Athletics) checks to climb, grapple, or avoid being disarmed.

infusion_list.name: Thunderstep Boots
infusion_list.description: Once per Short or Long Rest, the wearer can activate the boots as a Bonus Action to teleport up to 15 feet to an unoccupied space they can see, leaving behind a burst of thunderous energy that deals 1d6 Thunder damage to each creature within 5 feet of the origin point (Constitution save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier for half).

infusion_list.name: Warding Bond Band
infusion_list.description: While a willing creature wears this item, you can use a Reaction when that creature takes damage to transfer up to half that damage (rounded down) to yourself instead. You must be within 30 feet of the creature.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You have studied the arcane sciences and learned to channel magical energy through the devices you craft. You prepare Tinkerer spells from the Tinkerer spell list. You prepare a number of spells equal to your Intelligence modifier plus half your Tinkerer level (rounded down, minimum 1). You can change your list of prepared spells when you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.prepared_spells: Intelligence modifier + half Tinkerer level (rounded down, minimum 1)
mechanics.focus: Tinker's tools or crafted arcane device

features.name: Arcane Tinkering
features.description: You can produce small magical effects through your tools and devices. When you use Tinker's tools, you can perform any of the following: create a small clockwork device (Tiny object, AC 5, 1 HP) that moves up to 5 feet per round and performs one simple task you define when you create it; create a small, permanent sensor that emits a sound or light when a condition you define occurs (up to 10 feet away); or temporarily repair a damaged nonmagical item, restoring 1d4 HP to an object. You can have up to 3 such devices active at once. They cease to function if you are more than 100 feet from them or when you finish a Long Rest.

options: Create a Tiny clockwork device (AC 5, 1 HP): moves 5 ft/round, performs one simple task
Create a Tiny sensor: emits sound or light when defined condition occurs (range 10 ft)
Temporarily repair a nonmagical item: restore 1d4 HP to an object
mechanics.duration: Until you are 100+ ft away or finish a Long Rest

features.name: Magical Tinkering
features.description: At 1st level, you learn how to invest a spark of magic into mundane objects. To use this ability, you must have Tinker's tools or other Artisan's Tools in hand. You then touch a Tiny nonmagical object as an Action and give it one of the following magical properties of your choice: The object emits a bright light in a 5-foot radius and dim light for an additional 5 feet. Whenever tapped by a creature, the object emits a recorded message of up to 6 seconds that you voiced while touching it. The object continuously emits your choice of an odor or a nonverbal sound. A static visual effect appears on one of the object's surfaces. The chosen property lasts indefinitely. As an Action, you can touch the object and end the property early. You can bestow magic on multiple objects, touching one object each time you use this feature, though a single object can only bear one property at a time. The maximum number of objects you can affect with this feature at one time is equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1).

mechanics.activation: Action (must have Artisan's tools in hand)
property_options: Emits bright light 5 ft, dim light additional 5 ft
Emits recorded message (up to 6 seconds) when tapped
Continuously emits an odor or nonverbal sound
Static visual effect on one surface

features.name: Infuse Item
features.description: You gain the ability to imbue mundane items with certain magical infusions, turning those items into magic items. You learn 4 infusions of your choice from the Tinkerer Infusion list. Whenever you gain a Tinkerer level, you can replace one infusion you know with a different one. When you finish a Long Rest, you can touch a number of nonmagical objects equal to your number of active infusion slots, imbuing each with one of your known infusions. An infused item loses its magic if you die or if you imbue more items than allowed, in which case the oldest infusion ends first.

mechanics.infusion_process: Touch nonmagical objects during Long Rest
mechanics.item_becomes: Magical for overcoming Resistance and Immunity

features.name: Tinkerer Specialization
features.description: You choose a Tinkerer Specialization that reflects your area of expertise. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at levels 5, 9, 13, and 17.

features.name: The Right Tool
features.description: You have learned to produce exactly the tool you need from your kit. When you make an ability check using Thieves' tools, Tinker's tools, or any Artisan's Tools you are proficient with, you can add double your proficiency bonus to the check instead of your normal proficiency bonus.

mechanics.effect: Double proficiency bonus on checks with Thieves' tools, Tinker's tools, or proficient Artisan's Tools
mechanics.note: This is Expertise specifically for tool checks

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Tinkerer levels 8, 12, 16, and 19.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Specialization Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Tinkerer Specialization.

features.name: Homunculus Companion
features.description: You construct a small magical companion called a Homunculus. It is a Tiny construct that is friendly to you and your companions, and it obeys your commands. In combat, the homunculus acts on your Initiative. It can move and take Reactions on its own. On your turn, you can command it to take one action from its stat block using a Bonus Action. If the homunculus is destroyed, you can rebuild it over the course of a Long Rest by expending 100 GP worth of materials. The homunculus uses the following stats: HP = 5 + your Intelligence modifier per Tinkerer level (minimum 1 HP); AC = 13 + your proficiency bonus; Speed 30 ft, Fly 30 ft; Str 4, Dex 15, Con 12, Int 10, Wis 10, Cha 7. Its attacks count as magical.

mechanics.type: Tiny Construct

actions.name: Needle Sting
actions.description: Melee attack: proficiency bonus + DEX modifier to hit, reach 5 ft, one target. Hit: 1d4 + DEX modifier Piercing damage plus 1d4 Poison damage.

actions.name: Force Spark
actions.description: Ranged attack: proficiency bonus + INT modifier to hit, range 30/60 ft, one target. Hit: 1d6 + INT modifier Force damage.

actions.name: Help
actions.description: The homunculus takes the Help action, granting Advantage on the next attack roll or ability check made by a creature it assists before the start of its next turn.

features.name: Tool Expertise
features.description: Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make using Tinker's tools or Thieves' tools, if not already doubled.

mechanics.effect: Expertise with Tinker's tools and Thieves' tools

features.name: Flash of Genius
features.description: When you or another creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes an ability check or a saving throw, you can use your Reaction to add your Intelligence modifier to the roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

mechanics.trigger: You or visible creature within 30 ft makes ability check or saving throw
mechanics.bonus: Add Intelligence modifier to the roll

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Specialization Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Tinkerer Specialization.

features.name: Infuse Self
features.description: You have learned to apply infusions directly to your own body. You can now apply one of your active infusions to yourself without using an item slot. The effect lasts until you finish a Long Rest. This self-infusion does not count against your active infusion limit.

mechanics.effect: Apply one known infusion to yourself (no item required)

features.name: Magic Item Savant
features.description: Your skill with magic items deepens. You can attune to up to four magic items at once (instead of the normal limit of three). If a magic item requires attunement by a specific class, you qualify for attunement as if you were a member of that class.

features.name: Arcane Armament
features.description: You can now infuse armor and weapons you wear or wield with a secondary effect in addition to the item's primary infusion. Specifically, any armor or weapon you have infused also grants a +1 bonus to attack rolls, damage rolls, or saving throws (your choice, decided when applying the infusion). This bonus stacks with other bonuses granted by the infusion.

mechanics.applies_to: Infused armor or weapons you wear/wield
mechanics.bonus: +1 to attack rolls, damage rolls, or saving throws (choose when infusing)

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Specialization Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Tinkerer Specialization.

features.name: Soul of Craft
features.description: Your connection to the craft transcends mundane skill. You gain Resistance to Poison damage and immunity to the Poisoned condition. In addition, you always know if an object within 30 feet of you has been magically altered or is a magical item, though you don't automatically know its properties.

mechanics.passive_detection: Know if objects within 30 ft are magical or magically altered (not their properties)

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Specialization Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Tinkerer Specialization.

features.name: Magic Item Master
features.description: You can now attune to up to five magic items at once. In addition, when you use a magic item that has a limited number of charges, roll a d6 after each use. On a 6, the item does not expend a charge.

charge_conservation.trigger: After using a charged magic item
charge_conservation.on_6: Item does not expend a charge

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

mechanics.type: Feat (Ability Score Improvement or other qualifying feat)

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat of your choice.

mechanics.type: Epic Boon feat

features.name: Master of Invention
features.description: Your mastery of craft and magic reaches its apex. When you finish a Long Rest, you may choose two additional infusions to have active beyond your normal limit, for free, as long as both are applied to items you carry or wear. In addition, whenever you roll a 1 on an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can use your Reaction to reroll the die and take the new result.

mechanics.bonus_infusion_condition: Both applied to items you carry or wear

reroll.trigger: Roll a 1 on attack roll, ability check, or saving throw
reroll.effect: Reroll and use the new result

subclasses.name: Alchemical Savant
subclasses.description: Some Tinkerers find that the most powerful tools are not made of iron and gears, but of carefully combined reagents. Alchemical Savants are masters of concoctions, extracts, and alchemical formulae, blending science and magic into potions and volatile compounds that can heal allies or devastate foes.

features.name: Alchemical Formula
features.description: You always have the following spells prepared and they don't count against your prepared spells: Healing Word, Acid Splash, Create or Destroy Water, Purify Food and Drink, Web, Melf's Acid Arrow, Stinking Cloud, Blight.

features.name: Experimental Elixir
features.description: Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can produce a number of experimental elixirs equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1). Roll a d6 for each to determine its effect. A creature can drink an elixir as a Bonus Action. The elixir lasts 24 hours unused, then loses its potency. You can also create an elixir during a Short Rest by expending a 1st-level spell slot. Elixir Effects (d6): 1 — Healing: Drink to regain 2d4 + Intelligence modifier HP. 2 — Swiftness: Drink to gain +10 feet Speed for 1 hour. 3 — Resilience: Drink to gain 2d6 + Intelligence modifier Temporary HP. 4 — Boldness: Drink to gain Advantage on the next attack roll and saving throw made within 1 minute. 5 — Flight: Drink to gain a Fly Speed of 10 feet for 10 minutes. 6 — Transformation: Drink to cast Alter Self with no concentration for 10 minutes.

mechanics.creation_via_slot: Expend 1st-level spell slot during Short Rest for one elixir

effects_table.name: Boldness
effects_table.effect: Advantage on next attack roll and saving throw within 1 minute

effects_table.name: Transformation
effects_table.effect: Cast Alter Self (no Concentration) for 10 minutes

features.name: Alchemical Savant (Spellcasting Bonus)
features.description: Whenever you cast a spell using your alchemist's supplies as the spellcasting focus, you gain a bonus to one roll of the spell: a bonus equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum +1) to a spell's damage roll (if it deals Acid, Fire, Necrotic, or Poison damage) or to the number of HP restored by a spell (if it restores HP).

mechanics.requirement: Cast a spell using alchemist's supplies as focus
applies_to: Damage rolls dealing Acid, Fire, Necrotic, or Poison damage

features.name: Restorative Reagents
features.description: You can incorporate healing magic into your elixirs more efficiently. Whenever a creature drinks one of your experimental elixirs, it also loses the Poisoned condition if it has it. In addition, you can use your Bonus Action to administer an elixir to an incapacitated creature within 5 feet of you. Finally, you always have Lesser Restoration prepared.

mechanics.elixir_bonus: Drinking one of your elixirs also removes the Poisoned condition
mechanics.administer: Bonus Action to administer elixir to Incapacitated creature within 5 ft

features.name: Chemical Mastery
features.description: You gain Resistance to Acid damage and Poison damage, and immunity to the Poisoned condition. In addition, you always have the Greater Restoration and Cloudkill spells prepared, and they don't count against your prepared spells. You can cast each of them once without expending a spell slot, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Philosopher's Touch
features.description: You have discovered the secret of the philosopher's stone. When you create an experimental elixir, you can choose its effect instead of rolling randomly. In addition, whenever you use an experimental elixir or administer one to another creature, you can expend a spell slot of any level to enhance it: 1st or 2nd level slot — Double the healing or Temporary HP granted. 3rd or 4th level slot — Also grant one additional random elixir effect. 5th level slot or higher — The recipient gains all six elixir effects simultaneously for the stated durations.

mechanics.chosen_effect: You choose the effect instead of rolling

enhancement_via_slot.bonus: Also grant one additional random elixir effect
Recipient gains all six elixir effects simultaneously

subclasses.name: Arcane Gunsmith
subclasses.description: Not all Tinkerers look to potions or golems. Some see the potential in combining arcane energy with precision ranged weaponry. Arcane Gunsmiths engineer magical firearms and arcanically-enhanced bows, merging the precision of a sharpshooter with the versatility of a spellcaster.

features.name: Thunder Cannon
features.description: You craft a magical firearm called a Thunder Cannon. It is a two-handed Martial Ranged Weapon with the following stats: Damage: 1d8 Thunder; Range: 60/180 ft; Properties: Two-Handed, Loud (creatures within 100 ft who hear a shot must succeed on a DC 10 Wisdom save or know the direction of the shot). You are always proficient with your Thunder Cannon, and it counts as a spellcasting focus for you. If the Thunder Cannon is lost or destroyed, you can recreate it over a Long Rest for 25 GP in materials. You can upgrade it: at 5th level the damage die becomes 1d10; at 9th level it becomes 1d12; at 13th level it deals an extra die of damage on a Critical Hit.

mechanics.damage: 1d8 Thunder (d10 at lvl 5, d12 at lvl 9)
mechanics.loud_effect: Creatures within 100 ft hear the shot; DC 10 Wisdom save or know direction
mechanics.crit_bonus_at_13: Extra damage die on Critical Hit

features.name: Arcane Shot
features.description: You learn to channel arcane energy through your Thunder Cannon or other ranged weapon to unleash special effects. You learn 2 Arcane Shot options of your choice. You can use an Arcane Shot once per turn when you make a ranged attack using your Thunder Cannon or a bow, at no additional cost. You learn one additional Arcane Shot option at levels 7, 10, 15, and 18. You have a number of uses equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1) per Short or Long Rest. Arcane Shot Options: Banishing Shot — On hit, target makes Charisma save (DC = your spell save DC) or is Banished until the start of your next turn, then reappears in its original space. Blinding Shot — On hit, target makes Constitution save or is Blinded until the start of your next turn. Burst Shot — On hit, each creature within 5 ft of the target takes 1d6 Force damage (Dexterity save for half). Crippling Shot — On hit, target's Speed is reduced to 0 until the start of your next turn. Grasping Shot — On hit, spectral thorns appear around the target: it has Disadvantage on attack rolls and must succeed on a Strength check (DC = your spell save DC) or be Restrained until the start of your next turn. Seeking Shot — Ignore up to three-quarters cover on this attack. If it misses, you can reroll once. Shadow Shot — The attack deals an additional 2d6 Necrotic damage and the target cannot regain HP until the start of your next turn.

mechanics.usage: Once per turn on a ranged attack with Thunder Cannon or bow

options.name: Banishing Shot
options.effect: On hit: Charisma save or Banished until start of your next turn

options.name: Blinding Shot
options.effect: On hit: Constitution save or Blinded until start of your next turn

options.name: Burst Shot
options.effect: On hit: creatures within 5 ft take 1d6 Force (Dex save for half)

options.name: Crippling Shot
options.effect: On hit: target's Speed reduced to 0 until start of your next turn

options.name: Grasping Shot
options.effect: On hit: Disadvantage on attacks; Strength check or Restrained until start of your next turn

options.name: Seeking Shot
options.effect: Ignore up to three-quarters cover; if miss, reroll once

options.name: Shadow Shot
options.effect: On hit: +2d6 Necrotic damage; target can't regain HP until start of your next turn

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Arcane Reload
features.description: You can reload your Thunder Cannon using arcane energy rather than physical ammunition. Your Thunder Cannon no longer requires ammunition. In addition, when you use your Arcane Shot feature, you can expend a spell slot to empower the shot: 1st-level slot: add 1d6 to the shot's damage. 2nd-level slot: add 2d6 and impose Disadvantage on the target's saving throw (if any). 3rd-level slot or higher: add 3d6, impose Disadvantage, and the shot affects one additional target within 10 feet of the original target.

spell_slot_empowerment.bonus: +3d6 damage + Disadvantage on save + one additional target within 10 ft

features.name: Ever-Ready Shot
features.description: Your instincts with your weapon are near-supernatural. When you roll Initiative with no Arcane Shot uses remaining, you regain one use of Arcane Shot. In addition, you can use Arcane Shot twice per turn instead of once.

mechanics.initiative_trigger: Regain 1 Arcane Shot use if you have none remaining when rolling Initiative

features.name: Arcane Barrage
features.description: Once per Long Rest, when you take the Attack action, you can replace all attacks in that action with a single devastating Arcane Barrage. Choose a point you can see within 120 feet. Every creature within a 20-foot radius of that point must make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature takes 10d6 Force damage and one Arcane Shot effect of your choice. On a successful save, the creature takes half the damage and is not affected by the Arcane Shot effect.

mechanics.activation: Replace all attacks in an Attack action
mechanics.damage_fail: 10d6 Force + one Arcane Shot effect of your choice
mechanics.damage_success: Half damage, no Arcane Shot effect

subclasses.name: Warforged Smith
subclasses.description: Some Tinkerers dedicate themselves to a singular achievement: the creation of a mechanical warrior companion. Warforged Smiths build, upgrade, and fight alongside a mechanical construct — a metal ally that grows more powerful as the Tinkerer does.

features.name: Steel Defender
features.description: Your tinkering has borne fruit: you create a mechanical construct called a Steel Defender. It is a Medium Construct that is friendly to you and your companions and obeys your commands. In combat it acts on your Initiative. On your turn you can command it to take one action using a Bonus Action; otherwise it takes the Dodge action. The Steel Defender uses the following base stats, which improve as you level: HP = 2 + your Intelligence modifier + 5 × your Tinkerer level; AC = 13 + proficiency bonus; Speed 40 ft; Str 14, Dex 12, Con 14, Int 4, Wis 10, Cha 6. It has Immunity to Poison and Psychic damage, and the Poisoned and Charmed conditions. If the Steel Defender is destroyed, you can rebuild it over 8 hours of work and 100 GP of materials. The Steel Defender's attacks count as magical.

mechanics.type: Medium Construct

actions.name: Force-Empowered Rend
actions.description: Melee attack: proficiency bonus + STR modifier to hit, reach 5 ft, one target. Hit: 1d8 + STR modifier Force damage.

actions.name: Deflect Attack
actions.description: Reaction — when a creature the Steel Defender can see hits a target (other than itself) within 5 ft with an attack roll, the Steel Defender imposes a -1d4 penalty to the attack roll (potentially turning a hit into a miss).

features.name: Arcane Jolt
features.description: You learn how to channel magical energy into your Steel Defender's strikes or your own. Once per turn when either you or your Steel Defender hits a creature with an attack, you can channel an Arcane Jolt through the attack, causing one of the following effects: the target takes an extra 2d6 Force damage, or one creature or object you can see within 30 feet of the target is healed for 2d6 HP. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum once), and you regain all uses when you finish a Long Rest. The extra damage increases to 4d6 at level 15.

mechanics.trigger: You or Steel Defender hits a creature with an attack (once per turn)
effects: Target takes extra 2d6 Force damage (4d6 at level 15)
Creature or object within 30 ft of target regains 2d6 HP (4d6 at level 15)

features.name: Improved Defender
features.description: Your Steel Defender's Deflect Attack can now impose a -1d6 penalty instead of -1d4. Additionally, the Force-Empowered Rend action deals an extra 1d6 Force damage. Finally, the Steel Defender gains Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage from nonmagical attacks.

mechanics.force_rend_upgrade: Extra 1d6 Force damage on Force-Empowered Rend

features.name: Fortified Position
features.description: Your Steel Defender is a bulwark of protection. While your Steel Defender is within 5 feet of you, you and all friendly creatures within 5 feet of the Steel Defender gain Half Cover (+2 AC and Dexterity saving throws). In addition, you can now have your Steel Defender use two actions on its turn (still requiring your Bonus Action to command each one). Finally, you can use your Reaction to have the Steel Defender move up to its Speed toward a friendly creature that just took damage.

mechanics.half_cover_aura: 5-foot radius around Steel Defender — you and friendly creatures gain +2 AC and Dex saves
mechanics.double_action: Can command Steel Defender to take two actions (each requires your Bonus Action)
mechanics.reactive_move: Reaction: Steel Defender moves up to its Speed toward a friendly creature that just took damage

features.name: Perfected Automaton
features.description: Your Steel Defender has been refined to its apex. It gains the following upgrades: its maximum HP increases by 40; it gains a Fly Speed of 30 feet; its Force-Empowered Rend attack can now hit all creatures in a 10-foot line (Dexterity save DC = your spell save DC for half damage instead of an attack roll); and once per Long Rest, if the Steel Defender is reduced to 0 HP, it instead drops to 1 HP and becomes immune to all damage until the start of your next turn.

mechanics.rend_upgrade: Hits all creatures in 10-ft line; Dex save for half instead of attack roll

last_stand.effect: Drops to 1 HP instead; immune to all damage until start of your next turn
